CORE GAME RULES
It is the 31st century, a time of endless wars that rage
across human-occupied space. As star empires clash,
these epic wars are won and lost by BattleMechs,®
30-foot-tall humanoid metal titans bristling with lasers,
autocannons and dozens of other lethal weapons;
enough firepower to level entire city blocks. Your elite
force of MechWarriors® drives these juggernauts into
battle, proudly holding your faction’s flag high, intent
on expanding the power and glory of your realm. At
their beck and call are the support units of armored
vehicles, power armored infantry, aerospace fighters
and more, wielded by a MechWarrior’s skillful command
to aid him in ultimate victory. Will they become legends,
or forgotten casualties? Only your skill and luck will
determine their fate!

The product of more than twenty years of gaming
experience, Total Warfare™
                      Warfare ™ presents the rules of the
BattleTech® game system as never before. For the first time,
all the rules for various units that have a direct impact on
the deadly battlefields of the thirty-first century appear in a
unified rules set: from BattleMechs to ProtoMechs, Combat
Vehicles to Support Vehicles, infantry to aerospace fighters
and DropShips. Interwoven and meticulously updated, Total
Warfare provides the most detailed and comprehensive
rules set published to date for BattleTech      —the perfect
                                    BattleTech—the
companion for standard tournament play.

Total Warfare is the single-source rulebook for people who play
BattleTech.. It is not intended to teach new players the game, but
BattleTech
rather to serve as a reference work for people who know the
game, while introducing more technologies and expansive rules
than appear in the basic box set. The introductory game in the
BattleTech line is the A Game of Armored Combat box set. New
players should pick up that product before diving into this one.

Construction rules for the various units presented in Total Warfare
can be found in TechManual.™

in well over a decade, all the various rules sets that directly affect   for beginning BattleTech players. For more information about the
BattleTech ground warfare—including aerospace rules covering units       availability and use of these items, see Components, page 20 of
that can enter a planet’s atmosphere—appear in a single volume,          this book.
woven together to make sure that all rules function seamlessly
with one another. Total Warfare also includes additional material to
expand and enhance game play. Along with the rules of BattleTech,                               FICTION

a painting section that will have players quickly painting the plastic     Fiction has always played an

box sets, the ForcePacks, as well as priming them for the complete       more than a hundred novels

in Total Warfare; those rules are in the TechManual (see Core            “Story fiction” includes novels,

   The rules in this book supersede all previously pub-                  on; fiction that puts the reader
lished rules, including the BattleTech Manual, the BattleTech            inside the heads of the charac-
Compendium, BattleTech Compendium: The Rules of Warfare,                 ters that populate the universe.
BattleTech Master Rules (standard and revised editions) and              “Sourcebook fiction”—which appears in the various Handbooks,

   It is important to note that fiction, though essential in
making the game universe come alive, should never be
construed as rules. While BattleTech fiction usually attempts                 3-D TERRAIN VS.                                              COMBAT

to adhere to the aesthetics established by the rules, authors
often use creative license to accomplish the needs of a given                   PAPER MAPS
story.                                                                                                                                       HEAT

           ADVANCED RULES                                              com (see E-Publications on the following page).

  The rules presented in Total Warfare are considered the stan-
dard rules of BattleTech. They represent all tournament-appropri-
ate rules: the “big” BattleTech tournaments run directly by Catalyst
Game Labs at the Origins International Games Expo®, any Gen
Con Game Fair® and many other conventions all use the rules
presented in Total Warfare.
  Advanced rules—as found in Tactical Operations, Strategic
Operations (see Core Rulebooks, at right) and so on—provide more
depth for virtually every aspect of play. However, the additional
complexity makes such rules inappropriate for tournament play.         FORCEPACKS
                                                                          Catalyst has begun releasing a series of ForcePacks that contain
RULE LEVELS                                                            high-quality, fully-assembled plastic ’Mechs, ready to play
   Previously, BattleTech split its rules into levels. However, with   out-of-the-box.
the introduction of several new units and their construction              The Clan Invasion box includes Force Building rules that make
systems (notably IndustrialMechs and Support Vehicles), the lines      it easy to take the various ForcePacks and craft the force you’re
between the levels became blurred. The current rules are simply        looking to deploy during any scenario.
classified as standard or tournament and advanced or non-tour-
nament, as appropriate.                                                CORE RULEBOOKS

                                                                GREAT HOUSES
   The Great Houses have dominated most of the Inner Sphere’s two thousand star systems for centuries. Each House is ruled by a single individual
who wields immense power, shaping the futures of trillions of people. These dictatorships, benign or otherwise, sprang up in direct response to
humankind’s expansion to the stars. After the collapse of the central government that supported humanity’s first wave of expansion, regional power
brokers arose. They wrought the empires that exist today, binding nearby planets together with various forms of feudalism.
   The vast distances between planets made interstellar travel a rarity. Until humans developed the HPG system in 2630, interstellar communication
was extremely difficult and unreliable. This meant that each world enjoyed considerable autonomy. The warlords who ruled the planets rarely
faced the risk of overthrow by an invading military force. Variations on this feudal system remain in place today, resulting in a patchwork of mostly
independent local governments overseen by a neo-feudal lord.
   In keeping with their feudal origins, all the Inner Sphere nations include a noble class. Many noble titles are common to several nations. Terran
nobility originated with the feudal system of government most prevalent in the European Middle Ages, when a sovereign delegated responsibility
and authority over an area to select subjects in exchange for a personal oath of fealty. These subordinate leaders received a patent of nobility
allowing them to use a title commensurate with their importance.
   Nobility in the thirty-first century is largely hereditary, with the designated heirs of nobles inheriting their parents’ rights, estates and responsibilities.
Though most sovereigns can grant titles to their subjects, this practice is relatively rare—even for MechWarriors, the modern equivalent of knights.




                       THE CLANS
    While most of the peoples and social groups in the Inner                                             PERIPHERY STATES
Sphere are culturally descended from ancient forebears that
originated on Terra and brought their cultures with them (along                             Beyond the boundaries of the Inner Sphere lie the endless
with attendant ideologies, passions, bigotry and more) during the                        reaches of the Periphery, traditionally home to independent-
colonization of space over the past thousand years, the Clans are                        minded souls who sought escape from the often repressive
culturally unique. Made up of descendants from the original Star                         regimes of the Great Houses. The Periphery is the Inner Sphere’s
League Defense Force, the warriors of the Clans excel in battle and                      frontier, keeping the spirit of exploration and discovery alive
all things military.                                                                     and well. The less savory aspects of frontier living are equally
    The Clans came into being when the Star League Defense Force                         abundant; numerous pirate bands and petty bandit kingdoms
fled the Inner sphere almost 300 years ago, following the fall of the                    thrive in this almost lawless region of space. Largely unexplored
Star League. Under the direction of General Aleksandr Kerensky                           and sparsely populated compared with the Inner Sphere, the
and his son Nicholas, the army left and formed its own society on                        Periphery has nonetheless played a pivotal role in several major
what we now call the Clan homeworlds, located approximately                              interstellar events, including the fall of the Star League.
one thousand light-years from the edge of the Inner Sphere.                                 The Periphery’s reputation as backward and technologically
Clan society resembles that of ancient Sparta, focusing on the                           inferior is only partly true. Though many areas in it are
warrior ethic and dedicated to advancing the technology of war,                          underdeveloped industrially and well behind the technology
specifically the BattleMech.                                                             curve compared with the Inner Sphere, it includes scattered
    Each of the different Clans has its own flavor—all bound to                          regions that boast cultural and technological advancement. The
honor, some more, some less. In general, the Clans divide into two                       Taurian Concordat is well known for its excellent educational
camps: the Crusaders, who believe it their destiny to conquer the                        system and high literacy rate, while the Magistracy of Canopus
Inner Sphere and Terra, and the Wardens, who believe their role                          exemplifies some of the most progressive views on human rights
is to protect the Inner Sphere against an as yet unrealized threat.                      in known space.
While most of the Clans are secular, a few are given to mysticism. The
common spoken tongue among the Clans is based on Star League                              PIRATES
English, but they have enhanced it with a rich collection of unique                         Throughout humankind’s history, rogue elements have always
words. Clansmen also rarely use contractions, considering such                           lived outside the confines of civilization, willing to do whatever it
shortened speech vulgar and lazy.                                                        takes to survive. In practice, that usually means taking what they
    The most powerful Crusader Clans returned to the Inner Sphere                        need from someone else.
in the year 3050, in hopes of conquering it and reestablishing                              Though pirates are generally associated with the Periphery,
the Star League. The combined armies of the Great Houses                                 interested readers can dig into the history of any Great House
stopped them, and they have since gradually become permanent                             and find small-time, planet-bound pirates, or pirates that made
inhabitants of many Inner Sphere worlds. Other Clans, not wanting                        a name for themselves in a few close star systems. Some pirates
to be kept from the planets they see as paradise, have contrived to                      have become legends, feared and reviled (and sometimes
return to the Inner Sphere as well.                                                      romanticized) across known space: Lady Death, the Band of the
    Though these Inner Sphere Clans strive to retain their unique                        Damned and the New Belt Pirates are a few examples.
ways, various elements of Clan life have begun to erode through
continued contact with the Inner Sphere.




                                                                 MERCENARIES
  In a universe at war, some will always be willing to sell out to the highest bidder and fight someone else’s battles. From the legendary (Wolf’s
Dragoons, the Kell Hounds, the Eridani Light Horse, the Northwind Highlanders) to the unknown (the Medusans, the Stone Ponies), from mere
ad-hoc lances to multi-regiment commands, mercenaries are a staple of the Inner Sphere. Though not technically a power unto themselves, they—
perhaps more than any private army—have raised and toppled empires.

   BattleTech simulates combat between BattleMechs, Combat                          •      The word “’Mech” refers to BattleMechs/OmniMechs and
Vehicles, ProtoMechs, infantry and aerospace units (and any                                IndustrialMechs (bipedal and four-legged). “’Mech” never
other vehicles that might get caught in the crossfire) on a variety                        refers to ProtoMechs.
of terrain. This chapter describes the various combat units that                    •      The word “Omni” refers to OmniMechs and OmniVehicles.
compete on the BattleTech battlefield and the record sheets and                     •      The word “fighter” refers to conventional and aerospace fighters.
maps needed to play the game.                                                       •      The word “vehicle” refers to all Combat and Support vehicles.
   While Total Warfare provides a comprehensive rules set for all                   •      The word “infantry” refers to conventional and battle armor
unit types detailed within this section, players should remember                           infantry.
they are free to select which ever forces they wish to use in any                   •      The term “aerospace unit” refers to all types of aerospace
given game.                                                                                units: conventional fighters, aerospace fighters, small craft,
                                                                                           DropShips and Fixed-Wing/Airship Support Vehicles.
                                                                                    •      The term “ground unit” refers to ’Mechs (BattleMechs, OmniMechs
                                                                                           and IndustrialMechs); ProtoMechs; hover, tracked, VTOL, wheeled
                         UNITS                                                             and WiGE Combat Vehicles; hover, tracked, VTOL, wheeled and
                                                                                           WiGE Support Vehicles; battle armor and conventional infantry.
   In these rules, the term “unit” refers to any mobile element that                •      The term “ground vehicle” refers to hover, wheeled and
can be fielded in a BattleTech game—BattleMech, IndustrialMech,                            tracked Combat and Support vehicles.
ProtoMech, Combat Vehicle, conventional infantry platoon/Point,                     •      “Large Craft” is a general description used to refer to any aero-
battle armor squad/Point, small craft, conventional fighter, aero-                         space unit that weighs more than two hundred tons.
space fighter, DropShip or any Support Vehicle. Each unit moves
and attacks individually on the map.                                                  BATTLEMECHS
   During game play, each unit is represented by a counter or min-                      BattleMechs—the most powerful ground-based war machines
iature. A complete line of BattleTech miniatures is available from                   ever built—dominate the battlefields of the thirty-first century.
Iron Wind Metals (see Iron Wind Metals, p. 12), not to mention the                   These huge, mostly humanoid vehicles stand eight to fourteen
plastic miniatures available in the various BattleTech box sets. If                  meters tall and weigh as much as one hundred tons. They are
the correct miniature is unavailable, players may use whatever                       faster, more maneuverable, better armored and more heavily
miniature they have on hand or any other counter or item to                          armed than any other ground combat unit. Equipped with parti-
represent each unit, as long as it is clear what unit the counter                    cle projector cannons, lasers, rapid-fire autocannons and missiles,
represents and which direction it is facing (if it has a facing).                    these behemoths pack enough firepower to flatten city blocks.

ing terminology applies where appropriate (i.e., wherever the                        BattleMechs: those used primarily by the Inner Sphere, repre-
rules allow the specific unit types to be used):                                     senting variations of and improvements on the original ’Mech

play. Rules for using these aerospace units appears in Strategic                          Standard rail requires little technology to produce or maintain,

AEROSPACE UNITS
  JumpShips, WarShips and space stations also exist in the                                MOBILE STRUCTURES
BattleTech universe.                                                                         An exceptionally rare sight in the BattleTech universe, mobile
                                                                                          structures represent super-large units and mobile buildings. Such
JumpShips                                                                                 unique constructs are usually reserved for specialized planetary
  JumpShips provide the only means of transportation between the                          environments, where the costs of construction and maintenance
far-flung star systems of the Inner Sphere, the Periphery and beyond.                     are outweighed by the needs of survival, or the desire to plumb
These vessels make interstellar leaps of 30 light-years at a time by                      harsh environmental locations for rare minerals and so on.
harnessing the radiant energy of the stars with their huge solar-
energy sails and Kearny-Fuchida hyperdrive technology. JumpShips
are primarily used to transport DropShips between star systems.

  Support Vehicle record sheets, depending on the unit in                          terrain on movement and combat appear in the Movement
question, are identical in usage to the various record sheets                      and Combat sections.
described above (see Barrier Armor Rating below for the                               Note: Only full hexes are considered legal in game play.            INTRODUCTION
exception). Use the appropriate descriptions above when                            Players may not move into or through, or end their movement
reviewing a Support Vehicle record sheet for use.                                  in, a half hex. Units that do so (voluntarily or not) are consid-

Barrier Armor Rating (BAR)
  As described under Combat Vehicle Record Sheets, the                             Level
armor of a Support Vehicle has a unique Barrier Armor Rating                          The level of a hex is the height to which it rises above the           PLAYING
(BAR). This rating represents the fact that Support Vehicles are                   prevailing terrain. All terrain has a level, but each hex’s level is     THE GAME
not Combat Vehicles and so their armor is more susceptible to                      independent of the type of terrain it contains, such as woods
damage (see Damage, p. 206).                                                       or water. Hexes with levels higher than 0 are also referred to
                                                                                                                                                             GROUND
                                                                                   as hills. If it is not marked on the map, assume a hex’s level           MOVEMENT
                                                                                   is 0.
                                                                                      This height is expressed in terms of levels. Level 1 is
               MAPSHEETS                                                           approximately six meters high, waist-high to a ’Mech. A ’Mech            AEROSPACE
                                                                                                                                                            MOVEMENT
                                                                                   (bipedal or four-legged) standing behind a Level 1 hill may
   The 18-by-22 inch mapsheets used in BattleTech are divided                      be partially hidden, and a vehicle, ProtoMech or infantry unit
into six-sided areas called hexes (short for hexagon); whether                     behind a Level 1 hill is completely hidden. Level 2 terrain is
                                                                                                                                                             COMBAT
paper or game board-style, both are called “mapsheets” and                         approximately twelve meters high, roughly the same height
treated identically in the rules. The players use these hexes to                   as a ’Mech. A ’Mech standing behind Level 2 terrain is com-
regulate movement and combat by moving units from hex to                           pletely hidden. Level 3 terrain is approximately eighteen
hex during a turn. Each hex on the mapsheet represents an                          meters high, and so on. The level of a hex is considered equal             HEAT
area of ground 30 meters across (roughly 100 feet).                                to the highest level present in it.
   The forests, rivers, hills, buildings and rough areas on a                         Sublevels: Hexes with levels lower than 0 are referred to
BattleTech mapsheet represent a typical mixture of terrain                         as sinkholes. These hexes are marked in sublevels that cor-              BUILDINGS
found on the habitable worlds of the Inner Sphere. The fol-                        respond to levels in reverse. For example, a Sublevel 1 hex is
lowing symbols represent each type of terrain as described,                        six meters deep, while a Sublevel 2 hex is twelve meters deep,
and the accompanying text gives an overview of the terrain’s                       and so on.

                                                                                                                                                              31
                    If any part of a hex contains a sublevel, the entire hex is consid-
                 ered to be the deepest sublevel marked in it. The exception to this
                 rule occurs if the hex also contains a level greater than 0, in which
                                                                                          WATER
                                                                                             Water hexes are covered by streams, rivers, swamps, ponds or5
                                                                                          lakes. A water hex is defined by depth (see Level Change, p. 48).



                                                                                1
                 case the level of the hill takes precedence as described above.          Depth 0 water is very shallow, only ankle-deep on a ’Mech, and
                                                                                          represents terrain such as streams, swamps or shallow ponds.
                 CLEAR                                                                    Depth 1 water is six meters deep, or one level below ground level
                   Clear terrain represents fields, meadows and other grasslands.         (about waist-high on a ’Mech). Depth 2 water is twelve meters
                 The ground is firm and may be gently rolling, but its level does         deep, enough to just cover a ’Mech. Depth 3 water is eighteen
                 not change significantly from one side of the hex to the other.          meters deep, and so on. Even when a shallow stream fills only
                   If a hex is not clearly marked as containing another terrain type,     part of a hex, that entire hex is considered a water hex.



                                                                                2
                 assume it is clear.                                                        Water hexes effectively have two levels, the surface of the
                                                                                          water and the bed (or floor) of the body of water they represent.
                 LIGHT WOODS                                                              The surface level is equal to the level of the hex. The bed level is
                    Light woods terrain is covered with sparse trees up to twelve         equal to the level of the hex minus the depth of the water. For
                 meters tall. Most units cannot cross this terrain as easily as clear     example, for a Depth 2 river in a Level 3 hex, the surface is at Level



                                                                                                                                                         6
                 terrain. Unless the wood is relatively large (i.e., covers numerous      3 while the riverbed is at Level 1.
                 hexes), units may have line of sight through light woods. When
                 light woods affect line of sight, they do so for 2 levels above the       PAVEMENT



                                                                                3
                 level of their hex. (See Intervening Terrain, p. 100.)                     A paved hex offers a fairly smooth and very hard surface. Paved
                                                                                          hexes typically include roads, sidewalks and landing fields made
                 HEAVY WOODS                                                              of asphalt, cement or even cobblestone. This terrain increases
                    Heavily wooded terrain is covered thickly with twelve-                the speed of ground vehicles, but running ’Mechs and ground
                 meter-tall trees, making movement through these areas very               vehicles moving at flank speed may skid on paved hexes (see



                                                                                                                                                         7
                 difficult. Light woods often border heavy woods. It is difficult         Movement on Pavement, p. 61).
                 to see through heavy woods. As with light woods, heavy woods
                 affect line of sight for 2 levels above the level of their hex (see       ROADS



                                                                                4
                 Intervening Terrain, p. 100).                                              Roads are narrow strips of pavement that pass through other
                                                                                          terrain. All the rules that apply to a paved hex apply to a road
                 ROUGH                                                                    hex, except that units must move from one road or paved hex
                   Rough terrain represents broken, rocky and jumbled ground.             to another road or paved hex to be considered “on” the road. A
                 Though firm, the uneven surface makes it more difficult to cross         unit not on the road in a hex is considered to be moving on the
                 than clear terrain. Commonly encountered near cliffs and bluffs,         underlying terrain type.
                 rough ground may also be formed by the destruction of woods.

   When a road passes over water or over terrain that is lower                 This terrain type represents a variety of tracks, from tradi-
than the road level, that road is considered a bridge. Units                tional steel rails on wooden ties to advanced extruded fer-
moving along a road may use a bridge and so ignore the                      rocrete troughs for maglevs and more. All ground vehicles          INTRODUCTION
normal terrain restrictions and movement penalties they                     and ´Mechs treat railroad hexes as rough terrain, but railroad
otherwise would suffer while moving through the underlying                  hexes have no effect on any other unit’s movement and do
terrain (for example, through a water hex or across changes                 not affect line of sight.                                          COMPONENTS
of level). The Construction Factor of a bridge represents the
amount of weight each hex of the bridge can bear. Any bridge
not strong enough to support the weight of the crossing unit
                                                                                              COUNTERS

will collapse. (See Collapse, p. 176 in Buildings.)                                                                                              THE GAME

BUILDINGS                                                                      Certain terrain features, such as rubble—or buildings, if

  Buildings are usually multi-hex terrain, representing                     players wish to add buildings to a mapsheet that does not have       MOVEMENT
a variety of different structures, as described below (see                  building hexes already printed on it—can be represented on
Buildings, p. 166, for specific rules covering buildings). A build-         the map by cardboard or paper counters. You can make your
ing hex has the structure’s type and level printed on it.                   own, or purchase generic counters for such use. Rather than          AEROSPACE
                                                                            printing these kinds of features on the maps, using counters         MOVEMENT
Light Buildings                                                             to represent them allows the players to alter their locations
  Light buildings generally represent small wooden or sheet-                before the game begins, based on the requirements of the
                                                                                                                                                  COMBAT
metal structures through which most ’Mechs and vehicles can                 scenario being played or on mutual player agreement.




                                                                        8
move with little trouble.                                                      The following text offers an overview of counters used in
                                                                            the game and their effects. Specific rules regarding effects on
Medium Buildings                                                            movement and combat appear in the appropriate sections                 HEAT
  Constructed from stone, heavy wood and metal, medium                      later in this book.
buildings represent light industrial structures that offer more
substance than light buildings. Their heavier construction                  BUILDINGS AND BRIDGES                                                BUILDINGS
materials mean they can take more damage than light build-                     Buildings and bridges represented on a map by counters—
ings before being reduced to rubble.                                        as opposed to already printed on a map sheet—function just

Heavy Buildings                                                             Bridge Movement, pp. 166 and 62, respectively).
   Usually part of industrial complexes, heavy buildings are
constructed of reinforced concrete, built to bear large loads.              RUBBLE                                                               COMBAT
All but the heaviest ’Mechs can jump onto heavy buildings                      Weapons fire, fire damage and physical damage inflicted by        VEHICLES
without collapsing the structure.                                           BattleMechs can reduce a building to rubble. A rubble hex is
                                                                            difficult to move through.
Hardened Buildings                                                                                                                               SUPPORT
                                                                               Only the destruction of a building results in a rubble hex.       VEHICLES
   The builders intentionally strengthen hardened buildings                 The reduction of terrain other than a building results in a
to withstand combat. Of all types of buildings, hardened                    rough hex (see Clearing Woods, p. 112).
structures can bear the most weight and sustain the most                                                                                         INFANTRY
damage before being reduced to rubble.

                                                                                                                                                  UNITS
                                                                              BattleTech requires players to use two six-sided dice, pref-
                                                                            erably of two different colors. If the situation requires the
                                                                                                                                                  CREATING
                                                                            player to roll one die, the rules indicate this in shorthand as
                                                                                                                                                 SCENARIOS
                                                                            1D6. Unless otherwise noted, the abbreviation 2D6 means the
                                                                            player rolls both dice and adds the results together.

Father,                                                                          I know such thoughts are trivial next to the horrors unfolding, but
   While current events have conspired to keep me away from this              in such minutiae sanity can be found.
project, your words return to me all too often and I strive to find              Take care, father.
the time to update and expand my Secret History of the Clans. In
an attempt to calm my mind following the most recent fighting, I                Phelan
reread a passage I have perused at least a dozen times, only to spot            Arc-Royal, 14 July, 3069
an error that should have been caught long ago.
   Below is a supposed direct excerpt from Nicholas Kerensky’s own            Excerpted from the papers of Nicholas Kerensky:
papers, and yet the timeline is off. We have irrefutable proof that
the OmniMech debuted in 2854, while battle armor saw its first use               We must eradicate all vestiges of the tainted past so that we
in 2868, and then the Horses first fielded their genetically enhanced         can forge ourselves into a people worthy of our destiny. We must
Elementals in 2870. And yet Nicholas died in 2834, a decade before            kill the traditions that blinded and crippled us for so long, for the
dedicated research initiated the development of the OmniMech.                 old ways are like shackles that hinder our true strength. We are
   Is this simply an overzealous scientist casteman transcribing              a new people, reborn in the fire, and we must follow new ways.
material and updating it appropriately? Or, as you and I have                    Henceforth, our eight hundred warriors will be divided into
surmised on numerous occasions, is this a move of later ilKhans to            twenty Clans. And they will no longer fight as lances, companies,
make sure that the all-powerful Founder remained all-knowing in               battalions or regiments. Instead, they will enter battle in Points,
word as well as deed?                                                         Stars, Binaries, Clusters and Galaxies.
 SMALL FORMATIONS: POINT, STAR AND NOVA
   Each MechWarrior shall form a Point. In the absence of an OmniMech, two aerospace fighters or five battle-armored Elementals
 shall comprise a Point. (Editor’s Note: Nicholas may not have liked vehicles, but the Clans use them, with two in a Point.) Five Points shall
 form a Star and follow the orders of a Star Commander.
   A Star of OmniMechs and a Star of Elementals shall together form a Nova. Each OmniMech will carry a Point of Elementals
 into battle.

BINARY/TRINARY/SUPERNOVA                                                  LARGE FORMATIONS: CLUSTER AND GALAXY
   Two Stars shall form a Binary. Three shall form a Trinary.                A Cluster shall contain three to five Binaries, Trinaries or
Star Captains shall lead these forces. Nova pairs shall fight as          Supernovas. A Star Colonel shall command each Cluster. Three to
Supernova Binaries, and three Novas shall form a Supernova                five Clusters shall comprise a Galaxy, led by a Galaxy Commander.
Trinary.

                      Though almost annihilated during the FedCom Civil War, a lance of Davion Assault Guards show their indomitable will to defend their realm.

   This section provides the sequence of play for BattleTech                                  and avoid fire. In real-world terms, another ’Mech could easily fit
and presents the basic rules for playing the men and women                                    in that space as well. However, for ease of play, a ’Mech tactically
(MechWarriors, aerospace pilots, vehicle crews, infantry troopers                             controls the hex it occupies even though it does not physically fill
and so on) who pilot or crew the machines of war in BattleTech.                               that space. Therefore, only a single ’Mech is allowed in a hex.
   To begin a game, the players lay out the BattleTech mapsheet(s)                               Weapon ranges provide another example. Players will quickly
on a table or on the floor in a way agreed to by all players, or, if                          realize that the longest-range standard weapon in the game can
using a published scenario, according to the game setup given for                             only hit targets out to thirty hexes (900 meters) from the attacker.
the scenario to be played. This step may include placing buildings                            Real-world primary main battle tank weapons have operational tar-
of varying height and type on the mapsheets, or placing other                                 geting ranges in excess of 4,000 meters. Because BattleTech map-
types of counters. Guidelines for creating your own scenarios                                 sheets are only seventeen hexes long, recreating real-world ranges
appear in Creating Scenarios, p. 256.                                                         on a table would require more than seven mapsheets laid end to
   Next, players should fill out the appropriate record sheets                                end, for a playing space greater than twelve feet in length. Not
for each of their units involved in the battle. The ’Mech, vehicle,                           many people have that type of table space, nor would it provide
aerospace fighter and DropShip descriptions required to fill out                              players with any tactical maneuvering room. Anywhere a player
various unit record sheets can be found in any BattleTech techni-                             might move a unit on the map, an attacker could hit that unit.
cal readout, or players may purchase pre-filled out record sheets                                Finally, the abstractions of real-world factors such as firing
from store.catalystgamelabs.com.                                                              distance often can enhance the aesthetic of the game universe.
   If all players agree, they can create one-of-a-kind units or make                          BattleTech has always been about “in-your-face” combat, which
minor tweaks to existing units using the various construction                                 works best with closer ranges. Players are encouraged to remem-
rules found in TechManual.                                                                    ber such abstractions and not get bogged down in real-world
                                                                                              mechanics and physics. Just enjoy the game!
A NOTE ON SCALE AND THE RULES
  BattleTech turns represent ten seconds of real time, while each                              INDIVIDUAL UNIT RULES
hex on a mapsheet represents thirty meters of a battlefield (for the                             Beginning with Playing the Game and proceeding through
exception, see Aerospace Movement, p. 74). However, players should                            Buildings, the following sections present the rules as they apply
note that such “real world” terms are abstractions when applied to                            to all units equally, with specific exceptions noted in each section
the board game. BattleTech is a game, not a detailed simulation.                              under appropriate headers. ProtoMechs, infantry, Combat
Therefore, the real world must take a back seat to game play—for                              Vehicles, Support Vehicles and aerospace units have additional
simplicity, length of play, space required and simple enjoyment.                              specific rules, which appear in their respective sections.
   For example, while only a single ’Mech can occupy a hex, it does                              Because BattleMechs are the primary focus of BattleTech, the rules
not actually take up the entire hex. A 30-meter-wide hex offers                               often focus on ´Mechs first (movement, combat and so on), and then
plenty of room for a twelve-meter-tall ’Mech to move around                                   provide specifics for other types of units.

                                                                                                                                                     RARA
       At the beginning of the Movement Phase (Ground), Side
    A has eight units and Side B has five units. Side A wins
                                                                                                                                                                                HEAT
    Initiative. Before the first pair of movements, Side A does
    not have double or more the number of units Side B has
    remaining to move, and so Side B moves one unit, then
    Side A moves one unit. Now Side A has seven units left to                                                                                                                BUILDINGS
    move, while Side B has four units left to move. Because
    Side A still does not have twice as many units left, each side
    again moves one unit. Before the third pair of movements,                                                                                                               PROTOMECHS
    Side A has six units left to move, twice as many as the three
    units remaining for Side B. This means Side A must now
    move two units for every one that Side B moves.                                                                                                                           COMBAT
       The following table shows how many units each side                                                                                                                     VEHICLES
    moves during this Movement Phase (Ground).

                   WARRIORS                                                                                                                                                  SCENARIOS


   The soldiers who pilot BattleMechs are called MechWarriors;                                                                                                                PAINTING
aerospace and conventional fighter jockeys are simply called                                                                                                                 MINIATURES
pilots. A BattleMech or fighter is knocked out of action if its
MechWarrior or pilot is killed or seriously injured, even if the
machine itself suffers only minor damage.                                                                                                                                      INDEX
   Vehicles and DropShips are piloted by crews (several indi-
viduals who work together to make a unit function properly),
and infantry units represent a platoon of individual soldiers.
The battlefield performance of these unit types degrades
as their crews or platoons take damage. For purposes of
BattleTech game rules, the term “warrior” represents the             Leftenant Terrero of the Third Crucis Lancers suits up and prepares to board his Davion AS7-D Atlas.

                                                                            MARGIN OF SUCCESS/FAILURE (MOS/MOF)
                                                                               Most actions in BattleTech have simple pass/fail results. The
                GAME TERMS                                                  outcomes of some actions, however—most notably those involv-
                                                                            ing aerospace units—depend on the amount by which the roll
  The following terms describe important concepts used in the               succeeds or fails. To determine the Margin of Success or Margin of
BattleTech rules.                                                           Failure, compare the roll result to the target number. If the result
                                                                            is greater, the difference is the Margin of Success. If the result is
BASE TO-HIT NUMBER                                                          less, the difference is the Margin of Failure. If the dice roll matches
  A unit’s base to-hit number equals its Gunnery Skill rating (see          the target number, that result is a simple success.
Gunnery Skill Rating, p. 40).
DAMAGE VALUE                                                                     It is important to note that these terms are used exclusively.
   Damage Value is defined as the specific amount of damage                    Anytime ‘altitude’ is used in the rules, it is referring to airborne
each weapon inflicts on a target. The Weapon and Equipment                     aerospace units, while ‘elevation’ refers to airborne non-
Tables list the Damage Values of each weapon.                                  aerospace units and ‘levels’ refers to the height of terrain, or       INTRODUCTION
   Some weapons do not deal their full Damage Value to                         ground units, as appropriate.
a single location. Instead, the points of damage from the                        Heat: The only exception to this rule is heat, which is mea-
weapon are divided into Damage Value groupings (each                           sured in levels (see p. 158).                                          COMPONENTS
weapon has its own way to determine how the Damage Value
is divided up) and then each grouping is assigned to a sepa-                   Sublevels and Depths
rate location (see Cluster Weapons, p. 113).                                     A subset of levels, Sublevel refers to any level below 0, while       PLAYING
                                                                               Depth refers to water hexes.                                           THE GAME

armor” or “armor points,” or “armor points remaining” to refer-                are used interchangeably. Whether the term attack path or
ence the number of armor circles in a location, or the number                  flight path is used, in both instances it refers to a single row        AEROSPACE

of remaining armor circles in a location.                                      of hexes across a ground mapsheet over which an aerospace

                                                                                     Orkney represented a key world in the corridor

the ninety-degree slope with her Linebacker                           and Tomans—all prime staging planets for the race to reclaim
Prime. One hundred and eighty meters away                            humanity’s birthworld. The Falcons were too busy attacking the
stood the enemy Hellbringer. Green, leathery cactus-                 Lyrans to guard their takings. Such careless strategy, so common
shaped trees the size of ‘Mechs stood in clumps all around           among the Pigeons—it was laughable.
them, towering sentinels to the chunks of crystalline formations       Shayla’s Star was part of a larger force sent in to take the
that broke apart beneath the weight of her stumbling machine.        busiest of Orkney’s spaceport cities. Four of them already lay
   Star Commander Shayla of Striker Star Kappa Alpha powered         under Clan Wolf’s control. New Hampshire was the last. The city
back her Linebacker’s offensive as the ground gave way. The sixty-   rested amid the crystal mountain passes, its defenses dependent
five ton ‘Mech skittered downward several meters, sending up         on the planet’s natural landscape.
dust, rocks and the carcasses of groups of spires that resembled       Shayla smiled as the enemy’s laser fire went wild. In keeping
batches of dried coral.                                              the skidding Linebacker in sight, the Hellbringer had reached an
  “Savashri!” Shayla hissed. A wrong move in her descent and she     unsteady ledge of granite and crystal. The Falcon ‘Mech wobbled,
could topple over, careening end over end, likely crashing into      almost backpedaling. She watched as it found better footing fifty
one of the cactus trees before hitting bottom.                       meters closer to her Linebacker, but still one hundred and fifty
   Her left foot and leg found a stable ledge against a clump        meters away. Her grin widened. Orkney belongs to Clan Wolf.
of the odd trees. Just as the ‘Mech stopped, the Jade Falcon           Heat encased her in the cockpit of her ‘Mech. She reveled in
Hellbringer above her fired two of its medium lasers. A larger       the sensation. Cleansing. Victorious.
cluster of trees thirty meters higher on the mountainside took
   Her heads-up showed the other members of her team
nearby. Daryna in her Nova Prime was engaging a Jade Falcon
Black Lanner. Turan had neatly dispatched an Adder Prime with
his Kit Fox Prime. The enemy Adder lay in a smoking heap near
the lake below. Some of the cactus trees reached higher than
the ‘Mechs, with rounded tops that resembled giant green
mushrooms, each decorated with silver spikes as large as a
man’s head. The Star’s battlesuits used this on both sides for
hide and strike moves.
   The Hellbringer steadied itself on the treacherous footing.
Nearly hugging the mountainside, Shayla could not launch
her Streak SRM-4. She braced the Linebacker up on its right
elbow just enough to target the Hellbringer. Firing at a target
within one hundred and fifty meters, the missiles’ accuracy
would be sketchy at best. A range of two hundred and ten
meters would be preferable, but ideal situations came rarely
in battle.
   Shayla fired the LRMs from her left torso. Several went
wild, as she had expected, but one detonated against the
enemy’s left leg, throwing the Hellbringer off balance. Fire
sizzled around the Hellbringer’s leg as it re-evaluated its
precarious position.
  “By my hand you die, freebirth,” came Warren’s voice from
the cockpit of his Adder. Shayla nodded at her warrior’s work.
The enemy Nova Prime came tumbling down the
mountainside, losing metal along with mounted
weapons as its armor smashed into jutting granite
and thick cactus-trees. An arm bolted free and
stuck against the base of a large trunk that
clung to the mountainside.
   Targeting klaxons filled Shayla’s cockpit as
the Hellbringer fired on her. SRMs flew at
her position. One of them hit a nearby Wolf
battlesuit, sending the suit and its wearer
into an airborne pirouette. Another missile
struck the rocks, sending granite shrapnel
into the Linebacker’s right side. Shayla heard
the clanging of rock on armor as the shards
lodged in place.
   Two of the enemy missiles struck her ‘Mech,
smashing into the left stabilizing actuator joint
of the leg she had been using to cement her
position against the ninety-degree slope. The
computer warned her of imminent joint failure.
She silenced it with a slap of her hand. If she lost
that joint, she would fall.
   She refused to end up at the bottom of the
mountain. With an eye on her HUD to track the
Hellbringer’s movements, she scanned the mountain-
side around her. Her Linebacker’s left foot was braced
against a cactus tree, her right leg up with its knee
bent and foot pressed into the side of the mountain.
She had to reverse the stress on each leg without
straining the damaged joint. One wrong move and
the leg would buckle.
   A voice came through her neurohelmet’s speakers,
cold and haughty, issuing a batchall. “Once again the Jade
Falcons have shown our mettle on the battlefield against the
unweaned cubs who seek to take what is ours. I will defeat
you, Star Commander. In a few seconds I will send you down the             “Savashri!” Shayla threw the joysticks and pedals forward,
mountain.”                                                               pushing her ‘Mech into the mountainside. If she tipped away
   Surat. Shayla sneered at the Hellbringer through the ferroglass       from the mountain, her own weight would destroy her.
of her cockpit. Beyond it she could see the movement of battle-             The ploy worked. The Linebacker skidded a few meters down-
suits, popping up and down like grasshoppers in a spring field as        ward on its belly until the right foot found purchase against the
they engaged their jump jets.                                            base of another cactus tree. Now she was in front of one instead
  “Only your arrogance, little pigeon, leads you to believe you          of behind it. She stopped, clouds of dust surrounding her cockpit.
have won. A single victory is but a drop in the whole of the             She heard the plink of rocks as they bounced off the cockpit and
mightiest of Clans. The Wolves own this world—this battle is the         shoulders and tumbled to the lake below.
last of our victories here before we scour the Jade Falcon infesta-         She lay on her stomach, her ‘Mech’s arms out at its sides. Only
tion from this corridor of space. It is you who are defeated, Star       the harness held her in her couch. It bit into her sternum between
Commander. Bow to the might of Clan Wolf!”                               her breasts. Orange, red and green lights illuminated her controls.
   His response to her rebuke was to swing around with his re-           She took several deep breaths before pushing forward with her
maining medium lasers. As she had anticipated, the Hellbringer’s         joysticks, moving the ‘Mech out from the mountain on its elbows.
aim was less than accurate. He struck one of the larger nearby           The HUD showed both PPC cannon mounts intact and fully
cactus trees, with a root stalk as thick as her Linebacker’s ankle.      charged. But she was in no position to fire them.
The thick skin split as chunks of it flew in several different direc-       The Hellbringer was attempting to close the distance between
tions. Most of it hit Shayla’s ‘Mech, splattering it with viscous        them. Shayla could see the massive ‘Mech in the upper right
red fluid.                                                               corner of her shield, its fully charged PPCs glowing white. Three
   Now was her best chance to move. The HUD gave stress                  hundred and forty meters lay between her and the Falcon. Every
points in flowing numbers. She overlaid her internal readout             step brought the Hellbringer closer.
with a look at all systems on her schematic. The left actuator              The Falcon’s voice came again, arrogant as ever. “Again I show
flashed orange, not yet critical, but close. She had two move-           you the might of the Jade Falcon, little milk-cub. Once in range,
ments at best before it gave.                                            I will send your ‘Mech down the mountain where you belong.
   She needed to create her own cover in case her antagonist             Wolves should not dare the lofty heights—they are reserved for
realized what she intended and fired again at her leg. Shayla            the Falcons that fly above you. Order your men to surrender.”
targeted the Falcon ‘Mech’s right leg, which now stood in the               Shayla almost laughed at the demand. This surat had de-
same position as her left and showed damage. She would hit that          feated nothing. She could still see on her HUD the movement
leg as she made two swift moves, shifting the stress off her own         of all her ‘Mechs, a full roll call. Of her antagonist’s Star, there
damaged joint onto her right leg.                                        remained only four.
   She swiveled the rear-mounted small laser, its sighting limited         “Star Commander,” came the stern, deep voice of Joachim over
by her tilted position, and targeted the crystalline granite at the      the private channel. “You look like you could use support, quiaff?”
Hellbringer’s foot. The laser’s initial hit caused the encased air         “You have dispatched your enemy, quiaff.” Shayla knew he had.
and gas that formed the crystal to explode, sending up spears            She had seen it on the HUD.
of shrapnel into the Hellbringer’s torso. The laser’s heat also            “Aff.”
melted the crystal, which flattened beneath the enemy ‘Mech’s              “Aff.”
supporting foot.                                                            What they were about to do broke zellbrigen. But as a Wolf,
   She could not afford to hit one of the trees. She had seen a          Shayla knew that at some moments, she could honorably accept
PPC strike to a tree when a Jade Falcon ‘Mech fired at it. The tree      aid given by other members of her Star.
had shredded into rubbery strips that clung to the crystalline              The enemy Star Commander—as inflexible as he was ar-
rocks like green webbing. The resulting mess had tripped both            rogant—did not expect a third combatant. He had no defense
machines, entangling their limbs and sending them down the               prepared against the Alpha Adder’s large pulse laser as it melted
mountainside. No—Shayla wanted a clear shot at her target. She           armor along his left arm and torso. Joachim followed up with a
wanted to see the Pigeon’s eyes widen when he knew a Wolf had            spear of fire across the Falcon’s path, between the Hellbringer and
claimed her prize.                                                       Shayla. She used this distraction to push the Linebacker onto its
   The Hellbringer slid as the crystals turned to molten rock.           right shoulder until it faced the Hellbringer, which had begun
   Shayla slammed down her right footpedal while pressing her            returning fire at the Adder. Along with it, the enraged Falcon
joysticks forward to support her weight, shifting the Linebacker’s       sent a stream of curses and insults through the open channel.
position while maintaining balance. The ‘Mech felt sluggish, and        “Freebirth! Surat! You have broken zellbrigen!”
she gritted her teeth. The horizon dials tilted dangerously to the          Shayla checked her PPCs, then set her firing reticule over the
left, toward the drop. She threw all the ‘Mech’s weight onto the         Hellbringer’s chest. Joachim’s fire had loosened the Hellbringer’s
left joint, angled the hip and then moved the right leg down and         position and sent it closer to her. Two hundred and ten meters.
back. The left knee bent as the huge machine’s weight transferred       “You are right, little pigeon,” she said after opening her own
swiftly from left to right.                                              channel. “Without the mountainside, I would have beaten you
   The horizon dials steadied. She powered up both PPCs as she           fairly in a batchall of equals. But we are not in the realm of fair-
glanced at her heads-up to see the Hellbringer—                          ness—not with the Word of Blake winning on all fronts. Your
   The cactus-tree below her right foot gave, weakened by the            ineptitude at understanding your enemy once again is your

 for an answer.                                                           one, the jump jets offered a slight advantage.

 mountainside. Looking at her HUD, she saw a Jade Falcon Nova             The Nova had started firing medium lasers at Shayla and the

 a stark contrast to the green, spiraling forest.                         mostly missed Shayla, though some glanced off her shoulder.

   Conflicting Terrain and Levels: Even though all published                                                                                      COMPONENTS
mapsheets have level and terrain clearly marked, situa-
tions may arise that involve conflicting levels or terrain. For
example during a tournament, one player may place a map                                                                                              PLAYING
normally, while another places a map with the blank side                                                                                            THE GAME
up—effectively creating a single map filled with Level 0, clear
hexes. Players may also face this situation when using maps
                                                                                                                                                   GROUND
they have created.
                                                                                                                                                  MOVEMENT
   In such cases, the level of a hex is considered equal to the
highest level present in it. If any part of a hex contains a sub-
level, the entire hex is considered to be the deepest sublevel                                                                                     AEROSPACE
marked in it. The exception to this occurs if the hex also con-                                                                                    MOVEMENT
tains a level greater than 0, in which case the level of the hill
takes precedence as described above.

LATERAL SHIFT (FOUR-LEGGED ’MECHS ONLY)
  Four-legged ’Mechs may also move laterally, or sideways,
without changing their facings. A quad ’Mech making a lateral
shift moves into an adjacent hex that is not directly to its front or                             FACING
rear, while retaining its original facing.

of its turn may make one attempt to stand using the Minimum             opposing player.
Movement exception on page 49.
  A ’Mech may stand during the same Movement Phase that it              FACING CHANGE

during the Movement Phase. A ’Mech may attempt to stand                 degree turn costs a unit 3 MP.
even if missing one leg, or one arm and one leg, or both arms,

   If the player wanted to move the BattleMech into Hex D              into the clear hex (1 MP) and then, after climbing 1 level (1   COMPONENTS
(without going backward), the BattleMech would have to                 MP), enter clear Hex B (1 MP).
make a two-hexside facing change, at a cost of 2 MP.                      It costs 6 MP for the BattleMech to move to Hex C:

                                                                       straight forward into a clear hex (1 MP), then straight          MOVEMENT
                                                                       forward again into a clear hex (1 MP), then change facing
                                                                       one hexside to the left (1 MP) and then, after climbing one
                                                                                                                                         COMBAT
                                                                       level (1 MP), enter a heavy woods hex (3 MP).
                                                                          The ’Mech can enter Hex E, moving straight forward into
                                                                       the clear hex (1 MP), then straight forward again into the
                                                                       clear hex (1 MP) and then moving straight forward a final           HEAT
                                                                       time into the light woods Hex E (2 MP). Similarly, the ’Mech
                                                                       can enter Hex F by spending 5 MP: straight forward into the
                                                                       clear hex (1 MP), straight forward again into the clear hex      BUILDINGS
                                                                       (1 MP), change facing one hexside to the right (1 MP) and

                                                                       right (1 MP), then one more hexside to the right (1 MP), and

                                                                                                                                         INFANTRY
                                                                    MOVEMENT DICE
                                                                                                                                        AEROSPACE
                                                          To help remember which ’Mechs moved in what way,                                UNITS
                                                        movement dice are often used. Commonly, white dice
                                                        are used to mark ’Mechs that walked, black dice for those
                                                                                                                                         CREATING
                                                        that ran, and red dice for those that jumped (if using the
                                                                                                                                        SCENARIOS
                                                        optional Sprinting rules, yellow dice are typically used
                                                        when sprinting). The number displayed on the die is the
                                                                                                                                         PAINTING
                                                        Target Movement Modifier generated as a result of the
                                                                                                                                        MINIATURES
                                                        move, with “6” traditionally indicating a TMM of 0 (or
                                                        immobility). Also common is the rule that—like taking
                                                        your hand off the piece you are moving in chess—once                              INDEX
                                                        a movement die is placed, that move cannot be changed.
                                                          It’s best to use dice of a different size and/or color
                                                        than those used for rolling attacks, to prevent confusion.
                                                        During the End Phase, remove all movement dice from
                                                        the board.

1
  MP cost to move along the bottom of the water hex; Piloting Skill Roll required to prevent falling.
2
  Piloting Skill Roll required to prevent damage; infantry pays only 1 MP (except mechanized infantry, which pays 2 MP) to enter any building hex.
3
  If traveling along road; otherwise cost of underlying terrain.
4
  Hovercraft and WiGEs may enter all water hexes along the surface and may enter such hexes using flanking movement.
5
  No cost for infantry.
6
  If a wheeled Support Vehicle lacks the Off-Road chassis modification, then movement costs 1 additional MP per hex.
7
  Wheeled or tracked Support Vehicles with the Amphibious chassis modification can move through any water hex on the surface at a cost of 2 MP (see p. 56).
8
  IndustrialMechs can enter a Depth 2 or greater water hex. However, the IndustrialMechs must mount either a fuel cells, fission or fusion power plant and must mount the
     Environmental Sealing chassis modification to do so. If the IndustrialMech does not meet those requirements, it is considered destroyed if they remain in a Depth 2 or greater
     water hex (or prone in a Depth 1 water hex) in the End Phase of the turn immediately following the turn in which they entered it.
9
  Hover and Wheeled Support Vehicles with either the Monocycle or Bicycle chassis modification can enter a light woods hex.
10
   Infantry pays only 1 MP (except permitted mechanized infantry, which pays 2 MP) to enter any light woods hex.
11
   Infantry pays only 2 MP to enter any heavy woods hex.
12
   VTOL and WiGE vehicles can enter a woods hex provided their elevation is higher than the level of the woods in the hex.
13
   This only applies to WiGE units entering a hex whose level is higher than the unit’s current hex; see Wing-In-Ground-Effect, p. 55, for rules governing entering hexes whose level
     is lower than the unit’s current hex.
14
   Infantry can enter a water hex of Depth 1 or deeper if they are noted as having UMU MPs.

                                                                               VTOL MOVEMENT
                                                                                  Though this type of movement is primarily reserved for VTOLs,
                                                                               other units may share this movement type, such as Sylph battle
                                                                               armor; the rules governing that unit type will cover any additional
                                                                               rules beyond VTOL Movement that apply to movement. A unit
                                                                               using VTOL movement is considered airborne and therefore is
                                                                               harder to hit (see the Attack Modifiers Table, p. 117).
                                                                                  Like other vehicles, a VTOL may move at cruising or flank speed
                                                                               during the Movement Phase (Ground). To make a facing change or
                                                                               enter a new hex, regardless of the underlying terrain type, costs 1 MP.
                                                                               In order to make a facing change or enter a new hex, the VTOL must be
                                                                               at least one elevation above the level of the hex it currently occupies.
                                                                                  A VTOL may move vertically at a cost of 1 MP per elevation that the
                                                                               unit ascends or descends. A VTOL can move any number of elevations
                                                                               up and down in a single hex, as long as it has sufficient MP. Players
                                                                               must record the elevation of each VTOL on its record sheet’s Elevation
                                                                               Track at the end of the Movement Phase (Ground). A VTOL is a Ground
                      • jumping diagram •                                      Combat Vehicle and is always considered to be on the ground map-
                                                                               sheet. It is not an aerospace unit, does not fly using Altitudes, and does

        In the WiGE Movement
    diagram above, a WiGE
    vehicle is airborne at the start of its
    Movement Phase in Hex A on the Deep Canyon
    #2 map. The controlling player wants to move the
    WiGE to Hex B. He expends 1 MP moving into Hex 1 (1 to
    enter the hex; it automatically climbs an elevation). The WiGE
    is now at Level 5, one level above the Level 4 hex. Normally, if
    the player moved into Hex 2, the WiGE would automatically
    descend to Level 1 (one level above the underlying terrain,
    which is Level 0). However, the player does not want to
    descend to that level, because after his vehicle moves into Hex
    3, he would not be able to enter Hex 4. (Because he has not yet
    moved 5 hexes in the turn, he would land in Hex 3 unless he
    keeps moving, and the player does not wish to land there.)

                                                                                                                                                  57
 Buildings
  These stacking rules do not apply to units in the same
building hex on different levels. Within a building hex, apply
the unit stacking limits to each level of the building hex (see
Building Levels, p. 167).

ELEVATION AND DEPTH RULES
   All the above stacking rules apply to the elevation or depth
of every hex, with the exceptions noted below.

Airborne Aerospace Units
  The stacking rules do not apply to aerospace altitude move-
ment (see Stacking, p. 78, in Aerospace Movement).

Unit Heights
   Various units rise a number of levels above
the level of the underlying hex they are in,
depending on the unit type (see Unit Heights,
p. 99, in Combat). These heights must be taken
into consideration when dealing with the
stacking limits of each elevation (or depth) in
a hex.
   Jumping: The only exception to this rule is when jumping
movement is used. Any time jumping movement is used, the
jumping unit may ignore the heights of all ground units for the
purposes of moving through a hex occupied by an enemy unit.
The only exception to this rule is grounded DropShips, which act
as terrain Levels for purposes of how many Jump MP a unit must
have to jump over a grounded DropShip.                                                                     • stacking diagram •

                                                                                Movement Phase: Piloting Skill Rolls required because
                                                                            of movement (entering water, trying to stand up, entering
                                                                            rubble, avoiding falling damage and so on) must be made                    INDEX
                                                                            immediately following the action. Multiple rolls may be
                                                                            required during the ’Mech’s movement for a turn. For example,
                                                                            if a ’Mech is moving through three hexes of Depth 1 water, the
                                                                            player must make a Piloting Skill Roll when the ’Mech enters
                                                                            each water hex. If the ’Mech falls during the Movement Phase
                                                                            and has at least 2 MP remaining, it may attempt to regain its
                                                                            feet that turn.

    Roads that cross a water hex (or span a distance where the             Add +2 to the to-hit modifier for all weapons fire and physi-
underlying terrain of the hex is lower than the level of the bridge)     cal attacks made against a skidding ground unit during the turn
are considered bridges. Bridges are classified as light, medium,         in which it skid. In addition, apply a +1 to-hit modifier for all
heavy or hardened in the same manner as buildings, and have              weapons fire and physical attacks made by a skidding ground
the same range of Construction Factors (CF) as light, medium,            unit during the turn in which it skids.
