SIDESLIPPING
    While superficially similar to skidding, sideslipping units do
not use any of the rules for skidding unless specified otherwise.
Most notably, the to-hit modifiers that occur as a result of a unit                                                                                  INTRODUCTION
skidding are not applied, and as a sideslipping unit is not actu-
ally touching the ground as it moves, it incurs no damage by
sideslipping unless it runs into something.                                                                                                          COMPONENTS
    If a VTOL, WiGE or hover vehicle using flanking move-
ment makes a facing change at any point in the turn and
then attempts to enter a new hex in the same turn, before                                                                                               PLAYING
that vehicle actually enters the hex, the player must make a                                                                                           THE GAME
Driving Skill Roll to see if the unit sideslips.
    If the Driving Skill Roll is successful, the vehicle follows its
                                                                                                                                                      GROUND
desired course. If the Driving Skill Roll fails, the vehicle sideslips                                                                               MOVEMENT
a number of hexes equal to the Margin of Failure in the direc-
tion of the hex that it would have moved to without the facing
change; regardless of the MoF, a vehicle can only sideslip for a                                                                                      AEROSPACE
number of hexes equal to one less than the number of hexes                                                                                            MOVEMENT
entered in the turn prior to a sideslip. For example if a unit has
moved for 3 hexes and then is forced to make a Driving Skill Roll
                                                                                                                                                       COMBAT
with a resulting Margin of Failure of 4 or more, the vehicle could
only sideslip for 2 hexes. A Driving Skill Roll is not required if the
vehicle does not move after it changes facing.
    The player has no opportunity to change the elevation of a                                                                                           HEAT
VTOL or WiGE vehicle (see WiGE below for the exception) that
sideslips before it enters the new hex. Therefore, it is possible
for a vehicle to sideslip into an obstacle that causes it to crash.                          • sideslipping diagram •                                 BUILDINGS

        A Grasshopper in a Level 1 hex attempts to stand during
     the Movement Phase. The MechWarrior fails his Piloting Skill
     Roll, and the BattleMech falls again in the same hex. This
     means the BattleMech fell 0 levels. The player rolls a 1 on                                                                                                                 HEAT
     the Facing After a Fall Table and finds that the BattleMech
     landed on its front, so it takes the falling damage on its
     front. The Grasshopper suffers 7 points of damage (70 tons                                                                                                               BUILDINGS
     divided by 10 equals 7; the number of levels fallen plus 1
     equals 1; 7 x 1 = 7). The player divides the 7 points into one
     5-point grouping and one 2-point grouping. He then uses
                                                                                                                                                                             PROTOMECHS
     the Front column of the BattleMech Hit Location Table to
     determine the exact location of the damage.
                                                                                                                                                                               COMBAT
FALLING DAMAGE TO THE MECHWARRIOR                                                                                                                                              VEHICLES
    To determine if the warrior took damage when the ’Mech fell,
the player makes a second Piloting Skill Roll after every fall. First,
apply all appropriate modifiers to the target number (including                                                                                                                SUPPORT
                                                                                                                                                                               VEHICLES
all pre-existing damage modifiers, as well as any current situ-
ational modifiers such as taking 20+ points of damage in the
current phase, and so on). Then add +1 to the MechWarrior’s
                                                                                                                                                                               INFANTRY
Piloting Skill target number for every level above 1 fallen. If the
die roll result is equal to or greater than this modified target
number, the MechWarrior avoided taking damage. If the result                                                                                                                  AEROSPACE
is less, the MechWarrior takes 1 point of damage.                                                                                                                               UNITS
    Automatic Damage: The only ways in which automatic
damage occurs to a warrior whose ’Mech falls is if the warrior
is unconscious at the time of the fall, if the ’Mech is consid-                                                                                                                CREATING
ered immobile (for example it is shut down) or if the modi-                                                                                                                   SCENARIOS
fied to-hit number for the Piloting Skill Roll to avoid warrior
damage is greater than 12. In those three instances, the                                                                                                                       PAINTING
warrior automatically takes 1 point of damage. In any other                                                                                                                   MINIATURES
instance, there is always a chance that the Piloting Skill Roll
can stop damage to the warrior.
                                                                                                                                                                                INDEX
       The Grasshopper in the previous example has a Piloting
     Skill of 5. It does not have any pre-existing damage, it
     did not take 20 points of damage in this turn, and it fell 0
     Levels, and so no modifiers apply to the Piloting Skill target
     number. The controlling player therefore need only roll 5 or
     better to avoid damaging the MechWarrior.                           A Davion BattleMaster from the Third Crucis Lancers rights itself after skidding into a building.

NEAR ORBIT, DEHRA DUN                                                                                committing the full five Trinaries of
CIRCE, PENTAGON WORLDS                                                                             his force to the battle.
CLAN HOMEWORLDS                                                                                      The Jade Falcons had been accorded
16 SEPTEMBER 3057                                                                                safcon in system. Neither side would risk
                                                                                                WarShips in a naval battle. But once the
   Falling around the planet would                                                             DropShips slipped their moorings, they
have been better. An object in motion,                                                        would become rightful prey. Which suited
circling helplessly in the grip of blind                                                    Ilya well. Safcon to the surface would have
physics, would have been less noticeable                                                  left him with no opportunity to accrue honor.
than a bit of refined metal hanging in geo-                                                The Jade Falcons were attempting to cripple
synchronous orbit.                                                                     the Snow Ravens; claiming they had the right
   No matter how null its energy signature.                                          to retake genetic material traded to that Clan.
   Ilya flexed his muscles, rippling them through                                   Their raid would threaten the entire repository and
a series of isometrics designed to keep him from                                   set a precedent for other Clans to raid as well. The
growing stiff as he sat long hours at the controls of                             Snow Raven genetic store was made up of material
his Bashkir. The flight suit hugged his body close, the                         traded and won from other Clans in the centuries since
sensation hovering at the edge of feeling trapped.                            the Not-Named Clan had destroyed Dehra Dun with a
   Extra layers of insulation had been worked into the                       nuclear strike.
already heavy flight suits for the orbital pickets. There                     Ilya deactivated his neurohelmet’s visor. Sliding the
was no dishonor in harboring as much body heat as pos-                    viewscreen up out of his way, he looked directly at the stars—
sible, to keep reflexes sharp. To hang without energy at                 pinpoints of pure light etched on the ferroglass canopy of his
the edge of the absolute cold of space required intelligent             cockpit. Circe lay below him, its presence betrayed by a faint
marshalling of resources as well as a predator’s patience.            glow and the haze of upper atmosphere barely visible beyond
   A predator. Ilya clung to that image.                              the prow of the Bashkir.
   It was better than imagining himself a buffalo. He had seen           The twenty-ton craft was new to him, yet familiar. His first
herds of the dull herbivores with the males standing, back to         fighter had been a Bashkir, mounted with lasers—paired large
the herd, staring blankly over the waving grasses. Positioned by      and small—for the close personal combat he loved. The ship
instinct to face threats they lacked the wit to imagine.              around him now was armed with a pitiful small laser and twin
   Unlike those food beasts grazing on prairies thirty-six thousand   long-range missile racks. Not a weapons mix he would have
kilometers below, Ilya could imagine the threat bearing down on       chosen. But the defeat that had placed him in this cockpit had
Circe. The Galaxy Commander of the Jade Falcons’ Sigma Galaxy         also robbed him of the status to demand better. That would
had bid the First Falcon Dragoons to take the genetic repository      come after this day’s battle.
at Dehra Dun. The Fifteenth Raven Garrison Cluster had been              The proper showing here would earn him the right to demand
bid to defend. Neither Galaxy Commander had cut down, each            more than sharper claws for a tiny fighter.
   Ilya had earned a proper fighter nearly a decade ago. A
Stooping Raven. Despite Uniform Designation Protocols
requiring the craft be called an Issus, not a single Snow Raven
aerospace pilot ever failed to call the swift and nimble war bird
by its proper name.
   A mere week ago he had challenged for a new prototype of
the Stooping Raven—one that mounted an ATM-9 and a heavy
laser for close combat—and lost. Lost through a subtlety of
strategy on the other pilot’s part that bordered on dishonor-
able subterfuge. Yet even as he formed the partial excuse, Ilya
dismissed it. He’d been out-thought and out-fought in the
simulator dogfight. His opponent, a Bloodnamed warrior, had
defeated him honorably.
   Not so the pilot who had challenged Ilya for his own
Stooping Raven within hours of his defeat. She had been a
giant among the aerospace subcaste—a freakish one point
seven meters tall and heavily muscled. She had not challenged
him to a simulator duel, which would have measured their
piloting skills, but to unaugmented combat.
   Ilya had been foolish to accept so quickly, but the sting of
his earlier defeat prodded him to rashness. And more defeat.
He would never forget his feet kicking uselessly clear of the
floor as she held him aloft from behind, the arm clamped
around his throat stopping blood from reaching his brain. Nor
would he forget the faces of the watching warriors in the Circle
of Equals growing dim as his consciousness fled.
   That she had broken nothing, had left no permanent mark,
made the humiliation all the more bitter.
   Now he knew she waited below, ready to scramble the
Stooping Raven that had been his, while he hung here in the
Bashkir, which had been hers. Though he dared to hope the
Star to which he’d been relegated would prevent the Jade
Falcon onslaught, he knew their purpose was to slow the
invaders while Circe’s true defenders rose to the challenge.
   “Pirate point insertion.”
   The terse information came with no hooting alarms, no
frantic orders. The opponents’ arrival was expected, the war-
riors ready.
   Ilya slapped the neurohelmet’s visor in place.
Instantly open space replaced his view of the stars
from a darkened cockpit. Tied to the Bashkir’s
passive sensor array, the visor of the Snow Raven
flight suit presented all directions in hyperfocused
clarity. Glancing down, he saw Circe, to his left and
right the silvery curve of the upper atmosphere
pressing against the vault of space. Behind him, in the
reverse view just above his line of sight, he could see the
golden halo of the dawn line retreating toward the far
horizon. Only the pressure of the harness and the array of
controls suspended against the stars before him defeated
the illusion of flying unaugmented above his homeworld.
   Information flowed across the bottom of his view: hard data
and images broadcast by automated satellites to the dark and
silent aerospace fighters.
   A Sovetskii Soyuz had appeared at the lunar Lagrange point.
To choose the most obvious arrival point indicated either that
the Jade Falcons lacked imagination or were eschewing point-
less misdirection. Ilya cared only that their choice placed his
Point closest to their landing vector. The WarShip’s screen of
aerospace fighters—two full Stars—deployed even as three of the         war bird. The tactician within him admitted that the ninety-ton
four suckling DropShips slipped from their docking rings.               Scytha would more likely fall to an eighty-five-ton Xerxes than his
   The satellite’s sensors could not discern individual aerospace       forty-ton Stooping Raven.
fighters at range, but as they cleared the WarShip, the larger             His former Stooping Raven.

was identified.                                                         physics cared little for the ideal. To attack, the Bashkirs had to be in

prompting the computer to identify it as a Carrier. Despite its         deep into the atmosphere.
formidable weapons, the Jade Falcons were not willing to bid the           Through his neurohelmet visor, Ilya saw tiny sparks come to life:
valuable vessel in today’s assault.                                     the blue-white markers of his own Star’s IFF transponders coming

of Circe, but formed a complex screen between the ships and the         to maximum thrust. The heavy Snow Raven flight suit, which had

attack order.                                                           constrictions that kept blood flowing against angular accelerations
   The two smaller spheroid DropShips formed on the Lion with           that would have killed an unaugmented Elemental.

than sound navigation would dictate. Ilya’s readout on the Soyuz        matching vectors. Patir, a man half again his age whom he knew

from the satellite’s sensors. A second array activated immedi-             Attacking DropShips and their escorts with light Bashkirs
ately—more distant and thus less exact, but clear enough.               required cooperation—a concept at odds with personal honor,

nized milling. At first he found himself embarrassed on their behalf    When aerospace fighters engaged capital ships, the honor of the
as they stumbled through what should have been a simple forma-          individual warrior flowed through the whole.
tion. Then it occurred to him that the apparent blundering was a           The other Bashkir, armed with extended-range lasers, would
ruse intended to draw impatient defenders out of position with the      target an escort, stripping away the protector. Ilya would then
promise of easy prey.                                                   drive through the gap, fighting past the DropShip’s defenses to

picions. The confusion evaporated and the wandering mass of                A single Bashkir could not bring down a DropShip, but it

his ersatz sight and the coded audio tones of status readouts into      way. Three heartbeats and the salvo was away—ten long-range

battle—his entire life—was reduced to this one moment, guiding          rolled right, peeling away and down, diving into the gravity well
his war bird to its target.                                             the behemoths above him were fighting.
   The bow of the DropShip was useless to him. He needed to get            Circe spread out before him. His tactical display showed swirl-
past its waist, past the escorting fighters, to hit the thinner armor   ing sparks of red and blue-white as the first wave of Jade Falcon
of the aft quarters and the vital bay doors.                            aggressors broke against the rock of the Snow Raven defenders.
   A staccato vibration rose through his acceleration couch as the         Kilometers slipped under his wings as he turned, as tightly

wasting focus, part of his conditioned mind registered his Point        The mighty DropShips shrank to toys, to insects, as his fighter

a hole in the screen protecting the Lion’s midsection.                  Union-C. One run straight in, at speed, minimal evasion. A single

into his couch as the Bashkir leapt in pursuit of the missiles. His     its airframe moaning. Fully operational, he’d be hard pressed by a
target lock alarm sounded and faded as sheer speed carried him          Vandal with position on him. Now...
through the weapons arc of a second Tyre before the other craft            A Vandal from above? Even as he fought his wounded craft and
could fire.                                                             physics, staggering through evasive maneuvers that should have
   Space exploded.                                                      been easy as thought, Ilya wrestled with the mystery.
   Blinding flash after blinding flash—four, six, ten white-hot fire-      A trio of medium lasers burned into the rear edge of the ravaged
balls—as the Lion’s aft quarter anti-missile battery destroyed Ilya’s   wing, all but severing it. Giving up all hope of evasion, Ilya strug-
opening salvo.                                                          gled to keep his craft aloft. He didn’t need his diagnostics to know
   Ilya grinned.                                                        it was futile. He could feel the Bashkir dying around him.
   The thin air reduced the shockwaves to vibrations. The dust of          The Carrier. The Sovetskii Soyuz had carried the Vandals as cargo.
debris rattled and whistled off his invisible canopy as the Bashkir     Under cover of what he’d mistaken for confusion, they’d been
powered through the expanding cloud of destruction. So close            transferred to the Carrier and brought in for close launch.
the main guns would have difficulty tracking him, too fast for the         The Bashkir’s stuttering flight became a spiral, the horizon
anti-missile system to react and retarget, Ilya drove in, closer than   replaced by the plains of Circe. Sure of its victory, the Vandal broke
reason allowed, and unleashed a double flight at minimum range.         off, not even granting him the honor of a final salute as the pilot
   He saw a snapshot image of the impacts stitching across the          went in search of other prey.
armored portal. Then he was flying for his life.                           Three defeats in the space of seven days. First by superior tactics,
   Collision alarms hooted as he sideslipped, passing close enough      then by superior strength, and now by both in concert.
to the aft laser battery to count its rivets. The massive column of        Ilya looked at the ejection lever and considered whether his life
superheated plasma slowing the giant DropShip’s descent nearly          was worth saving.
blinded him before the visor compensated. Established strategy at

                   A lance of Corsairs joins a combined aerospace fighter force from House Kurita’s Galedon Regulars, along with local militia support, to intercept incoming raiders.

   Movement for aerospace units is more complex than for                                                Atmospheric
ground units. Different movement systems apply depending                                                  The term “atmospheric” serves as a prefix to other game
on whether a unit is operating in space, in high atmosphere                                             terms—atmospheric movement, atmospheric map, atmospheric
or in low atmosphere. Aerospace movement in atmosphere                                                  turn and so on—to describe any portions of a turn, or any actions
is a subset of these units’ standard movement in micrograv-                                             that occur, in a planet’s atmosphere at high or low altitude.
ity (space); the following rules apply to movement in space
and also provide the basis for all other aerospace movement.                                            Ground Map
   BattleTech aerospace units (including conventional fighters,                                           A ground map represents the mapsheets used by BattleTech
Airships and Fixed-Wing support craft) change their position                                            ground units.
and location on the mapsheet by applying thrust to control
their velocity. During the Movement Phase (Aerospace) of                                                Large Craft
each turn, each player must choose how he will apply thrust                                               As defined in the Components section, “Large Craft” is a general
to control his unit’s position. As with ground units, the player                                        description used to refer to any aerospace unit that weighs more
must announce what movement mode he is using when it                                                    than two hundred tons (such as a DropShip or WarShip).
is his turn to move an aerospace unit. Unlike ground units,                                               The term “Large Craft” is used in these rules when the rules
aerospace units must move in a specific order during the                                                apply to more than one type of Large Craft, in order to make the
Movement Phase (Aerospace). All DropShip movement is                                                    integration of additional Large Craft (such as WarShips, which are
completed before any other aerospace unit moves. Next,                                                  described in Strategic Operations) simpler. Whenever the rules
small craft, Fixed-Wing and Airship Support Vehicles move.                                              only apply to DropShips, the term DropShip is used; WarShips
Aerospace and conventional fighters move last. Conventional                                             cannot operate in atmosphere, and so all rules for atmospheric
fighters, Airships and Fixed-Wing Support Vehicles can only                                             movement use the term DropShip.
operate in atmosphere (see Atmospheric Movement, p. 78).

   Space turns—those where the aerospace action does not occur
in atmosphere—represent one minute of “real time,” meaning
                                                                                               THRUST POINTS
that six ground turns occur between each space turn. Each hex                        Each unit capable of movement has two thrust values. Safe
on a space map represents roughly eighteen kilometers.                            thrust is the number of Thrust Points a unit can spend in a single
  An atmospheric turn equals a ground turn of ten seconds, with                   turn without suffering adverse effects. Maximum thrust is the
each atmospheric hex representing five hundred meters (roughly                    total number of Thrust Points a unit can spend in one turn and
corresponding to one BattleTech mapsheet).                                        is equal to 1.5 times the safe thrust value (round up). Spending
  This information is provided solely for reference. Players should               a number of Thrust Points greater than the safe thrust value
remember to not let these “real-world” numbers come between                       degrades the unit’s handling, making it harder to control and
them and having fun.                                                              increasing the difficulty of targeting enemy units (see Aerospace

                                                                                  USING THRUST POINTS
   Unlike ground units, aerospace units do not expend Movement                       In space, each unit travels at a constant speed and heading unless
Points (MP) to enter hexes. Instead, aerospace units have a velocity              thrust from the engines is used or the unit is affected by the pull of a
that indicates the number of hexes through which they must travel.                planet’s gravity (see p. 80). At the beginning or end of a unit’s move-
   In the frictionless vacuum of space, a moving unit continues in                ment, it can spend Thrust Points to change its velocity or facing. For
a straight line at a constant speed unless affected by an outside                 simplicity, spending thrust does not consume a unit’s fuel.
force. This application of force can alter velocity or direction. It
may be a natural force, such as gravity, or a generated force, such               Changing Velocity
as the thrust from a unit’s engines. A similar principle applies in                  Each Thrust Point spent increases or decreases the unit’s veloc-
atmosphere, with momentum carried forward from one turn                           ity by 1. Aerospace units have no maximum velocity, though the
to the next. However, atmospheric friction drag will steadily                     size of the playing area creates practical limits. A unit’s velocity
decrease a unit’s velocity unless engine thrust is applied.                       cannot be reduced below zero, because aerospace craft cannot
                                                                                  move backward.
MOVEMENT DIRECTION                                                                   Velocity changes made before movement affect the current
   An aerospace unit can move forward into the hex it is facing.                  turn’s movement. Velocity changes made at the end of movement
It cannot move backward or into any other hex unless it first                     take effect in the following turn. A unit that changes velocity at
changes its facing (see below).                                                   the end of one turn cannot change velocity again at the start of
                                                                                  the next turn.

ers, DropShips and so on), regardless of the controlling player(s),                    first two turns of the game, spending 5 Thrust Points in the first
move only in their subphase. Within each subphase, the Unequal                         turn and 6 Thrust points in the second; the unit ends the second
Number of Units rule applies (see p. 39). The following list shows                     turn with a Velocity of 11.
the movement subphases in order (all players start at 1, resolve                           During the Movement Phase (Aerospace) of Turn 3, John’s
all movement, move to 2 and so on).                                                    fighter still has a Velocity of 11. He can spend Thrust Points
                                                                                       at the start or at the end of the unit’s movement. If he spends
1. DropShips                                                                           Thrust Points at the beginning to brake, the number of points
2. Small Craft                                                                         spent will subtract from the unit’s velocity this turn.
3. Fighters                                                                               John realizes he needs to move in the opposite direction
                                                                                       quickly, but doesn’t want to chance forcing a Control Roll. He
                                                                                       spends 5 Thrust Points at the start of his turn (his maximum
                                                                        PMEPME        Safe Thrust) to bring his unit’s Velocity down to 6; now he must
                                                                                       move the fighter six hexes. Unless he spends additional thrust to
                                                                                       change facing, the fighter must move forward in a straight line.
                                                                                           If John had chosen to spend his fighter’s Thrust Points at
                                                                                       the end of the unit’s movement, the fighter would need to
                                                                                       move 11 hexes, after which it would end the movement with a
                                                                                       Velocity of 6. That means the fighter would start the following
                                                                                       Movement Phase (Aerospace) with Velocity 6.
                                                                                           Finally, John can choose not to spend any Thrust Points,
                                                                                       moving his fighter the required 11 hexes in a straight line for
                                                                                       Turn 3’s movement and ending the turn with Velocity 11. His
                                                                                       fighter then starts Turn 4 with that velocity.

sides that point away from the planetary surface map edge—or
else make an immediate Control Roll. However, a spheroid unit                            HIGH-ALTITUDE TABLE
can hover by pointing its nose to face one of the two hexsides
facing away from a planet and reducing its Velocity to 0. The unit
must spend 2 Thrust Points each turn to offset gravity. While the          Hex Row                    Altitude (km)              Max. Safe Velocity
spheroid unit is hovering, this thrust will not increase the unit’s        Ground                          0–17                           2
velocity. A hovering spheroid may move into adjacent hexes

without changing facing at a cost of 3 Thrust Points per hex (and
without having to pay to offset gravity).                                  Row 2                          36–53                           6

                                                                        LOW-ALTITUDE MOVEMENT
                                                                           The following rules apply to movement on a low-altitude map,
                                                                        modifying the standard rules where appropriate. Units entering a
                                                                        ground hex on the high-altitude map move to the low-altitude map.
                                                                        In each turn, a unit must move a number of hexes equal to its veloc-
                                                                        ity, though special maneuvers (see p. 85) can reduce this number.

                                                                        Terrain and Altitude
                                                                           Low-altitude atmospheric operations use the BattleTech hex-
                                                                        based maps, either the terrain side or the blank side. Each hex
DFDF                                                                   of the low-altitude map roughly corresponds to one BattleTech
                                                                        ground mapsheet.
                 Clan Ghost Bear Beta Galaxy aerospace fighters.

ing along a runway or open ground until they reach liftoff velocity.

Proximity Damage
   The fusion exhaust of a DropShip can cause immense damage               DROPSHIP EXHAUST DAMAGE TABLE
to units too close to the ship when it lands or takes off. Any unit
within six hexes of a spheroid unit as it lands or takes off suffers                              Distance       Damage
damage according to the DropShip Exhaust Damage Table below,
broken into 5-point Damage Value groupings and applied using                                     Same Hex      Destroyed
the appropriate hit location table; that is, the “attack” occurs                                  1 Hex           12D6
along the line of sight between the DropShip’s center hex and the                                2 Hexes          10D6
affected unit’s hex. Spheroid DropShips inflict this damage on any
unit within six hexes of the ones adjacent to the central landing                                3 Hexes           8D6
hex, rather than on units within six hexes of the central hex.                                   4 Hexes           6D6
   This damage only applies to units in the rear arc of an aerodyne
DropShip when it takes off.                                                                      5 Hexes           4D6
   Fighters: The exhaust of a fighter landing or taking off causes                               6 Hexes           2D6
no damage.

PMEPM                                                                      •    Only the minimum number of units are moved to ensure
                                                                                 that the stacking rules are not violated in the hexes where

                                                                                 second, and so on.

                                                                                 enter a hex; see p. 49).

                                                                            ing to mount the carrying unit (see Lift-off, p. 88). However, the
                                                                            exhaust of an aerospace unit taking off can cause tremendous

and are not counted for ground units’ stacking purposes in any way.                   Some situations or types of damage can cause an aerospace

LAKE CARANTHA, ALRAKIS                                                   Olivares studied the fallen machine for an instant. The pilot
DIERON MILITARY DISTRICT                                               would be rattled, dazed.
DRACONIS COMBINE                                                         Ignoring the shells falling all around him, Olivares strode
24 APRIL 3039                                                          forward, crouched down and pointed his left arm at the Locust’s
                                                                       canopy. He never decided to fire, but suddenly emerald light
    The powerful crump of Tai-sa Esau Olivares’ heavy artillery was melting through the ferroglass and blasting the cockpit into
 rolled over the sparkling blue water of Lake Carantha like a smoldering wreckage.
 peal of thunder heralding the coming of a great storm. It was a         Olivares spared no thought for the pilot whose life he’d just
 unique sound, deeper and more substantial than the crack of a brutally ended. He planned to give the Twenty-third Arcturan a
 tank’s gun, more sonorous and stately than the deadly rattle of a bloody nose. Faced with such savagery, the Steiner commander
’Mech’s autocannon.                                                    might hesitate: a minute, five, maybe even ten.
    To Olivares it was the sound of salvation.                           And everything was measured in time.
    The rain of carefully placed shells ripped into the Lyran striker    He glanced down at his tactical schematic. Blue triangles were
 company, forcing back the light, fast ’Mechs. Buying his support falling back through the forest. The plan was working. But it
 troops precious time to evac.                                         could only work for so long.
    Time.                                                                Olivares stepped back out of his own artillery’s fire zone,
    From this point on, everything would be measured in time.          pulling out of the woods and taking station with his regiment
    Olivares stalked his Dragon forward, toward the whistle of north of the woodland. What was left of his regiment.
 falling shells. It was a dangerous, desperate maneuver, but now         Olivares was a big man, two meters of lean muscle crammed
 was the time for dangerous, desperate maneuvers. He came into his Dragon’s tight cockpit. He rubbed his chin, feeling the
 upon a flat white Locust, the light ’Mech painted in the colors stubble against his palm. During his long journey to respect-
 of the Twenty-third Arcturans—the Frost Giants. The battered ability he’d given up the nose stud and the blood-red scharacki
 Locust was moving back, pushing its way through spruce and feather. But shave? That was asking too damn much.
 beech, and some trees that looked like giant orange willows.            He glanced out his canopy. Fate had sent them a gorgeous
    Trying to get clear.                                               summer day. Sunlight glistened on the surface of the blue lake
    Olivares pulled his reticle over the light ’Mech’s back. The like spun gold. Sigmundrac’s white skyline was visible on the far
 symbol flashed gold and his finger curled around his secondary side. Olivares knew if he cracked his hatch he’d catch the perfume
 trigger. His autocannon/5 tore into the Locust’s rear armor, fol- of blooming wildflowers carried on a cool breeze.
 lowed by twin beams of emerald fire. Heat spiked in his cockpit,        Only the desperate roar of combat marred the illusion.
 but he held the shot despite the sweat that stung his eyes and          Eight days before, a surprise attack by the Elsies had pushed
 made his hand slippery on the trigger. Despite the shrill cry of      his troops out of Sigmundrac. Olivares had lost a company of
 the heat alarm.                                                      ’Mechs and a battalion of infantry. And now the Lyrans were
    The Locust shuddered and went down, with a crash that shook driving him toward the Caradhradene Mountains, where they’d
 the trees and drowned out the roar of artillery.
 crush his force like a piece of iron worked between a hammer
 and an anvil.
    The battle was over. But with the planetary HPG in ene-
 my-held territory, Olivares could not request permission to
 retreat off-world. He pushed the thought away. Allowing his
 troops to be slaughtered was no way to serve the Dragon. So
 he’d ordered a fighting withdrawal. That order had been the
 beginning of his troubles rather than the end. Somehow he
 had to hold his line while the bulk of his troops embarked
 their DropShips, leaving a rapidly shrinking force to fight a
 horribly dangerous delaying action.
    Olivares saw movement and looked down. A pair of troop-
 ers was moving left. His artillery spotters, brave souls who had
 crept up on the Steiner line to call in the deadly rain of shells.
    A Steiner Phoenix Hawk pushed through the trees. The
’Mech was slim and humanoid. Except for its immense size, it
 might’ve been a soldier in heavy armor.
    Olivares scarred the flat white paint on the Hawk’s chest
 with his lasers, melting the armor below. Two
 other gray and red Combine ’Mechs stepped
 forward to help, but the enemy ’Mech ignored
 them. It raised the laser clutched in its right
 hand and the spotters disappeared in a flash
 of ruby light. Then the Phoenix Hawk rose on its
 jump jets, fleeing its enemies.
    Olivares stepped his Dragon forward and released a flight
 of LRMs. The missiles caught the Hawk in the back. A plume
 of plasma stuttered out and the Phoenix Hawk tumbled out
 of the sky.
    Which didn’t solve Olivares’ main problem. Suddenly, his
 artillery had lost its eyes.
    For a moment he considered calling a light ’Mech forward
 to spot, and then thought better of it. At some point his artil-
 lery would have to withdraw anyway. “Artillery Actual, this is
 Legion Actual. Tiger Six Orange.”
   “Tiger Six Orange,” answered the artillery commander. “Hai,
 Olivares-sama.”
    And just like that, the heavy crump of the big guns
 fell silent.
    Olivares keyed the loadmaster’s frequency. “Sho-sa,
 report status.”
    Static flared briefly before Olivares heard Sho-sa Leonardo
 Nicosia’s deep voice. “At least an hour, Tai-sa.”
    Olivares’ heart sank. “Come again, Sho-sa.”
   “We require six zero minutes for evac.”
    Olivares closed his eyes. His forces couldn’t hold out that
 long. “Dammit, Nicosia, that ain’t gonna cut it.”
   “It requires time to load and secure equipment,” Nicosia
 said stiffly. Then, perhaps remembering who he was talking
 to: “The Snow Crane is fully loaded. I could order her to boost.”
   “Hell, no,” Olivares snapped. “I mean, iie.” He’d need the
 Leopard’s guns later. “Just hurry it up. Load the people first. If
 necessary we’ll spike the guns and leave them.”
   “Hai, Tai-sa.”
    He glanced toward the city and saw one of his Lightnings
 dropping like a stone. Suddenly the sleek fighter pulled out
 of its power dive and shot away fifty meters off the deck.
 Incandescent white fire rippled along the ground in the
 Lightning’s wake.
   The Elsie commander was trying to bring up his armor and               He sprayed laser fire into a pine tree. For a heartbeat the
artillery. Olivares’ aerospace forces were fighting a desperate        tree withstood the assault, and then it exploded in a shower

minutes with their daring attacks. But their efforts wouldn’t turn     branches and needles that littered the forest floor were dry
the battle. Olivares didn’t have many support forces either—his        and brittle.
were boarding DropShips.                                                  Ready to burn.
   Damn it, he needed those minutes.                                      The flames caught. Suddenly, a thousand fires bloomed
   As he watched, one of the Lightnings dove for the ground,           where there had only been one.
pursued by an Elsie Stuka. Coherent light sparked between                “All ’Mechs, this is Olivares. Withdraw west toward the lake.
them. The Lightning never pulled up. An immense orange fire-           Fire the forest as you go. Keep your backs to the water. No
ball rose on the horizon.                                              Elsies get past you.” He listened to his commanders acknowl-
  “Iie,” Olivares whispered fiercely. “I will not break.”              edge the order. Then he switched frequencies. “Armor Actual,
   The Lyrans were advancing through the evergreen forest              this is Legion Actual. We’re sending them to you one at a time.
that shadowed the lake’s shore line. Smart move. It prevented          Hurt them.”

slow the Lyrans down.                                                  from tree to tree and roaring with the fury of a thousand ’Mechs.
   Olivares keyed his tactical channel. “Armor, wheel about and           Olivares and Lukin came across Smith’s Warhammer trying

the forest.”                                                           with his autocannon to give the pilot something to think about
   A chorus of hais sounded over the tactical channel as his war-      while the world around him burned.
riors threw themselves into the teeth of the Lyran advance.               The Awesome broke and ran. Nothing terrified MechWarriors
   Olivares stalked his Dragon forward. A quick glance at              more than fire.
his schematic told him his artillery had cut the Lyran striker            Olivares tied into his armor’s tactical channel just to hear
company down to size. A limited victory. His warbook identi-           the whine of the tanks’ PPCs. His Manticores were picking off
fied a Victor, a Zeus, two Crusaders and a Grasshopper. The Elsie      the panicked Lyran ’Mechs one at a time. A glance at his rear
commander had brought up his assault battalion.                        monitor showed patches of blue through the burning branches.
   He saw a flash of white against dark green and charged              He turned, stepped out of the forest and crossed a short beach,
forward. The Quickdraw pilot didn’t see him until the last minute.     the mud giving under his Dragon’s 60-ton weight. Then he was
By then it was too late.                                               in the water, where seventeen of his ’Mechs waited for him.
   Olivares rushed forward at full speed, pulling up at the last          His cockpit temperature dropped five degrees in thirty
minute and clipping the Quickdraw in the leg. The limb wasn’t          seconds. The heat alarm fell silent.
designed to take a 60-ton ’Mech smashing into it at eighty                An Elsie Stinger came stumbling out of the forest and pulled
kph. It snapped like a dry twig. The Quickdraw tumbled to the          back as every Combine ’Mech targeted it at the same time.

Then he turned and ran in the direction the Quickdraw had              gies slicing into the little ’Mech. It fell forward into the lake.
been heading.                                                            “Legion Actual, this is Armor Actual, we are engaging a large

ing under the attack of an Elsie Marauder. For a second the              “On our way,” Olivares shouted. “’Mechs, one hundred meters

back was the friendly Quickdraw.                                          His pilots slogged through the lake, then cut across the plain

   A flicker of orange light at Olivares’ feet caught his attention.   Lyrans turned to face the new threat, while others continued

   Suddenly he had an idea.                                            hammer and anvil.
   They started to fall back, to regroup.                                 The hatch swung open. Sho-sa Leonardo Nicosia entered.
   Olivares had won a temporary advantage. He was still out-          “Gomen nasai, Tai-sa, but I—” The man’s eyes widened as he took
gunned and outmanned, and once the Lyrans formed up,                   in the scene. Shock warred with despair on his face, but then
they’d slaughter his troops, but he had the opening he’d been          he got control of himself. “Tai-sa, perhaps if you petition the
looking for.                                                           Coordinator—”
  “Eleventh Legion of Vega, all forces, this is Legion Actual. Fall      “Silence,” Olivares roared. “I left orders not to be disturbed.”
back to the DropShips. I say again, FALL BACK TO THE DROPSHIPS.”          Nicosia bowed his head. “Hai, Tai-sa. But we just received this.”
   It was a race. A running firefight all the way back to the LZ,      He handed Olivares a message.
until Olivares lumbered up over a shallow ridge and saw his               The Tai-sa took the paper without looking at it. “Get out.”
waiting DropShips below: a Leopard, two Unions, his Fortress-            “Sir, it’s a transmission from an ISF operative who managed to
class flagship and more descending to provide fire support.            get to the Sigmundrac HPG—”
   A Lyran Stalker followed him up over the ridge. The big guns          “Get out.”
on all four DropShips opened up. The Elsie ’Mech dissolved.               Nicosia blanched. “Hai, Tai-sa.” He bowed from the waist and
   At that moment, Olivares knew the Eleventh Legion of Vega           quickly departed, shutting the hatch softly behind him.
would survive the battle of Alrakis.                                      Olivares fingered the printout. Perhaps Nicosia was right. The
                                                                       Coordinator might not forgive an unauthorized retreat, but his
                                                                       son? Theodore Kurita was more pragmatic. Less bound by tra-
FORTRESS-CLASS DROPSHIP ASTRINGENT BEAUTY                              dition. And as Gunji-no-Kanrei, Deputy for Military Affairs, his
EN ROUTE TO NADIR JUMP POINT, ALRAKIS                                  influence within the Draconis Combine Mustered Soldiery was
DIERON MILITARY DISTRICT                                               considerable. Maybe...
DRACONIS COMBINE                                                          Iie.
24 APRIL 3039                                                             Olivares had served with Theodore, owed his command to
                                                                       Theodore. It was the Kanrei’s example that had caused him
   Tai-sa Esau Olivares centered the elegant rice paper on the         to fight so long and hard to regain his honor. He would not
desk and taped it down so it would not flit away during the            abandon it now.
many odd accelerations that came to a DropShip. The same                  He drew a deep breath. His gaze fell across the message.
message was recorded in his command log, but it might matter              A chill took him.
that he had composed the note in his own hand.                            The message was brief, a list of worlds: Alnasi, Kessel, Vega.
   It might matter a great deal.                                       Athenry, New Mendham, An Ting. It went on from there. Alrakis
   He lighted a candle, trusting the ship’s ventilation system         was not alone. The Federated Commonwealth had launched a
to filter out the trace of smoke. His white silk kimono shifted        massive assault along four separate axes, striking at the heart of
against his skin as he carefully arranged the ceremonial items:        the Draconis Combine.
the large white cushion on a tatami mat, a second piece of rice           Ten years before, during the Fourth Succession War,
paper resting on a black tray along with a brush and ink block, a      Hanse Davion had digested large chunks of the Capellan
lacquered cup, a flask of sake.                                        Confederation. Now it was the Combine’s turn.
   His wakizashi. The sword’s blade was carefully wrapped in rice         The message revealed the work of the yellow bird, the mythi-
paper and bound with a red cord.                                       cal creature capable of killing the Dragon.
   He knelt on the white cushion and drew a deep breath, com-             He let the paper fall from his hand. If the yellow bird truly
posing himself for what must come next.                                threatened the Combine, then she would need all her warriors
   This was not how seppuku was supposed to be. He should              to survive. Perhaps a small role remained for him to play.
have been attended by his officers and family. But he took on             Olivares rose to his feet, crossed the room and detached
the added burden of performing the ritual alone as a message           the piece of rice paper he’d taped to his desk. He touched it to
to the Coordinator, the same message written in delicate kanji         the candle flame, allowing the yellow fire to consume his final
calligraphy on the rice paper taped to the desk.                       words.
   I alone am responsible for the debacle on Alrakis.                     Perhaps he was wrong. Perhaps the Coordinator would still
   For centuries, no Combine commander could retreat off-              order him to commit seppuku. If that day came, he would face
world without the Coordinator’s explicit permission. The penal-        it like the samurai he was. But until it did, he would fight the
ties were harsh: death for the commander, his staff and a third        yellow bird with all his strength, all his will.
of the unit’s soldiers.                                                   And if he were wrong, the sword would still be there.
   And so Olivares had compounded his shame with a lie. In his            Hai.
note he had said he’d quieted his officers’ objections by telling         The sword would always be there.
them he had permission to withdraw. In truth, the subject had
never come up.
   But perhaps the lie would be enough to save his people.
   A small ding told him there was someone at his stateroom hatch.
  “Go,” Olivares snapped.

   After all players complete Movement Phase (Ground) and                                       GAME TERMS
Movement Phase (Aerospace) of a turn, units engage in combat.                                      Before reading this section, players should revisit the defini-
BattleTech units use two forms of combat: weapon attacks and                                    tions of the most important game terms in BattleTech, which will
physical attacks. Units make weapon attacks using armaments                                     provide a solid framework from which the rules in this section
such as missiles, lasers and autocannons. For physical attacks,                                 can build (see Game Terms, p. 42). Players should also familiarize
each unit has a variety of options. Most physical attacks rely on                               themselves with the record sheets of the various units (especially
the weight of the attacking unit to inflict damage.                                            ’Mechs), paying particular attention to the definitions of location,
   In BattleTech, weapon and physical attacks (as well as any other                             internal structure, slot and so on (see Record Sheets, p. 11).
type of damage, such as falling) first inflict damage on the outer
armor that protects every unit (except conventional infantry).
When an attack or series of attacks destroys all of a location’s
Armor Points, any remaining damage affects the unit’s internal                                         ATTACK DECLARATION
structure in that location (or eliminates the trooper, in the case of
battle armor). Anytime a unit’s internal structure takes damage, a                                As described in Playing the Game (see p. 36), all attacks are
critical hit may result that can knock out a weapon or movement                                declared before any are resolved. Only weapon attacks declared
system or even destroy the unit.                                                               during weapon attack declaration are resolved in the Weapon

                                                                                                                                                            99
Terrain Height and Depth
   A hex’s level is marked on the map. Hexes with
levels higher than 0 are also referred to as hills.
Hexes with levels lower than 0 are also referred to
as sinkholes. All affect LOS in the same way.
   If targeting a hex, the level of the adjacent hex
along the LOS between the attacker and target is
considered to be 1 level lower.
   Woods: Woods rise 2 levels above the level of
the underlying hex they occupy. Units occupy-
ing woods hexes are standing on the underlying
terrain, not on top of the trees (see VTOL Movement,
p. 54, for an exception to this rule).
   Buildings: Buildings rise above the level of
the underlying hex they occupy for a number of
levels equal to the level listed for the building. For
example, a Level 2 building on a Level 4 hex makes
Level 6 the total height of the hex.
   Water: Water hexes descend to a specific depth
below the surface; the surface of the water is actu-
ally at the same level as the surrounding terrain.
The depth represents the bottom of the body of
water. This means that water intervenes for LOS
purposes as if it is at the level of the surrounding
terrain, while a ’Mech in the water stands on the
bottom, at the depth level of the hex, and rises 2
levels above that depth.

INTERVENING TERRAIN
   Terrain along the LOS between the attacker and the target
that actually lies within the LOS (not including the hexes
occupied by the attacker and target) has the potential to
be intervening terrain. The terrain of these hexes may or
may not intervene in LOS, depending on the terrain’s level
relative to the attacker and target. Likewise, features of the
terrain in the hexes along the LOS (buildings, water, woods
and so on) may or may not intervene in LOS, depending on
their level relative to the attacker and target. Only terrain
features that have levels, such as trees and buildings, can
intervene in LOS. For example, rubble would not intervene,
though the hex containing the rubble might (depending on
its underlying level).
   Terrain along the LOS between two units intervenes if:

•    The level of the terrain or feature is equal to or higher
     than the level of both units; or
•    The terrain or feature is adjacent to the attacker and
     equal to or higher than the attacker’s level; or
•    The terrain or feature is adjacent to the target and equal to
     or higher than the target’s level.                                                  • line of sight diagram •
                                                                        Light Woods: Three or more hexes of intervening light woods
Effects of Intervening Terrain
                                                                     block LOS. One hex of intervening light woods combined with
  Intervening terrain has the following effects on LOS.
                                                                     one or more hexes of intervening heavy woods also block LOS.
  Buildings and Bridges: Intervening building hexes block LOS;
                                                                        Water: Intervening water blocks LOS unless attacker and target
bridges, however, do not.
                                                                     are completely submerged and no other terrain intervenes. See
  Heavy Woods: Two or more hexes of intervening heavy woods
                                                                     also Water Hexes, p. 102; Terrain Modifiers, p. 107; and Partial Cover,
block LOS. One hex of intervening heavy woods combined with
                                                                     p. 102 (see Torpedo Launcher, p. 138, and Multi-Purpose Missiles,
one or more hexes of intervening light woods will also block LOS.
                                                                     p. 229, for the exceptions).
  Hills: Intervening hills block LOS.
                                                                        Other Units: Except for grounded DropShips, intervening units
                                                                     have no effect on LOS or attacks; grounded DropShips block LOS.

Dumping Ammunition                                                        merged. If a unit submerges during the turn it is dumping
  During a game, a player might wish to dump the ammunition               ammunition, all three torso locations automatically flood (see
carried by his ’Mech or vehicle. He accomplishes this by opening          Underwater Units, p. 121).
the ammo loading doors on the back of the ’Mech or rear of the

  During the End Phase of a turn, a player can announce that his
unit will dump ammunition in the next turn. The unit may dump                               FIRING ARCS
any or all of the ammunition it carries, but ammunition must be
dumped by slot (or by ton on vehicles; .5 tons if it is the only MG           If an attacking unit has LOS to its intended target, the attack-
ammo on the vehicle); if any ammo in a slot is dumped, all of it          ing player can then check the firing arcs of his unit’s weapons to
must be dumped (for vehicles, the entire ton must be dumped).             see which weapons can hit the target. There are four firing arcs as
Dumping is carried out during the following turn.                         shown on the Firing Arcs Diagram: the forward arc (in yellow), left
                                                                          side arc (in blue), right side arc (also in blue), and rear arc (in red).
                                                                             The firing arcs extend from the firing unit to the edge of the
                                                                          playing area. The maximum ranges for weapons are described in
LSLS
                                                                          the Weapons and Equipment Tables, beginning on page 303.
                                                                              Infantry: Infantry do not have firing arc restrictions.
                                                                              ProtoMechs: ProtoMechs use the firing arcs for ’Mechs and can
                                                                          twist their torsos according to the standard rules (see Rotating the
                                                                          Firing Arc, p. 105). If the ProtoMech is carrying a main gun, that
                                                                          weapon can be fired at targets in the forward, right side and
                                                                          left side firing arcs. The weapon is rotated if the torso is twisted,
                                                                          giving it a potential 360-degree field of fire.
                                                                              Split-Location Weapons: A weapon on a ’Mech whose critical
                                                                          slots are split between a torso and arm location uses the torso
                                                                          firing arc.
                                                                              Vehicles: Vehicles use the firing arcs for ‘Mechs. If a vehicle has
                                                                          a turret, weapons in it fire into the forward arc, but this firing arc
                                                                          can be rotated as described in Rotating the Firing Arcs, page 105.
                                                                             Aerospace Units: Aerodyne units have four arcs: nose, left,
                                                                          right and rear (see Aerodyne Firing Arcs, p. 236.)
                                                                              Spheroid units have six arcs: nose, front left, front right, aft left,
                                                                          aft right and aft (see Spheroid Firing Arcs, p. 235).
                                                                              Grounded aerospace units have specific rules for firing arcs (see
                                                                          Attacks By Grounded Aerospace Units, p. 249).
           TSH-7S Tai-sho, Twenty-second Dieron Regulars (House Kurita)
                                                                        Leg-Mounted Weapons: Leg-mounted weapons may not
                                                                     fire through a hex that provides the firing ’Mech with partial
                                                                     cover. The firing arc is always “forward” for forward-mounted
                                                                     leg weapons, and is not affected by torso twisting.                         INTRODUCTION
                                                                        Vehicles: Weapons mounted on the front location of a
                                                                     vehicle may only fire into the front arc. Weapons mounted in
                                                                     the turret can also fire into the forward arc, though this firing           COMPONENTS
                                                                     arc can be rotated as described in Rotating the Firing Arcs, below.
                                                                        Prone ’Mechs: The standard arcs for attacker and target do
                                                                     not change if either one is a prone ’Mech.                                     PLAYING
                                                                                                                                                   THE GAME
                                                                     LEFT SIDE ARC
                                                                        Weapons mounted in a ’Mech’s left arm may fire at targets
                                                                                                                                                   GROUND
                                                                     in the left side arc and forward arc.                                        MOVEMENT
                                                                        Vehicles: Weapons mounted on the left side of vehicles
                                                                     may only fire at targets in the left side arc.

                                                                                                                                                  MOVEMENT
                                                                     RIGHT SIDE ARC
                                                                        Weapons mounted in a ’Mech’s right arm may fire at targets
                                                                     in the right side arc and forward arc.                                       COMBAT
                                                                        Vehicles: Weapons mounted on the right side of vehicles
                                                                     may only fire at targets in the right side arc.

REVERSING (FLIPPING) ARMS                                                    Each weapon may be fired only once per turn, including
   ’Mechs constructed without hand and lower arm actuators in             weapons that can fire more than one shot in a single turn (see
both arms and which do not have any weapons split between                 Rapid-Fire Weapon, p. 114).
torso and arm locations can flip their arms over and fire backward.          A unit cannot make a weapon attack against another unit
This ability only applies to ’Mechs originally constructed without        occupying the same hex. See Point-Blank Weapon, page 114, and
those actuators (or OmniMechs that remove their hand and                  Infantry, page 212, for exceptions.
lower-arm actuators for a certain configuration), not ’Mechs that            As defined under Game Terms (see p. 42), a target can be
lose them due to critical damage.                                         another unit, or a clear hex, or almost anything in between.
    A ’Mech that intends to reverse its arm-mounted weapons must          Players can fire weapons at almost anything, even if the result will
flip both arms during weapon attack declaration. This maneuver            not inflict any damage. For example, a player can shoot at a hex
takes the place of a torso twist (a ’Mech cannot torso twist and          to heat up his ’Mech so that it can use triple-strength myomer
reverse its arms simultaneously). After flipping its arms, the ’Mech      (see p. 143), or a player may simply wish to expend ammunition.
may then fire any arm-mounted weapon into the rear firing arc             Regardless of why or what target is chosen, if a weapon is fired,
instead of the usual firing arcs for those weapons.                       all its effects must be taken into account (ammunition must be
    If a ’Mech that can reverse its arms loses one during a game,         expended, heat tracked, specific weapon effects dealt with and
it can still reverse the remaining arm. During the End Phase,             so on, as appropriate for each weapon).
reversed arms automatically return to the standard front arc.
    Prone ’Mechs and ProtoMechs: Neither of these units may               BASE TO-HIT NUMBER (GATOR)
reverse their arms.                                                          The base to-hit number for a weapon attack is equal to the
                                                                          firing unit’s Gunnery Skill Rating (see Skills, p. 39).

                                                                          MODIFIED TO-HIT NUMBER
           FIRING WEAPONS                                                   The modified to-hit number equals the base to-hit number
                                                                          plus all applicable modifiers for range, minimum range, move-
   After a player has determined that a target lies within LOS and        ment, terrain and other factors discussed in To-Hit Modifiers.
has determined the firing arc of his weapons, the unit may make a            If the modified to-hit number is greater than 12, the shot auto-
weapon attack. The player counts the range in hexes to the target         matically misses. If a player determines that his unit’s declared
to find the base to-hit number for the attack. For each weapon he         attack will automatically miss, he can choose not to make the
fires, the player determines if the shot is more or less difficult than   attack, thereby avoiding a waste of ammunition and some heat
                                                                          build-up. He may not switch his attack to another target.
                                                                             If the modified to-hit number is 2 or less, the shot automatically
                      G.A.T.O.R.                                          hits. Because some weapons have additional effects based on the
                                                                          to-hit roll result, even automatic hits may require the player to
                                                                          make a to-hit roll (see Weapons and Equipment, p. 113).
     The attack process is not complicated—simply a matter
  of rolling 2D6 and trying to equal or exceed your modified
  Target Number. However, it’s the “modified” part that can               TO-HIT MODIFIERS
  trip a player up: juggling the many potential modifiers can               The base to-hit number may be modified by several factors,
  be imposing.                                                            including range, terrain, movement, multiple targets, heat
     After you understand the Target Number Modifiers section             and damage, and prone or immobile targets. All modifiers are
  below, you’ll find that “GATOR” is a helpful mnemonic that              cumulative.
  reminds you what needs to be taken into account:
  G Gunnery Skill Rating of the attacker (the base Target                 Attacker Movement (GATOR)
    Number for the attack). Then add:                                        A moving attacker must constantly adjust his aim to compen-
  A Attacker movement modifier                                            sate for his movement, so an attacking unit’s to-hit number is
  T Target movement modifier                                              modified by its own movement using the values in the Attack
  O Other modifiers (potentially a big category, but typically            Modifiers Table. The attacker movement modifier is based on the
    only includes woods, partial cover, and heat)
                                                                          movement mode the attacking unit used in the turn, regardless
  R Range modifiers
                                                                          of the actual MP expended or distance moved.
    You start with your Gunnery Skill Rating, then add all other             Infantry: Attacker movement modifiers do not apply to infan-
  applicable modifiers as indicated, and the result is your modified      try units when they attack.
  Target Number to roll against. In the Target Number Modifiers
  section that follows, modifiers are listed in GATOR order, begin-
  ning with the Attacker Movement Modifier.
Target Movement (GATOR)
   A moving target is harder to hit, and so
an attacking unit’s to-hit number is modi-
fied by its target’s movement using the                                                                                                   INTRODUCTION
values in the Attack Modifiers Table. The
target movement modifiers are based
on the hexes traversed rather than the                                                                                                    COMPONENTS
number of Movement Points spent. If the
target moved backward and forward in
the turn (regardless of facing changes),                                                                                                     PLAYING
base the movement modifier on the                                                                                                           THE GAME
number of hexes moved from the hex
in which the unit last reversed its move-
                                                                                                                                            GROUND
ment. For example, if the target moved                                                                                                     MOVEMENT
backward three hexes and then forward
two hexes, the target movement modi-
fier would be based only on the final two                                                                                                  AEROSPACE
hexes of movement, resulting in a Target                                                                                                   MOVEMENT
Movement Modifier of 0. If the target
jumped in the current turn, the player
                                                                                                                                           COMBAT
must add a jump modifier in addition to
the modifier for the number of hexes moved.

and target (including the target’s hex) is the range.

tions, Hyper-Assault Gauss Rifles, or Rapid-Fire weapons firing

   Non-aerospace units firing at airborne aerospace units cannot fire
at other non-aerospace units (including grounded aerospace units) in
the same turn. Also, a non-aerospace unit cannot attack more than one
airborne aerospace unit in a turn. See Attacks by Grounded Aerospace                                                                    INTRODUCTION
Units, page 249, for the exception.
   For an extensive example of non-aerospace units attacking an air-
borne aerospace unit, see page 247 in the Aerospace Units section.

example) firing at airborne aerospace units use all the standard rules for

ground units.                                                                                                                             THE GAME

unless specifically stated otherwise by the rules for that unit, infantry
cannot attack airborne aerospace units.                                                                                                   GROUND
