Firing at Buildings
  Treat all weapon attacks against building hexes as though firing                                                                       AEROSPACE
at an immobile target, applying the standard –4 to-hit modifier (see                                                                     MOVEMENT
Attacking Buildings, p. 171).

LRM Indirect Fire                                                                                                                        COMBAT
   Units armed with LRM-type weapons may fire those missiles indi-
rectly. Indirect fire allows a unit without a direct line of sight to a target
to attack that target, though a friendly ground unit must have a valid
line of sight to the target (this unit is referred to as the spotter). In order                                                             HEAT
to serve as a spotter, the unit must not have charged or launched a
Death From Above attack that turn. An attacker with a valid LOS to
a target cannot make an LRM indirect fire attack, even if that attack                                                                    BUILDINGS
would have a better to-hit modifier.
   Resolve LRM indirect fire attacks in the turn they are launched. The
base to-hit number is the firing unit’s Gunnery Skill. Use the following
                                                                                                                                        PROTOMECHS
modifiers:

•    Range modifier based on the range between the target and the
                                                                                                                                          COMBAT
     firing unit, including minimum range modifiers;
                                                                                                                                          VEHICLES
•    +1 for indirect fire;
•    All standard modifiers for target movement;
•    All standard modifiers for attacker movement and a modifier for                                                                      SUPPORT
     the spotter’s movement (infantry have no attacker movement                                                                           VEHICLES
     modifier for spotting);
•    Terrain modifiers based on line of sight from the spotting unit;
     this includes the +1 modifier if partial cover exists between the                                                                    INFANTRY
     spotting unit and the target. If no spotting unit is required (due to
     an attached Narc beacon, for example), no terrain modifiers apply.
     (Regardless of whether partial cover shields the target from either                                                                 AEROSPACE

     type. OS type weapons do not explode due to critical hits or             •   2 ER Micro Lasers

•    PB: Point-Blank Weapon. Point-blank weapons can only be                  •   LB 5-X AC
     used against targets in the same or adjacent hexes.                      •   Streak SRM-2
•    E: Electronics. ECM systems may jam electronic equipment                 •   SRM-4 with Artemis IV FCS
     (see ECM Suite, p. 134). Any Type E item may be turned either            •   ATM-6
     on or off in the End Phase of a turn.                                    •   LRM-15 with Artemis IV FCS

as Damage Value, which appears under the Damage Value                of the game (see ’Mech Critical Hit Effects, p. 125). The corre-

Table, and can hit multiple locations. Each cluster is resolved                                                                                                        UNITS
as its own separate hit.
   Underwater Units: Whenever an underwater unit takes a
hit that inflicts damage, the controlling player rolls 2D6. On a                                                                                                      CREATING
result of 10 or greater, the unit’s hull has been breached. The                                                                                                      SCENARIOS
unit has lost integrity in that location, and it fills with water.
Only make this roll for flooding at the moment an underwater                                                                                                          PAINTING
unit takes damage, not for a damaged unit that later enters                                                                                                          MINIATURES
the water. If all of a location’s armor is destroyed (whether this
occurred before the unit entered the water hex or while it is
submerged), that location is automatically breached.                                                                                                                   INDEX
  Treat all of a unit’s components in a breached location as
nonfunctional. None of that location’s actuators, weapons
or other equipment works. If the breached location contains
engine slots, the engine functions as if it took as many critical

hits as engine critical slots in that location; if the breached

ers for other damage; the +5 modifier for leg destruction replaces           No: Proceed to Step 6.

ing the MechWarrior during the fall. Regardless of how or in what           4. At the beginning of the current phase, did the location

damage. It may only make one attempt per turn. If the ’Mech                  No: Roll once on the Determining Critical Hits Table. Any critical

is considered a run.                                                      destroyed; any armor remaining at that location (whether front

per turn; it is considered to have walked if it changes its facing.       this fashion does not cause ammunition or other components

   Four-Legged ’Mechs: The effects of loss of legs are applied to         6. Is there damage remaining?
a quad ’Mech as follows:                                                     Yes: Go to Step 7 to allocate remaining damage.
• One destroyed leg: Immediately falls and loses all move-                   No: Attack is finished.
       ment and combat bonuses gained from being four-legged:
       it can no longer make lateral shifts, it loses its –2 modifier     7. Did the damage result from an ammunition (or other
       to Piloting Skill Rolls, it must make a successful PSR to stand    internal component) explosion?
       after falling, and it suffers a +2 modifier to Target Numbers         Yes: Go to Step 8.
       for firing when prone. In addition, a –1 MP penalty replaces          No: Damage transfers to the armor of the next location inward
       any penalties associated with damaged leg actuators in             (see Damage Transfer Diagram). Go to Step 1.
       the destroyed leg. If the ’Mech jumps, it still must make the
       usual PSR required for jumping and missing a leg, with a +5
       modifier.
 • Two destroyed legs: Functions with the same restrictions
       described above for a biped ’Mech that has lost one leg: it
       immediately falls, has only 1 MP, and so on.
 • Three destroyed legs: Functions with the same restrictions
       described above for a biped ’Mech that has lost both legs: it
       immediately falls, has only 0 MP, and so on.
 • Four destroyed legs: Automatically falls (if not already
       prone) and is immobile (see p. 110).

DAMAGE RESOLUTION
  To apply damage from an attack, begin with the amount of
damage the attack inflicts and its hit location, and start at Step 1.
Answer each question yes or no, and follow the instructions.

1. Does the location have armor?
   Yes: Check off one armor circle on the Armor Diagram in the
appropriate location for every point of damage taken, until all
damage is applied or all armor in the location is destroyed. Go
to Step 2.
   No: Proceed to Step 3.

2. Is there damage remaining?                                             SLLS
   Yes: Go to Step 3 to allocate remaining damage.
   No: Attack is finished.                                                                   NJT-2 Ninja-To, Ryuken-yon (House Kurita)
                                                                      is automatically transferred to the appropriate torso (which is
                                                                      destroyed, so the damage ultimately transfers to the center torso).

                                                                              A Grasshopper’s left arm is hit by an attack from a PPC         INTRODUCTION
                                                                           (Damage Value 10), a large laser (Damage Value 8) and
                                                                           two 5-point groups of long-range missiles (Damage Value
                                                                           1 per missile hit, for 5 points per Damage Value grouping).
                                                                                                                                              COMPONENTS
                                                                           Before this turn, the BattleMech had its full Armor Value of
                                                                           22 in that arm.
                                                                              The PPC hit reduces the Armor Value by 10, so the
                                                                                                                                                 PLAYING
                                                                           Grasshopper’s player fills in ten armor circles. The laser           THE GAME
                                                                           hit does 8 points of damage, and so the player fills in
                                                                           eight more circles, leaving four. The first cluster of missiles
                                                                           reduces the Armor Value by another 5 points, but since the           GROUND
                                                                           Grasshopper’s remaining Armor Value is 4, that leaves 1             MOVEMENT
                                                                           point of damage that the hit location cannot absorb.
                                                                              The remaining 1 point of damage from the first cluster           AEROSPACE
                                                                           of missiles transfers to the ’Mech’s internal structure, and        MOVEMENT
                                                                           so the player fills in one circle on the Internal Structure
                                                                           Diagram, leaving ten circles out of the original eleven. The
                                                                           last group of missiles reduces the internal structure by            COMBAT
                                                                           another 5 points. The player fills in five more circles on the
                                                                           Internal Structure Diagram, leaving five. (The attacking
                                                                           player would make two rolls on the Determining Critical
                                                                                                                                                  HEAT
                                                                           Hits table, p. 124, because two different hits damaged the
                                                                           internal structure of the left arm.)
                                                                              If the Grasshopper’s left arm takes a hit from a
                                                                           weapon that inflicts 5 or more points of damage, the arm            BUILDINGS
                                                                           will be destroyed.

has a detrimental impact on the ’Mech’s operator because of radia-
tion leakage. The first critical hit to a fission engine causes the pilot   Head Blown Off (Head)
to suffer one hit every six turns (beginning six turns after the turn         A hit blows off a ’Mech’s head when the player rolls a 12 on
in which the original damage was caused). A second engine hit               the Determining Critical Hits Table for the head hit location. This
decreases this damage interval to one pilot hit every three turns.

Foot Actuator (Leg)
   This critical hit destroys the muscle (actuator) in the foot. The
controlling player must make a Piloting Skill Roll at the end of
the phase in which the critical hit occurred, with a +1 modifier
(in addition to any other standard modifiers); see Making Piloting/
Driving Skill Rolls, page 59.
   For each foot actuator damaged, reduce the ’Mech’s Walking
MP by 1 and add a +1 modifier to any subsequent Piloting Skill
Roll. (The player must refigure the Running MP as well; multiply
the new Walking MP by 1.5, rounding up.) A Piloting Skill Roll is
required whenever the ’Mech jumps; the roll is made at the end
of the ’Mech’s movement. For each foot actuator critical, apply a
+1 to-hit modifier to all kick attacks.
   These effects are cumulative with other leg and foot actuator                                                                                  DFDF/MD
damage.

and puts the ’Mech out of commission for the rest of the game.        any explosive components in that section do not explode.

 3

  The C3 computer system can link up to twelve ’Mechs or
vehicles together—utilizing a series of C 3 Master and C3                                                                                                        PAINTING
Slaves—in a communications network that will share target-                                                                                                      MINIATURES
ing information.
  To make an attack using a C3 computer network, calculate
                                                                                                                                                                  INDEX
the to-hit number using the range to the target from the net-
worked unit nearest the target. Use the firing unit’s modifiers
for movement, terrain effects, minimum range and so on. A
weapon attack using a C3 network must conform to standard
LOS restrictions and cannot fire beyond its maximum range,

                                                                            Forces from the Word of Blake’s First Division surround a Steel Viper Goshawk 3.


                                                                                                                                                                  131
                          • c3 diagram •

       In the C3 Diagram, the ’Mech in Hex A is facing enemy units
    in Hexes B, C, D and E, which are connected to a C3 network.
    The VTOL in Hex B is closest to the enemy, at a Range of 2; even
    though it is at Elevation 10, elevations are never taken into
    consideration when determining range to target. The ’Mech in
    Hex C can attack as though it were at a Range of 2, provided
    the weapons it fires have a Maximum Range of 4 or more. The
    Combat Vehicle in Hex D can also fire as though at a Range of
    2, but must add the terrain modifier for firing through the light
    woods in Hex F. The ’Mech in Hex E can’t attack the ’Mech in                       • c3 configuration diagram •
    Hex A because no line of sight exists between the two units.
    In all cases, if the ’Mechs in Hex C, D or E fired weapons with
    minimum ranges, those ranges must be taken into consid-             Master connects three C3 Masters together to form a company-
    eration, with the range determined from the attacker to the         level (12 unit) C3 network; as noted above, the C3 network can
    target, not the range from the VTOL in Hex B to the target.         never be extended beyond 12 units.

   In Configuration 2, everything remains the same, but
instead of a separate unit carrying the C3 Master that links
                                                                                                                                              COMBAT
the three lances, a single unit carries two C3 Masters; in the
third lance, the C3 Master can control three C3 Slaves now, as
opposed to the two C3 Slaves it controlled in Configuration 1,
maintaining a twelve unit C3 network.                                                                                                            HEAT
   In Configuration 3, three of the C3 Masters are carried by a
single unit, with a single C3 Master carried by a separate unit.
As a C3 Master cannot control more than three C3 Masters or                                                                                   BUILDINGS
three C3 Slaves, the network is down to only eleven units.
   Finally, in Configuration 4, all four of the C3 Masters are
carried by a single unit. As with Configuration 3, since a C3
                                                                                                                                             PROTOMECHS
Master can only control three C3 Masters or three C3 Slaves, the
network is down to only ten units.                                                           • c i diagram •

                                                                         Water
         PHYSICAL ATTACKS                                                   The following rules apply to physical attacks between units
                                                                         that occupy water hexes.
   ’Mechs can make seven different types of physical attacks:               Any successful physical attack that occurs underwater inflicts
punching, clubbing, pushing, kicking, charging, death from               half its standard damage (round down), including a ramming
above or physical weapon attacks (this last attack requires that         attack by a surface naval vessel. A physical attack against a
the ’Mech mount a specific physical attack weapon, such as a             surface vessel is only considered underwater if the attack origi-
hatchet, sword and so on).                                               nated underwater, so a ramming attack by a surface vessel would
    In order to make a physical attack, the unit must be adjacent        not be considered underwater.
to its target and the target must be within the attacking ’Mech’s           Depth 1 Water: A ’Mech standing in Depth 1 water may make
forward firing arc (see Punch Attacks, Charge Attacks and Death          any physical attack, provided the unit meets the normal require-
from Above Attacks for exceptions).                                      ments for each type of attack. However, the physical attack cannot
    The rules for physical attacks assume that attacker and target       be made against a unit that is underwater, unless the attack begins
are ’Mechs standing at the same level. Special rules regarding           underwater as well. For example, a ’Mech standing in Depth 1
other types of units, different levels and prone ’Mechs appear           water adjacent to a submerged submarine in Depth 1 water can
starting on page 150.                                                    only make a kick attack, since the kick attack occurs completely
    Aerospace Units: Aerospace units cannot make physical                underwater. The ´Mech cannot make any other attack, including a
attacks. They have their own special physical attack akin to charg-      charge or death from above attack, against the adjacent submarine
ing, called ramming (see p. 241), but it only applies to aerospace       because a portion of the attack would take place outside the water.
units ramming other aerospace units. Aerospace units cannot                 Depth 2+ Water: A ’Mech standing in Depth 2 (or deeper) water
intentionally ram a ground unit.                                         can make any of the following physical attacks, provided the unit
    IndustrialMechs: During the End Phase of any turn in which           meets the normal requirements for each type of attack: charging,
an IndustrialMech is the target of a successful physical attack,         clubbing, kicking, punching, pushing or a physical weapon attack.
roll 2D6 and consult the Determining Critical Hits Table (p. 124).
To determine the location of any resulting critical hits, roll once
on the Front/Rear column of the ’Mech Hit Location Table (see
                                                                            PHYSICAL ATTACK MODIFIERS TABLE
p. 119). The controlling player makes only one roll per turn,
regardless of the number of successful physical attacks the                              Attack Type                                         Modifier
IndustrialMech suffers.                                                                   Charging                                              +0*

have their own unique attacks (see Anti-’Mech Attack, p. 220).                            Clubbing                                               –1
    ProtoMechs: ProtoMechs cannot make physical attacks; they                Death From Above (DFA)                                            +0*†
can, however, make a single “frenzy” attack (see p. 187).                                  Kicking                                               –2
    Vehicles: The only physical attack a vehicle can make is a

Vehicles) and grounded DropShips receive a ­–2 to-hit modifier.                the difference between the two skill ratings as a modifier to the to-hit number. If the tar-
                                                                               get’s skill rating is lower, add the modifier to the to-hit number. If the attacker’s Piloting
    Initiative and Displacement: If one unit’s charge, push or DFA             Skill Rating is lower, subtract the modifier from the to-hit number.
attack would displace the target of another unit’s physical attack,        †All the normal attack modifiers apply, including the attacker’s jumping movement, but the
the unit with the lower Initiative resolves its physical attack first.         roll is not modified for terrain. For DFA attacks against infantry targets, apply an additional
If both attacking units are on the same team, the controlling                  +3 to-hit modifier (because infantry units have no Piloting Skill, neither player needs to

player may determine which attack is resolved first. If the target
of an attack is subsequently displaced before the attack can be

target of the attack, apply an additional +1 modifier and resolve          The base to-hit number for a physical attack is equal to the
the attack against the new target normally. If no valid target           attacking unit’s Piloting Skill Rating (see Skills, p. 39).
exists, the attack automatically fails.
    Multiple Physical Attacks: A ‘Mech may only make a single            MODIFIED TO-HIT NUMBER
type of physical attack in a single turn: charging, clubbing, death        The modified to-hit number equals the base to-hit number
from above, kick, punch, push or physical weapon attack. Even            plus the modifier for the specific physical attack as noted on the
if a ’Mech mounts two physical weapons (identical or otherwise),         Physical Attack Modifiers Table. All other standard modifiers for

a single punch attack, a player can punch with one or two arms           target movement modifier, damage to actuators, terrain and
(see Punch Attacks, p. 145).                                             so on, unless specifically stated otherwise by the rules for each

for Charge, Ram, Death From Above, and similar attacks, a unit           which never apply.
that begins the turn shutdown or unconscious should be treated              Physical attacks are not Piloting Skill Rolls, and as such, Piloting
as though it automatically took its movement action first.               Skill Roll modifiers do not affect physical attacks.

   The attacker takes damage as if from a successful attack on its legs.                     same level. Most physical attacks against vehicles occur only if the

LOCATION AFTER ATTACK                                                                        of physical attacks can be made in various situations. Players
   At the end of a death from above attack, the attacker lands in                            must use different Hit Location tables to determine the location
the target’s hex. If the DFA is successful, the target is pushed one                         of damage from punching, clubbing, physical weapon or kicking
hex in the direction opposite the attack. If the attack fails, the                           attacks against an opponent on various levels.
target chooses an adjacent hex and moves to it, even if immo-                                   ’Mechs cannot make punch attacks against ground vehicles or
bile or prone. This motion might result in an accidental fall from                           infantry unless the ’Mech is one level lower than normal because
above or a domino effect or even a Piloting Skill Roll for entering                          it is prone, on lower terrain or standing in Depth 1 water.
a building hex; see Unit Displacement, page 151.
   If the target unit cannot be displaced into the appropriate hex
(for example, if the terrain is prohibited), the hex chosen must be
as close as possible to the original hexside through which the
target unit would have moved. For example, if the direction of the
attack would have pushed the ’Mech through Hexside A, the con-
trolling player then looks at hexsides B or F to see if those hexes
are passable, then at hexsides C or E, then finally at hexside D. If
two equally distant hexes, such as B or F, are open, the appropri-
ate player may choose either one, as described above.
   If all the surrounding hexes contain impassable terrain, the
target unit cannot be displaced. For example, the target ’Mech
may be on Level 0 terrain surrounded by Level 3 or higher hills.                              WBWB
In this case, if the attack succeeds, the target is destroyed. If the
attack fails, the attacker is destroyed.                                                       Captain Ian McKinnon prepares to hatchet a Thunder Hawk from the Thirty-second Lyran Guard.

                                                                                   Table (or a randomly determined Hit Location Table for other

    If a unit accidentally falls one level or less or is forced into a
 hex occupied by another unit that violates the stacking rules
 (such as a ’Mech entering a hex that contains another ’Mech,
 or friendly vehicles and so on), a unit already in the hex is                                                                                                      INTRODUCTION
 forced out of it in the direction opposite the hexside of where
 the accidentally falling unit entered the hex. If either unit is a
’Mech, both players must immediately make Piloting Skill Rolls                                                                                                      COMPONENTS
 to avoid falling once all displacement is resolved.
    When the domino effect originates from one of a unit’s
 four side hexes, however, the unit can choose to avoid the                                                                                                            PLAYING
 domino effect by moving one hex directly forward or back-                                                                                                            THE GAME
 ward, assuming it has sufficient Ground MP remaining from
 the Movement Phase (meaning it cannot have jumped), and it
                                                                                                                                                                      GROUND
 is mobile (standing, if a ’Mech). For a vehicle, the player must                                                                                                    MOVEMENT
 make a successful Driving Skill Roll for the dodging unit; if the
 dodging unit is a ’Mech, the player need only have passed
 the Piloting Skill Roll given above. The unit attempting to                                                                                                         AEROSPACE
 dodge cannot move backward, however, if its player declared                                                                                                         MOVEMENT
 running/flanking movement for the turn. If the Piloting/
 Driving Skill Roll was not successful, the unit does not move
                                                                                                                                                                     COMBAT
 out of the way.

Broken Lands: Feuerberg.                                               basalt lava crack and give under the sixty-five ton weight of his

   The cinder cone rose out of a fractured landscape the color of        This was a dangerous course. Step wrong and he would crash
night. None could doubt that it ruled here. The volcano filled the    through the brittle surface to one of the hollow lava tubes that
air with the stink of sulfur, lent the land the heat that pulsed in   riddled the Broken Lands. But he would never find Watanabe’s
its angry heart.                                                      lance if he kept to the safety of the road.
   Mark had never fought in a worse place.                               Sometimes the most lucrative trades required the most risk.
   Aside from clouding his sensors, the heat sapped agility from         He made his way toward a ridge, labored up its side.
his ’Mech. The brittle landscape made it nearly impossible to            And saw a lone Hunchback.
maneuver beyond the single ferrocrete road that curled through           It stood four hundred meters out, painted dark blue with gold
this accursed place.                                                  and red trim. The colors of RDF 4.
   He pounded the useless display. Where were the stravags               Mark pulled his reticle down over the Hunchback’s left knee. The
hiding? He could abandon passive sensors, initiate a radar sweep.     icon flashed gold and he let go with a flight of Short Bow LRMs.
But that would reveal his own position.                                  Realizing he was being hunted, the Hunchback’s pilot turned
   Neg.                                                               as the missiles hit. Multiple explosions wrenched the knee joint
   He was a Diamond Shark, a predator of the dark seas of Strana      just as the pilot put his weight on it. The Hunchback toppled over.
Mechty. He would strike without warning.                                 Mark drew back.
   He could have chosen to use exercise rounds for this
demonstration, but he wanted to bloody the Hansa, prove
the worth of the Ha Otoko.
   One down, three to go.
   He crossed the road and headed east, Star Captain
Borghev trailing behind him. She held back far enough to
keep her radar signature distinct from his. She was not to
take part in the live fire exercise. Four to one, those were the
odds Mark had agreed to with Major Hohiro Watanabe.
   He stumbled as the ground shook. A harmonic tremor, a
sign of the coming eruption. A careful study of the seismic
traces would give an approximate idea of when it was
coming. Mark hoped it wouldn’t come soon.
   He pointed his ’Mech’s right arm at a distant hill. “That
will give me a commanding view of the landscape.” He
started forward, Anna behind him. Brittle rock crunched
under their feet.
   The land itself was a mixture of volcanic forms: meter-wide
blisters where lava had hardened around upwellings of gas,
tree molds where molten rock had taken on the twisted
shape of the trees it had killed, fumaroles where the earth
belched poisonous hydrogen sulfide.
   None dared live in the shadow of this great beast. The
closest village—Kepten, a hamlet so small it did not even
appear on local maps—lay a good twenty klicks away. Mark
glanced up. A plume of black ash marked Riga’s gunmetal
sky. He wondered if twenty klicks was far enough.
  “I see nothing on my scanners, saFactor,” Anna reported. “A
Hansa trick, quiaff.”
  “Neg,” answered Mark, “A Hansa negotiation tactic. It is
important to know the difference.”
   Anna was a fine warrior, which was why Mark had chosen
her to escort him on this venture, but she had much to learn
about the art of trading. She made a small hmm sound
that was neither challenge nor agreement, inviting him
to read into it whatever he wished.
   Mark smiled. Perhaps she had potential after all.
  “We are here to show them the value of the Ha Otoko,”
he said. “This is why I agreed to four to one odds.
When my single machine defeats Watanabe’s lance,
the Hansa will be forced to acknowledge the ’Mech’s
value. But our ‘Bladed Man’ is a missile carrier. If it cannot
see, it cannot fight. The Hansa force us to battle in this
wasteland, thinking to gain an advantage and therefore
a lower price.”
  “Perhaps, saFactor. But I do not trust the Hansa.”
  “And I do? Why do you think you are here, Star Captain?”
  “How do we know they will not just take what they want?”
  “All trade relationships are built on trust.”
  “Meaning . . . ?”
   Mark laughed. “Meaning that the other side
understands the cost of cheating you well enough
that they would never try it.”
  “I still would feel better if Razor Tooth were here.”
   After much pressing the Hansa had finally allowed
Mark’s DropShip to inspect the inner belt processing
plant that was to supply the germanium quota the Hansa
were offering in trade. The Razor Tooth had left just before
commencement of the live fire exercise.
  “It is not important that Razor Tooth is nine days out. It is          minutes Mark was drenched with sweat, and not just because of
important that the Hansa know she will be back.”                         the high ambient temperature.
  “We have battled them often, quiaff?”                                    “We are fortunate,” Anna said. “We have managed to avoid
  “Aff. But that is why this contact is so valuable. If the Hansa        the Hansa.”
acknowledge the value of our trade goods, their resistance to our           Mark’s lips tightened.
merchant caste will wither away and we can deploy our WarShips              His skill as a trader had earned him the new rank of
and Galaxies to more profitable markets.”                                saFactor, but before joining the merchant caste he had been a
  “It sounds like a flanking movement.” Mark could hear the              Bloodnamed warrior—and those instincts never went away.
sudden understanding in her voice.                                       Something did not feel right. “Hardly fortunate, I think. This is a
  “Trade is war by another name,” he said.                               Hansa world, Anna. And the Hansa have more wealth than most
  “Still, it is risky.”                                                  Periphery states. Do you really believe Watanabe would not have
  “All things of value are.”                                             taken the precaution of placing satellites in orbit to track us?”
   In truth, this venture was risky for another reason. Many within        “Perhaps there is not one due for an overhead.”
Clan Diamond Shark opposed trading military technology with                 Mark offered her a humorless laugh. “Watanabe has planned
Periphery states. Mark meant to change that view. And why not            everything else.”
start with the Ha Otoko? It was a solid second-line design, one             She kept silent for a long moment. “A trap, then.”
that any Periphery military would be happy to have.                        “Aff.”
   The name Ha Otoko meant “bladed man,” and the ’Mech did                 “What do we do?”
have sharp lines. It looked very much like a Japanese samurai              “What can we do? Keep your eyes open.”
complete with helmet and armor, a design specifically intended              After another twenty minutes they saw Kepten on the horizon.
to entice the followers of bushido. No surprise the Combine had          A jumble of concrete boxes, nothing taller than two stories. As
purchased the ’Mech. Nor that a second Spheroid power, the               close to a refuge as Mark could imagine.
Lyran Alliance, had followed the first.                                     He guessed they would be ambushed before they reached it.
   In the end, who could argue against also selling them to the             Minutes ticked by, but the ambush did not materialize. Could
Hanseatic League?                                                        they be that lucky?
   He set his sensors on passive and stalked his ’Mech partway up           They rejoined the road as it passed through Kepten. No traffic
the hill, proceeding just far enough for his cockpit to peek above       moved in or out of the hamlet. They saw no one on the streets,
the crest. No reason to make this easy for Watanabe.                     as if the village knew they were coming. An ugly feeling crept
   What he saw chilled his blood.                                        through Mark’s gut. “Do you see anyone moving?”
   He had found the Hansa, all right. They were half a klick east,         “I see people inside some of the buildings,” Anna answered.
but not the rest of Watanabe’s lance.                                      “But are they moving?”
   A company.                                                               A long pause. “Neg. And my thermal scan says none of them are
   Some kind of scout company, a collection of light, swift ’Mechs.      warmer than ambient.”
Which meant Watanabe’s lance of heavies was elsewhere. That                 Mark tried to swallow in a dry mouth. He stopped next to a bar,
meant sixteen to two, deadly odds even for Bloodnamed Clan               enhanced the image of a shape behind a window. It was a man,
warriors fighting Periphery barbarians. Worse if Watanabe had            a scorpion tattoo on his bulging left bicep. Lady Death’s symbol.
also managed to pry infantry or armor out of his RDF commander.          His head lolled forward, but he sat upright. As if he had been tied
   It also meant they had been betrayed.                                 to the chair.
   Mark ducked down and turned toward Anna’s Dire Wolf. “I ask             “Watanabe’s Regional Defense Force,” Mark said slowly. “They
for surkairede, Star Captain.”                                           hunt pirates, quiaff?”
   Anna understood his meaning at once. “They lied,” she said grimly.      “Aff.”
  “Aff. I saw a company beyond the hill.”                                   Mark screwed his eyes shut against the horror of this place.
  “Very well, saFactor. What do we do now?”                             “We are in very big trouble.”
   Mark’s mind raced with possibilities. He did not believe the            “I do not—”
Hansa had the courage to destroy a Clan DropShip, not when                 “Ask yourself: how could the Hansa keep our ’Mechs in such a
it would only earn them a visit from a WarShip. It followed that         way that the theft would not invite retaliation? What if they could
whatever Watanabe was planning had to look like an accident.             convince Razor Tooth that we had been destroyed, swallowed by
   Which meant witnesses would pose a problem.                           the earth itself?”
  “We head for the village.”                                                Her Dire Wolf turned, seemed to consider the monstrous shape
  “You expect help from the villagers, quineg?”                          of Feuerberg. “Our warriors would never believe it.”
  “Neg. But Watanabe cannot very well cover up a firefight within          “They will if they see an entire Hansa town destroyed by the
a population center.”                                                    volcano. Think, Anna. This town does not exist on Hansa maps.
  “And knowing this, he may choose not to have one at all,” Anna         I would wager quite a bit that this town is populated by pirate
said. “Very well, saFactor. To the village, but not by the road.”        corpses, but once it is flooded with lava none of that will be
  “Agreed,” Mark said. He glanced at a map. “If we head off at zero      obvious. It will look like a tragedy.”
seven eight, we can thread our way between the Hansa forces.”              “But we are Clan. Surely the Hansa know we will fight to the
   The two ’Mechs set out across the fractured landscape, making         death, that we will not allow them to take our ’Mechs.”
as much speed as they dared. It was a nerve-wracking transit,               Mark frowned. She was right about that. Had Watanabe made
knowing that any wrong step could hobble them. After fifteen             one fatal mistake? “I do not—”

   He ducked left as a beam of green light flashed by him. Anna                     Mark turned his ’Mech and lumbered back the way he had
stepped forward and answered with her Ultra-5 autocannon,                        come. A few more steps and his Ha Otoko would have locked up.
ripping into the lead Jenner that raced toward them. The scout                   He would have been trapped like a fly in amber.
company was upon them, the fastest ’Mechs only half a klick out.                   “We must withdraw, saFactor,” Anna called to him. “I cannot hold
  “Up ahead,” Anna shouted. “An unpaved spur vees off the main                   much longer.”
road. It runs through a defile. It will slow them down, force them                 “Anna. They maneuvered us into this place so our ’Mechs would
to come at us one at a time.”                                                    lock up from the heat. So we could be captured. We cannot go
   Mark did not like it, but they were out of options. “Very well.               down the defile.”
Keep behind me. I will hit them as long as they are in range.”                     “But there are two lances of light ’Mechs out there. We cannot
   He fought his ’Mech through a clumsy pivot. Damn the heat.                    hope to stand against so many.”

around the Jenner, costing them precious seconds.                                  “What does that mean?”

and wiped sweat from his face with the back of his arm. What he                     Mark stalked his ’Mech forward, twisted past Anna’s Dire Wolf.
would not give for an autocannon right now.                                        “What are you doing?” Anna hissed.

through his cockpit, flash-drying the sweat on his skin. He could                  “This is saFactor Mark Sennet for the scout company

                    MMMM

                    Even as the colossal machines of Clan Diamond Shark and House Kurita collide, they have to be weary of nature’s fury and the excessive heat of a coastal lava flow.

    One of the most severe problems facing any ’Mech in combat
 is internal heat build-up. Though every ’Mech can dissipate heat
 through its heat sinks (devices designed to pump heat away from

                                                  David L. McCulloch




BASINGSTOKE                                                                                                        The nice recruitment
CITY                                                                                                          officer in his spotless uniform
PRESTON                                                                                                    with the chest full of medals
FREE WORLDS LEAGUE                                                                                      had missed some important details,
21 JUNE 3068                                                                                         Aiello mused sourly as he checked
                                                                                                 his spare magazines for the tenth time
    Emile Aiello’s teeth rattled as another tremor                                           that morning. Little incidentals—like being
shook the dilapidated office building. Fine dust                                          expected to place one’s soft and fragile body in
drifted down, adding a light frosting to the mottled                                   the path of towering metal avatars of war. The sort of
grays of the Marik urban camouflage field uniform that                             thing that probably would have dissuaded even gullible
clad his squat and muscular frame. The dust obscuring his                      farm boys like sixteen-year-old Emile from joining the army.
corporal’s stripes puffed upward into the air as he absently               Even ten years ago, he’d had at least that much sense.
brushed the grit from the cold metal-and-plastic AX-22 assault            Another tremor shook the heavy building. Whatever caused
rifle cradled in his left arm. Hard, dark eyes set in a scarred face   them was getting closer.
stared into the grey dawn light. In the gloom around him, tense          “Get ready! Stay calm!” Behind Aiello, Lieutenant (Junior Grade)
figures crouched unmoving at the third floor windows as another        Santorini was pacing up and down the platoon’s position, with
tremor rippled through the building. Chunks of yellowing plaster       Sergeant Isaacs at his shoulder. In the dusty twilight the lieutenant’s
clattered onto the piles of tired furniture stacked against walls      olive skin had a marked greenish cast to it. “You all know the plan!”
covered in peeling wallpaper. The wallpaper had long since                Yes, Aiello thought as he continued to scan the roofline across the
abandoned its pristine whiteness for the faded color of old bones.     street. They did. More important, so did Santorini—after Isaacs had
    Somewhere out in the city, BattleMechs were on the move.           spent the better part of the previous evening telling the lieutenant
 just what the plan was going to be. The iron-haired sergeant
 might not afford junior officers the level of respect considered
 appropriate from a noncom in FWLM service, but she was a
 real wizard when it came to small unit tactics—the reason she
 was still in the service. Few officers lasted long in Third Platoon,
 Delta Company, Second Battalion of the 97th Marik Infantry
 Regiment. Some of the more skilled (or lucky) ones got promoted
 rapidly and went on to greater things. Others had been killed or
 wounded in gruesome ways that FWLM recruitment literature
 never talked about. Two had been cashiered. One had simply dis-
 appeared. The task of breaking in the replacements fell to Isaacs—
 which probably went a long way toward explaining her attitude.
    The unmistakable tearing sound of a heavy autocannon cut
 into the ponderous silence between titanic footfalls.
    Aiello pondered just how long Santorini would last. He
 was not alone; in whispers, PFCs Ferrier and Hendman were
 running a book with the rest of his squad. “Hendman! Ferrier!
 Shut it!” he hissed as Santorini came back up the line. Aiello
 wished Isaacs could make the lieutenant just stand still for a
 moment and shut up.
   “Now remember, our orders are to maintain the foothold on
 this side of the river. The Nineteenth Megrez Home Guard is
 on our flank, so don’t go ruining their day with stray rounds!”
 Santorini delivered his typical pre-battle pep talk, while Isaacs
 kept busy whispering into the lieutenant’s ear.
   “Those pukes from Megrez couldn’t maintain a foothold on
 a pile of Branth droppings!” Hendman grunted as Santorini
 passed out of earshot. For the tenth time in as many minutes,
 he checked the missile loaded into the SRM launcher braced
 on Ferrier’s shoulder.
   “None of that, now! We’re all one big happy Free Worlds
 League!” Ferrier delivered this last in a fair imitation of Lieutenant
 Santorini without taking his eye from the sights of Second
 Squad’s one and only support weapon. Aiello’s squad mates
 fought to stifle their laughter. Then the building trembled again,
 bombarding them with more misshapen chunks of discolored
 plaster. Mirth evaporated as hands tightened on weapons.
    Aiello spotted movement on the Irian Media Interstellar
 building across the abandoned street. Snapping the lens
 protectors open, he pressed the rubber eyepieces of his
 binoculars to his face and trained them on the high roof. PFC
 Valdez came into focus. Aiello waved to signal that she had
 his attention. Valdez raised her right hand in a fist, then raised
 four fingers. A pause, then she held up six fingers, and then
 five. Finally she signed two fingers walking, then made a sharp
 gesture across the throat.
   “Sarge!” Aiello called back over his dust-encrusted shoulder.
“Valdez says we’ve got BattleMechs coming! Four bandits!
 Sixty-five tons plus!”
    His shout brought Isaacs striding up, a surprised Lieutenant
 Santorini scurrying in her wake. “What about support?”
 Isaacs’ drawl sounded calm, almost bored.
   “She says no support.”
    If correct, that was good—very good. If
 the Lyrans were coming in without infan-
 try support, Third Platoon might
 survive the next half hour.
 Some of them, at least.
   Isaacs pondered this information. “Just a probe, then. Better tell     blast appeared, it was the damage wrought inside that counted.
our lookouts to come on in.”                                              Normally even that damage would have been little more than
   Aiello nodded in agreement. If the Elsies were serious they’d          cosmetic...until some overly confident MechWarrior parked sixty-
be sending in the big stuff. Considering that the Lyrans, either          five tons of BattleMech on the roof.
through luck or design, were about to hit the Marik line at its              Aiello heard the shriek of metal stressed beyond endurance,
weakest point—the junction between Second Battalion and                   punctuated by the staccato snapping of cross-supports as they
the displaced Megrez Home Guard—the sergeant had sounded                  gave way under a strain the building’s architects had never fac-
almost cheerful.                                                          tored into their design. With appalling suddenness, the build-
   The savage rip of an autocannon echoed again as Aiello                 ing collapsed onto itself, as if in slow motion. The fifth floor
signaled to Valdez and O’Flagherty to abandon their exposed               went first, the damaged structure on the floor below unable
lookout position. This time the roar of weapons fire was accom-           to support the weight above it. Structural failure rippled up
panied by the sound of breaking glass and falling masonry. As             through the sixth, seventh and eighth floors. Then the whole
far as Aiello knew, there were no troops forward of their posi-           disintegrating mass hammered into the lower floors like a
tion. Perhaps the approaching Lyran was shooting at shadows.              titanic pile driver.
Aiello took a sip of tepid water from his canteen to wash away               The sound was indescribable. Vast clouds of dust billowed
the chalky taste of dust and spat on the cracked tile floor. Better       outward from the seat of destruction, enveloping Third Platoon’s
at shadows than at him.                                                   refuge like a tsunami, blasting in through the glassless windows
                                                                          and coating everything in gray.
                                                                             Coughing, Aiello pulled himself up to peer at the destruction.
                                                                          Smoke and dust filled the air, choking breath and making eyes
   With a tornado’s roar, BattleMech jump jets rattled the grimy          tear. He could just see a fresh mound of still-settling wreckage
windows and broken light tubes as the distinctive hunched form            across the street. Somewhere beyond the veil of dust came the
of a Skye Jaegers’ Axman, its five-ton club held menacingly aloft,        sound of battle. The other ‘Mechs must have run into Second
rose into view. It alighted on the IMI building’s flat roof with a        Battalion’s picket line. Aiello hoped they were having as much
teeth-rattling impact. Now more than dust was making Aiello’s             luck as Third Platoon. A ragged cheer swelled to full voice, joined
mouth so dry. Third Platoon would be lucky to scratch the paint-          by whoops of victory as Third Platoon pulled themselves to their
work on that BattleMech, let alone penetrate the ten tons of              feet and surveyed the altered cityscape.
ferro-fibrous armor protecting it.                                          “Well, now,” Aiello said. “That went about as well as could be
   Sergeant Isaacs, though, had avoided the IMI building as               expected.”
the best place for Third Platoon to dig in for good reason. Even             Unfortunately, their celebrations proved premature.
though its height afforded an excellent view of the surrounding              With a grating sound of protest—as if some monster were
streets, all of its structural strength was concentrated in its core.     drawing titanic metal fingernails with vindictive delight across an
The outer sheathing of emerald glass would become a whirlwind             unimaginably gigantic blackboard—the Axman levered itself up
of glass shards just as deadly to lurking infantry as any autocan-        from the center of the debris pile. Its hatchet was gone, its right

to flame. Indeed, Third Platoon was counting on it.                       dripped sickly green coolant. Almost every square millimeter of
   Aiello didn’t have to wait for the order Isaacs barked a moment        the Axman’s armored hide was caked in dust or dulled where the
later. “Semis! Target the fourth floor! Fire!” First and Fourth squads’   distinctive checker-work paint scheme had been scoured down
semi-portable autocannons roared into life, belching meter-long           to the Kallon Unity Weave ferro-fibrous armor. Only the Steiner
tongues of malevolent yellow flame. The air filled with the acrid         fist emblem miraculously remained, clenched as if in defiance of
taste of propellant as the crews raked the frontage across the            their ambush attempt.
street. The rest of Third Platoon added to the weight of fire with           One final heave sent more rubble spilling out into the street

building’s interior.                                                      from the fall and lashed out blindly. Regardless, the result was

   The warheads found their mark with deadly accuracy. The                and surely harked back to the heyday of the First Star League, but

first murderous attack on the IMI building. Spectacular as the            from heavy weapons—for a while.
   The roar of the autocannon died. “I think we pissed him                 Stumbling in the dark on uneven floors, Aiello expected the
off,” Ferrier said, bracing the SRM launcher into firing position.      building to disintegrate around him as sixty-five tons of infuriated

slapped Ferrier’s helmet. A sharp squeeze of the trigger sent the       the platoon down the stairwell with reckless haste. The others came
missile straight into the Lyran war machine’s broad chest. The          scrambling after him—half carrying, half dragging the wounded.
semis renewed their song of destruction accompanied by the                 Aiello hit the fire door at a run. After the dread-infested dark,

tide of near-impotent bullets.                                          way hit him like a drink of sweet spring water. No time to stop
   As if to ridicule their feeble attacks, the Axman fired its auto-    and admire the scenery, though. With Second Squad in the van,
cannon again. Further down the room, two figures flew back              Aiello raced down the alley toward the river.
from the windows, as if jerked by invisible strings.                       He had gone perhaps fifty meters when the sounds he’d been
   Aiello looked up at Hendman and slapped a fresh magazine             dreading came from behind—the thud of impact and falling
into his AX-22, oblivious to a cut sliced across his left cheek by      masonry. The Axman had broken through the building’s stone
a flying shard of granite. “Yup. I’d say so!” Two more from First       façade at last.
Squad were down now. So was Lieutenant Santorini. The air was              He endured a moment of endless silence before the dark ferro-
thick with the smell of cordite and coolant, cooked meat and            crete convulsed under him. Aiello found himself thrown into the
blood.                                                                  air with the rest of Third Platoon. He landed on his left side. Hard.
   Once more the Axman’s autocannon fell silent. Swinging up his        His shoulder exploded in pain and a yawning blackness sucked
reloaded weapon, Aiello sprayed fire into the ‘Mech’s elongated         at his consciousness.
head, more in the hope of distracting the MechWarrior than                 He came to on a cold surface, gazing up at an empty gray sky
doing any real damage. The BattleMech shifted its feet, leaning         as he tried to remember who he was. Then memory came rushing
forward. Aiello’s experienced eye read the move. “Sarge!” he            back, along with throbbing agony in his shoulder. He was lying
called to Isaacs. “Think we’re going to have company in here!”          on his back in the center of the grimy alley. Fighting to ignore his
   The Lyran MechWarrior had to realize by now that he couldn’t         screaming shoulder, he pushed himself up to a sitting position
blast Third Platoon out of their building. Not quickly. Not if he       with his right arm. He saw Sergeant Isaacs kneeling next to him,
wanted his Axman fit to deal with the Marik BattleMechs that            blood streaming from her nose.
Aiello prayed were rushing to intercept the Lyran thrust at this          “Looks like he found the basement,” she croaked.
very moment. The Axman’s pilot had two choices: bypass this               “I didn’t even know that building had a basement.” Aiello got his
stubborn infantry position and find something else to molest,           legs underneath him on the second attempt. Around him, Third
or physically root out the infuriating insects impudent enough          Platoon’s dazed survivors were beginning to regain their feet.
to challenge the king of the battlefield. So how angry had they            Isaacs stood up, looking as shaky as Aiello felt. She nodded
made that MechWarrior? Aiello hosed down the Axman’s cockpit            toward the ruins of their hastily abandoned refuge. “Neither did he.”
once more for good measure.                                                The grating sound that came from beneath their feet was felt
   The Axman lunged at the building.                                    more than heard. Aiello shot an incredulous glance at Isaacs.
   The collision threw Aiello to the floor so hard, he wondered if     “You’ve got to be kidding me! What does it take to stop this guy?”
it was worthwhile getting back up. The structure shook a second            Isaacs didn’t answer. “Third Platoon! Everyone move it! Now!”
time as sixty-five tons of BattleMech slammed into the ferrocrete       She grabbed Aiello’s good arm and pushed him down the alley.
outside. What remained of the ceiling plaster finally gave up its      “Quick! Take the first right. Head for the storm drain.” Then she was
battle against gravity. It came crashing down in ragged sheets,         gone, shoving stragglers in the same direction.
throwing white powder everywhere. Aiello crawled out from                  Waving Ferrier and Hendman ahead of him, Aiello began to
under crumbling bits of roof and pulled himself up to the window.       jog unsteadily. He could hear grunts and curses mixed with the

its feet—an operation hampered by its missing arm. Whether              fought the urge to look back. Rounding the corner, he immedi-
from its encounter with the building or the sidewalk, plates of         ately saw the storm drain. But it was not the sight of Isaacs’ sanc-
armor had been torn away from the left thigh, exposing myomer           tuary that brought a sudden grin to a face caked with dust and
bundles that writhed as the ‘Mech continued to struggle. Aiello         blood. The squat shape of an FWLM SRM carrier was parked just
thought he spotted actuator damage, too. With all the shifting          beyond the ferrocrete spillway.
rubble, the ‘Mech couldn’t get its left leg under it.                      As Third Platoon piled into the ready-made trench, thunder-
  “Oops!” Aiello muttered as the Axman overbalanced and fell            ous footfalls made it abundantly clear that the Axman had freed
forward. It crashed into the building and then slid back to the         itself once more. Bringing up the rear, a sprinting Isaacs leaped
street, jolting the structure once more.                                down into cover. Spraying wild shots from its remaining lasers,
  “This place can’t take much more of this. Time for us to go.”         the BattleMech came staggering round the corner—straight into

   Ominous grinding sounds told of movement in the street as            clear. For a moment the ravaged titan stood motionless. Then it
they ran for the emergency stairwell.                                   collapsed backward into a two-story garage.
                                                                           Wiping his face, Aiello noticed the blood for the first time. He
                                                                        looked across to where Isaacs lay panting. “Can we go home now?”
                     SLSL

                           House Marik’s First Atrean Dragoons deploy to secure a city on a world in House Davion’s Sarna March during Operation Guerrero.

   The rampant destruction caused during the early Succession                                can bear. Two numbers describe buildings in BattleTech: the
Wars appalled even the most hardened soldiers. Until the Jihad,                              Construction Factor (CF) and levels.
recent decades saw the evolution of a common practice to avoid                                  Default Values: If a scenario does not specify a building’s type,
combat in populated areas, especially among the warrior Clans,                               treat it as medium, Level 2.
who consider the collateral damage caused by such battles
unacceptable. Despite this unwritten law, combat still had a                                  CONSTRUCTION FACTOR
tendency to drift from the intended battlefield into urban areas.                               The Construction Factor (CF) determines how a building’s

set ambushes in cities, counting on the unspoken truce in those                              CF is the number of damage points a building hex can take
areas to offer an extra measure of surprise. The Jihad has opened                            before being reduced to rubble; this damage, regardless of when
up a whole new chapter of brutal combat within cities.                                       it occurs, is tracked across a game until the building hex CF is

plicate warfare in the thirty-first century. Battles fought in long,                         levels of a building hex under some circumstances (such as Area

enemy hiding places and offer limited protection from weapons                                tons of additional weight each level of a building hex can support
fire require a change in tactics and operations. In urban combat,                            without collapsing.
even conventional infantry may substantially damage a ’Mech.                                    The range of possible CF values for each building type appears

ing’s CF, assume that a light building hex has CF 15, a medium                 BUILDING MODIFIERS TABLE
building hex has CF 40, a heavy building hex has CF 90 and a
hardened building hex has CF 120.                                                                                                                        INTRODUCTION

Battle Armor
   A battle armor unit with Jumping MP may enter a building at
any level higher than ground level (Level 0), provided that level
is equal to or less than the sum of the battle armor’s Jumping MP                             LEVEL 4
                                                                                          LEVEL 3
plus the level of the battle armor unit in the hex where the jump                     LEVEL 2
started. The unit must have sufficient Jumping MP to allow it to                  LEVEL 1
enter the target building hex (1 MP); it must have line of sight              LEVEL 0
to the target building hex level before starting movement; and
it cannot enter such a hex if that would violate the stacking rules.
Finally, when using Jumping MP to enter a building hex below
the level of the roof, the battle armor unit’s movement ends
immediately upon entering the first building hex.
   When the battle armor unit jumps into a building hex (not
including the roof ), it must make an Anti-’Mech Skill roll. The
controlling player should first consult the Piloting Skill Modifier
column of the Building Modifiers Table and apply the appropriate
target number modifier for the building type. If the roll is suc-
cessful, the battle armor unit takes no damage. If unsuccessful,
the battle armor unit takes a single hit, with damage based on

        A ’Mech and a rifle (ballistic) foot platoon are on the roof
     in Hex A (Level 2). The controlling player wishes to move his
    ’Mech off of the building as quickly as possible. Hexes 1, 2 and
     3 all have levels within the two-level limit on level changes
     for ’Mechs, and so the ’Mech can enter any of them provided it
     has sufficient MP available. If it had jump jets, the ’Mech could
     simply jump to its target hex, as none of the hexes into which
     it might move contain buildings (a ’Mech cannot enter a build-
     ing hex using jump jets, except to land on a roof).

Infantry Inside Buildings
   Because infantry are comparatively small and are trained to
use even the least bit of concealing terrain to their advantage,                                                                               RARA
units outside a building hex cannot fire directly at infantry units
inside a building. They must fire at the building hex instead. As                           Clan Wolf Elementals finish securing a building.
with other units, the attacker must have a valid line of sight to
the building hex, as well as the level within the building occupied       AREA EFFECT WEAPONS
by the target infantry unit. Damage applied to the building hex             Area-effect (AE) weapon types (as defined under Weapons
from an attacker that did not have LOS to the level occupied by           and Equipment, p. 113) affect building hexes in a unique fashion.
the infantry has no effect on the infantry unit and only damages          Because such weapons are designed to blanket an entire hex,
the hex’s CF.                                                             effectively creating a “sphere of damage,” AE weapons do not

units inside the building hex, does so according to the Infantry          building (and any units inside it) at levels above and below the

determine hit location (see Attacks Against Battle Armor, p. 219).        may have LOS to higher levels of the building in that hex and so

   The collapse of hex E means that ten of the building’s eigh-
teen hexes have collapsed. Ten is more than half, and so the                                                                                 DFDF
rest of the building hexes automatically collapse as well. These
events must be resolved before the players take any other
action in the game.
    First, the players apply damage inflicted by the collapsing
building. In the case of the Marauder, at the beginning of
the Ground Movement Phase, Hex E had a CF of 8. Divided
by 10, this gives a base damage of 0.8. The building had two
levels above the Marauder, and so the ’Mech takes 3 points of
damage (base damage 0.8 x 3 = 2.4, rounded up to 3). Player 2
assigns the damage using the Front/Back column of the ’Mech
Punch Hit Location Table.
    Player 1’s units, all in Hex B, fare somewhat worse. At the
beginning of the Ground Movement Phase, Hex B had a CF
of 39; divided by 10, this gives a base damage of 3.9. Luckily,
the Spider is on the roof, meaning that no building levels are
above it. It therefore takes 4 points of damage (3.9 rounded
up), assigned against the Front/Back column of the ’Mech Hit
Location Table. The battle armor is likewise on the roof, but
battle armor takes double damage from buildings: in this
case, a 5-point and a 3-point hit. The Locust is at Level 2 in Hex
B. With only 1 level above the ’Mech, it also takes 4 points of
damage, applied against the Front/Back column of the Punch
Hit Location Table.
   The unluckiest unit is the rifle platoon on Level 0. It has three
building levels above it, and as a conventional infantry unit, it
takes three times standard damage from a building collapse.
The platoon takes 47 points of damage: base damage of 3.9,
plus (3.9 x 3) 11.7 for the levels above the unit, then x 3 for being
infantry (46.8, rounded up). The unit only has 28 troopers, and
so is destroyed.                                                                 RFL-8D Rifleman, Twenty-sixth Lyran Guard (House Steiner)

                                                                             ORBITAL INSERTION , MELISSIA
                                                                               LYRAN ALLIANCE
                                                                                  15 AUGUST 3064

                                                                                                Nikol ignored the heavy vibrations of re-entry as
                                                                                               the Broadsword-class DropShip plowed through
                                                                                                 the thick atmosphere of Melissia, and finished
                                                                                                  suiting up in the cramped corner of the cargo
                                                                                                    bay. The one-piece suit flowed like liquid
                                                                                                     metal (instantly warm to the touch), the Clan
                                                                                                      copper composite mesh woven with biosyn-
                                                                                                     thetic fiber ribbings into a second skin.
                                                                                                      “Five minutes to combat drop,” an electronic
                                                                                                  voice echoed.
                                                                                                “You mean second combat drop,” Nikol snapped
                                                                                             to a bay that contained only strapped-down cargo
                                                                                             and a labor casteman in a loader exoskeleton
                                                                                            moving an ammo crate. During combat operations?
                                                                                           Brave, or stupid? She shook her head at questions
                                                                                      that could easily apply to her, stomped the two paces to
                                                                             the leg of her ProtoMech and scrambled up the lean-to ladder.
JADE FALCON CLAN HALL, SUDETEN                                               Nikol couldn’t believe that Star Colonel Uvin Buhallin had actu-
JADE FALCON OCCUPATION ZONE                                                  ally ordered two Clusters of Elementals, along with her Point of
30 OCTOBER 3063                                                              ProtoMechs, to cram into every available space in order to fool
                                                                             the Lyrans into thinking only a Star of ’Mechs were about to

chamber, acknowledging the conclave of Bloodnamed warriors                   Buhallin’s order or not didn’t matter. They drop the Elementals
of the Jade Falcon Clan Council on Sudeten. “Scientist-General

upgraded and on-line,” he said. He suppressed a shiver at the

momentary quiver in his voice. They are warriors, but I am a scientist.
                                                                             newest over-sized Erinyes battle armor.
I serve the Clan, just as they do. “The first run of Spirit BattleMechs is
