                                                                          Jason Schmetzer




                                                                                           was on his wing, and the others staggered
                                                                                          behind for almost a kilometer. Marko nodded,
                                                                                         checked the timer and toggled the Star channel
                                                                                       on his comm systems.
                                                                                       “Twenty-five seconds, Red Bears,” he said. “Right
                                                                                    now our Elementals are holding against stravag
                                                                                   bandits from the Inner Sphere. Right now, on the hal-
                                                                                 lowed plains where we claimed this world ten years ago,
                                                                             Ghost Bear warriors are fighting and dying.” He squeezed
                                                                        his control yoke again and swallowed. “Not against scum. Not
                                                                      any longer.” He sent a pre-arranged signal on another channel.
                                                                     “We are the only support in range. We are the Red Bears.”
                                                                        “It is time.”
                                                                         As one the ten Xerxes, awash in the actinic white glare of
                                                                      Thule’s sun, fired thrusters and flipped end for end, until they
ORBITAL SPACE                                                         were flying tail-first and canopy-down. Marko waited for the
THULE                                                                 last few seconds to tick off the chronometer that glowed on his
GHOST BEAR DOMINION                                                   heads-up display. The 1 flickered and died. Marko jammed his
15 SEPTEMBER 3060                                                     throttle forward, igniting the fusion-fired torch at the rear of his
                                                                      fighter.
   Star Commander Marko rolled his Xerxes to port, bringing the          The Star staggered, slowed and fell, flaming spears of righ-
white-streaked brown orb of Thule into view through his cockpit       teous fury from the sky.
canopy. He and his Star hung two hundred kilometers above
the Fresdon Desert. He craned his neck, looking. Not even his
genetically perfect eyes could pierce the veil of distance and
see the battle raging below. His fingers twisted the controls in       Heat shuddered from the ground in waves, distorting the view
frustration. Maybe Snow Ravens could deal with waiting better.       of the approaching BattleMechs until they looked like waver-
He knew the equations as well as any Raven; he knew he had to        ing shapes from a Nova Cat’s vision. Elemental Star Captain
wait for the window.                                                 Drew Kabrinski of the Fifty-fourth Provisional Garrison Cluster
  “Star Commander,” his wingman said, “thirty seconds to de-         lowered himself a few centimeters and looked around him. Beta
orbit burn.”                                                         Point was still huddled around the fallen Commando. The three
   Marko rolled his fighter back to its previous orientation. He     remaining troopers of Gamma Point huddled with them, their
checked his tactical display, noting the precise separation of the   backpack missile launchers empty. The blasted corpse of the
other nine Xerxes in his Star. Each was where it belonged. Lucien    bandit Valkyrie was still smoking, providing enough cover for the
 Elementals of Delta and Epsilon Points to hide. The rest of the
 pirate scout lance had fled, but Drew didn’t doubt they’d be
 returning with the rest of their stravag company.
   “Less than a minute, Star Captain,” his Point second said.
 Timothy had the rest of Alpha Point concealed behind them.
 They were the vanguard, the only Point not hidden amid the
 shattered BattleMechs. They would stall the bandits’ advance
 openly, and the rest of the Star could fall on them from ambush.
   “We cannot hold them all,” Timothy said.
    Drew grinned in his helmet, safe from the other’s view.
“We are Ghost Bears, Timothy,” he said. He beckoned with his
 small-laser arm. “We, all of us, are a pack. We will hunt, and
 these dezgra pirates will feel the power of our jaws and claws.”
 He lowered his arm and his voice. “It will be a battle worth
 remembering. Perhaps I’ll include it in my Great Work.”
    Timothy’s armor stared at him, then bobbed its helmet.
“Aff, Star Captain.” The Star thought little of his chosen Work,
 but not all warriors took the same path to enlightenment.
 History was not well thought of in the Clan. He did not
 mind; the Work was his, not theirs.
    Drew looked back at the approaching ’Mechs.
 He saw the range counting down, watched it fall
 beneath a kilometer. An amber signal burned to life
 in the upper-left edge of his display, and his grin
 widened. Worthy, indeed.
   “Four minutes,” he said. “We hold them for four
 minutes.”




   The interference cleared from Marko’s display as
the fiery cocoon of ionization fell away. He heard
the shrill scream of air outside his canopy as his Star fell
through the ever-thickening atmosphere. The Xerxes began
to respond as he manipulated his rudder pedals, and the
brown desert below him took on definition. He saw a tiny
speck of black that his brain recognized as a pall of smoke. His
lips drew back from his teeth as he kept the throttle forward,
driving the 85-ton aerospace fighter faster down the gravity
well.
  “Star Commander!” Lucien shouted, but static and a rippling
boom cut off his words. Marko craned his neck, searching the
sky for his wingman’s aircraft, but saw only the gray-red-black
mussel shape of a falling explosion.
  “Fighters!” one of the other pilots called.
  “Savashri!” Marko spat, and jerked his yoke to port. He
kicked his starboard rudder and put the fighter into a spin,
trying to evade the weapons lock that screamed from his
computers. The sky lit with more explosions, and Marko
saw a white-painted hull flash by as the enemy fighters
climbed past them.
  “They caught us while we were in the ionization blanket.”
Marko knew his pilots knew that already, but he spoke the
words nonetheless. “Deal with these interlopers and then get
dirtside. Our people are counting on us.”
   Acknowledgments rang in his helmet as the Point
Commanders peeled away from the formation in pairs. Marko
manipulated his controls and flipped his Xerxes around,
venting plasma exhaust toward the surface while he dumped
velocity. The g meter rose, while the weight pressing on Marko’s          Sentinel moved, trying to get a shot around the armored bulk of
chest climbed. The Xerxes fought its away around gravity and              the mangled forearm.
hurled itself after the offending enemy aerospace fighter. Lucien            Drew let out half a breath and squeezed the trigger. The
would have done the same for him, Marko knew. This surat was              Sentinel’s autocannon exploded as the battlesuit’s laser reached
his, and his alone.                                                       through the damage from the SRMs and detonated ammunition

ics in the lower right corner of his HUD. Marko waited until the            “Delay them we can,” Timothy said, taking advantage of the
computer froze on one fighter model, then frowned. A Rapier. It           momentary confusion to burn his own scar on the Sentinel’s
was rare to see a vintage fighter like that in the inventories of the     armor. “But for how long?”
Inner Sphere. He pulled the Xerxes into a bank and followed the              Drew looked to the sky, past the chronometer that stood
enemy fighter around.                                                     almost at zero, and wondered the same thing.
  “Star Commander!” Point Commander Devin shouted. “I have
a positive ID on markings. These are ComStar fighters! What is
ComStar doing supporting bandits?”

a firing solution. The powerful extended-range large lasers in            Star Commander ripped its wings away.

pelted the Xerxes as Marko followed his prey. He was a Ghost              teeth and pulled on his yoke, bringing the heavy fighter around.
Bear, and he had the scent of his enemy.                                  He waited, his hawk’s eyes watching while his ears counted the
   The hunt was on.                                                       tones, until the shrill solid whine of a lock reverberated through

    Drew Kabrinski ducked his head as he fired the short-range            port wing tore loose and fell, sending the 85-ton fighter into a
 missiles mounted in the shoulders of his battle armor. The pair          death spin that would last for almost a hundred kilometers. The
 of fat-bodied munitions spiraled in, both of them true on target,        other intruding fighters had already been dealt with.
 and struck the bandit Sentinel on the right arm. He allowed                 Compliments rang on the Star channel, but Marko ignored

    The remaining BattleMechs were moving cautiously, trying              together the nine remaining fighters embraced gravity.
 to smoke out the Elemental ambush they knew had to be                       They were late.
 waiting. The enemy commander was smart, but not that smart.
 Their target had to be something in the depot at the edge of
 the Fresdon Desert, but the bandit was allowing himself to be
 slowed by a group of armored infantry.                                      Drew grunted as he hit the Sentinel, the shock of the impact

 the objective.”                                                          Sentinel’s armor and hold on. A series of clangs and double-click
   “Then let us be glad you are not a bandit, Star Captain,” Timothy      signals on the radio told him his entire Point had managed to
 said. His battlesuit landed a dozen meters or so away from Drew’s.       attach itself to the 45-ton ’Mech.
“Or else we would be chasing them, instead of evading them.”                “Target the gyro,” he ordered, and suited action to word.

   Like great white arrows, contrails billowing behind them, a           and ran, ignoring the fighters and dredging every last erg of
cluster of fighters dove for the ground.                                 strength from their myomers as they fled.
  “Get ready,” he called on the Star channel.                               Marko let them go. He reduced his throttle and began a slow
                                                                         orbit of the battlefield. “Epsilon,” he said, “high-altitude pursuit.
                                                                         Keep an eye on the rest of those ’Mechs. Maybe we can catch
                                                                         their DropShip, too.” He watched the two fighters peel away from
    At sixty kilometers, Marko’s heads-up display pinged and             the orbiting cloud, then looked back at the ground. Only one red
 painted green and red icons on the ground below. Marko had              icon remained.
 to adjust the magnification before he could distinguish between            The Sentinel.
 the green clusters—depleted Points of Elementals, he knew—
 and the red icons of pirate BattleMechs. He sent targeting data
 to the rest of the Point and adjusted his descent, flaring out and
 angling for a strafing run.                                                 Drew watched the other BattleMechs retreat from over the
   “Shock barrage,” he ordered. “Show the surats we are here.”            Sentinel’s shoulder with satisfaction. It irked him to allow bandits
    The Star fell into a staggered column, with Beta Point leading        to escape, but he knew the Ghost Bear fighters would tail the
 and Marko in the second position. The Beta Point Commander              ’Mechs back to their DropShips. He turned his attention back to
 selected a targeting zone between the bulk of the ‘Mechs and             his own task, and climbed the last meter to the Sentinel’s cockpit
 the largest concentration of Elementals. A red target indicator          hatch.
 appeared on Marko’s HUD. Beta Point fired. Marko fired. The rest            His claw made short work of the entry locks, and he kicked the
 of the Star, stretched out behind them, fired.                           hatch inward with one battlesuit boot. He had just enough room
    From the ground it looked as though a flickering red wall of          to stick his right-arm laser in and fire.
 laser light divided the battlefield. Volley after volley of carefully       And fire.
 targeted cannon shells exploded into the desert sands, raising              And fire again.
 massive clouds of dust and smoke. Some of the other Clans                   The Sentinel collapsed, a marionette with cut strings. Drew
 might call such a display wasteful, but Marko knew the truth             withdrew his laser and leaped, firing his jets for as long as they
 that all Ghost Bears knew. Fear was a powerful weapon, not to            would burn. Timothy and the Point Commanders were waiting
 be wasted. From the brief scan his sensors had made, Marko saw           for him when he landed.
 the battle had paused as the bandits eyed the line the fighters            “We could not have defeated them without the fighters, quiaff?”
 had drawn. They must know they had already crossed it.                   Timothy asked.
   “The Elementals have held,” he said, speaking to his pilots.              Drew reached back and unsealed his helmet. The hot, dry air stank
“Now it is our turn.”                                                     with burnt metal and overheated coolant, but it was fresh. “Aff.”
    He spotted a concentration of enemy BattleMechs a safe                  “Then we were fodder for the fighter pilots?” said the Beta
 hundred meters away from the nearest Elemental Point. “Target,”          Point Commander.
 he said, and tapped in the data. “Epsilon, Gamma, move to the              “Neg,” Drew answered. “We did our part. The bandits died here,
 east. Delta, Beta, from the west.” He selected a lone ’Mech, a           and not closer to the depot.” He looked up as a single Xerxes
 thirty-ton Javelin near a Sentinel crawling with Elementals, as his      heavy fighter screamed overhead. It dipped its wings in salute
 own target.                                                              and then ripped the air as it climbed toward orbit. “The angels
   “Engage,” he said, and jerked back on his yoke. The Xerxes leapt       did the rest.”
 into a long climb. Marko held the stick back and let the fighter           “Angels?”
 turn over. It fell again into the gentle caress of Thule’s gravity,        “An ancient saying.” Drew turned his back to the downed
 and Marko adjusted his targeting. The Javelin was firing at the          Sentinel. “From before humankind left Terra. Warriors on the
 Elementals on the Sentinel.                                              ground would refer to their supporting fighters as ‘angels on
   “You threaten Ghost Bears,” he whispered, too low for his micro-       our shoulders.’” Drew raised his battle claw toward the dwindling
 phone to pick up. “Now we threaten back.”                                fighter in salute. “Guardians from on high.”

                     Aerospace fighters from Clan Ghost Bear’s Beta Galaxy begin an attack run against forces of Clan Nova Cat during the Combine-Ghost Bear War.

  As with movement, combat involving aerospace units is                                       LARGE CRAFT WEAPON BAYS
complex when compared to ground unit combat rules. Different                                     Many Large Craft carry dozens of weapons, and so it is imprac-
systems apply depending on whether a unit is operating in space,                              tical to make to-hit rolls for each individual laser, autocannon,
in atmosphere against other aerospace units or in atmosphere                                  PPC and missile launcher. Instead, Large Craft use a system
against ground units. Rules for aerospace combat in atmosphere                                that places similar weapons in a group, for which the control-
are a subset of the standard rules for combat in microgravity                                 ling player makes a single to-hit roll and hit location roll. This
(space). The following rules apply to combat in space and also                                system speeds up play involving heavily armed Large Craft. Such
provide the basis for all other aerospace combat.                                             weapon groups are known as “bays,” with each bay containing
   Some of the rules for aerospace combat against ground units                                a number of weapons of the same class. For example, different
appear in the Combat section; this section fully fleshes out those                            types of lasers appear in the same bay.
rules for aerospace units in game play.                                                          In the game description of a Large Craft (on a record sheet or
                                                                                              in a technical readout), each row in the Weapons Table represents
SCALE                                                                                         a bay of one or more weapons in the same class. A weapons bay
   Aerospace units encompass a vast range of sizes, from ten-ton                              is fired as though it were a single weapon, generating heat and
fighters to million-ton WarShips. Using the same scale for such                               inflicting damage in the same manner even though it may actu-
units is impractical, so weapons and armor are divided into two                               ally consist of many individual weapons.
categories—standard and capital. When standard-scale weapons
attack standard-scale armor, or capital-scale weapons attack                                  AEROSPACE UNITS VS. GROUND UNITS
capital-scale armor, the effects follow the standard rules: one                                 Though Total Warfare integrates aerospace and ground units
point of damage destroys one box of armor. With weapons and                                   as much as possible for ease of play, important differences exist
armor from different categories, several additional effects come                              between the two because of inherent differences of design and
into play (see Damage, p. 238).                                                               use. These are not simply small differences of modifiers between
   While Total Warfare does not cover WarShips and similar Large                              units (as between different types of ground units), but core differ-
Craft that mount capital-scale armor as well as additional capital-                           ences in game mechanics that affect all aerospace units and are
scale weapons, the rules in this section make the integration of                              distinct from similar rules governing ground units. The following
such vessels simple and easy. These rules also define such units                              short list provides a quick thumbnail reference guide to these
to eliminate potential confusion when players are looking at                                  differences.
record sheets or technical readout statistics (such as Technical                                 Players should not consider this list a substitute for reading the
Readout: 3057, Revised) and wondering why such large units                                    rules in this section, but instead as a way to lay the groundwork
appear to have so little armor in comparison to DropShips and                                 of important differences from previous sections, from which the
fighters. (WarShips and similar Large Craft are covered in detail in                          rules in this section can easily build.
Strategic Operations.)

    AEROSPACE WEAPON RANGE TABLE                                      Aerospace units employ a modified version of the
                                                                    ground-unit rules to determine range to the target and
      Range Bracket       Hexes (Standard)    Hexes (Capital)       range modifiers. Unlike ground units, where every weapon                    INDEX

    Short                       0­–6              0–12              has its own short, medium or long range, aerospace
                                                                    units use standardized ranges that apply to all weapons
    Medium                     7–12               13–24             equally. The four range brackets for all aerospace weapons
    Long                       13–20              25–40             are Short, Medium, Long and Extreme, as shown on the
    Extreme                    21–25              41–50             Aerospace Weapon Range Table.



                                                                                                                                               235
                                                                          Angle of Attack
                                                                             The direction of the target’s movement relative to the
                                                                             attacker—the angle of attack—can have a significant
                                                                               impact on the accuracy of a shot. Firing at a target’s aft
                                                                                 offers the least amount of relative motion, while firing
                                                                                    at the target’s side as it flies past offers the greatest
                                                                                      difficulty. To determine the angle of attack, find
                                                                                        the attack direction and use the appropriate
                                                                                         modifier as shown on the Aerospace Attack
                                                                                         Modifiers Table.
                                                                                            Targets in the Same Hex: If two units in the
                                                                                         same hex wish to attack one another, players
                                                                                         should first determine what unit types are
                                                                                         attacking. Treat the unit with the higher Initiative
                                                                                         as one hex back along its direction of travel.
                                                                                            Per the Movement Subphases rule (see p. 76),
                                                                                         for the purposes of attack angles, fighters are
                                                                                         considered to have a higher Initiative than
                                                                                         small craft, which have a higher Initiative than
                                                                                         DropShips. If the two units in the same hex are
                                                                                         identical (two DropShips, two aerospace fighters
                                                                                         or two small craft), then treat the unit with the
                                                                                         higher Current Velocity as one hex back along its
                                                                                         direction of travel. If two units of the same type
                                                                                         also have the same velocity, roll 2D6 and treat
                                                                                         the unit with the higher result as one hex back
                                                                                         along its direction of travel. Re-roll any ties.

                                                                                        Effects of Atmosphere
                                                                                       Atmospheric distortion, clouds and winds dra-
                                                                                  matically reduce the ranges of weapons carried by
                                                                               fighters and DropShips. To reflect this, each atmosphere
                                                                             and ground hex on a space map counts as six hexes for
                                                                         range purposes. If this conversion results in a shot passing
              • aerodyne firing arcs diagram •                        through one attack range and into the next, use the longer range.
                                                                        The space/atmosphere interface also reduces the effective
   For example, any standard-scale weapon that has a maximum          ranges of weapons and counts as three hexes for purposes of
range of Medium can hit a target within twelve hexes of the firing    determining range. Standard-scale weapons can fire into or out
                                                                      of but not through an interface hex. A unit must occupy an inter-
unit, but cannot strike a target thirteen or more hexes away.
                                                                      face hex in order to fire at units in space and in the atmosphere.
   As noted under Scale (see p. 234), range works differently

Location Table to determine the                                          ways described below. If more than one of these conditions
location that took damage, using the appropriate column based            applies, the attacking player must roll for each occurrence. For
on the attack direction. Each entry on the table has two parts. The      example, if a single strike exceeds the Damage Threshold, causes
first indicates the armor facing from which damage is subtracted.        SI damage and is a natural 12, the player must roll three times for
The second is used for determining critical hits (see p. 237).           critical hits.
   Above/Below: The Above/Below column is only used in spe-                 To determine if a unit takes a critical hit, roll 2D6 whenever one
cific situations (see Air-to-Air Attacks, p. 241). When determining      of the four conditions below is met.
the side or wing affected by an above/below shot, roll 1D6. On a            For attacks against units mounting standard-scale armor, on a
result of 1–3, the right side or wing is affected; on a 4–6, the left.   result of 8 or higher the appropriate critical hit for that location
   Cluster Weapons: Determining hit locations for cluster weapons        takes effect, as shown on the Hit Location Table (see p. 237).
works differently with aerospace units than with other unit types.
The Attack Value of all cluster weapons except Plasma weapons
and Ultra Autocannons (or all cluster weapon bays except Ultra
Autocannon bays) is divided into 5-point Attack Value groupings,
with any remaining damage assigned to its own grouping. The
attacking player then makes a separate hit location roll for each
Attack Value grouping. Ultra Autocannon divide their published
Attack Value in half and apply each half as one of two separate
clusters. Plasma weapons function as non-cluster weapons against
heat-tracking units, applying heat normally. Against targets that
do not track heat, Plasma Rifles add 2D6 to their 10 point Attack
Value and Plasma Cannons roll 3D6 for an Attack Value, and both
weapons apply all damage in this manner as five-point clusters.

                                                                                 A hit that inflicts critical damage has the following effects          SUPPORT
                                                                             per the hit location roll made for the attack (see Hit Location            VEHICLES
                                                                             Table, p. 237).
                                                                                 Avionics: The flight computer is damaged. The first two hits
                                                                                                                                                        INFANTRY
                                                                             each impose a +1 penalty on all Control rolls. The third avion-
                                                                             ics critical hit destroys the system and imposes a +5 penalty
                                                                             to Control Rolls. Each avionics hit also forces the unit to make
                                                                                                                                                      AEROSPACE
                                                                             a Control Roll.                                                            UNITS
                                                                                 Bomb: One bomb is rendered useless; it does not explode
                                                                             nor causes damage. If the unit carries no bombs, the critical hit
                                                                             has no effect. The controlling player determines which bomb                CREATING
                                                                             the critical hit affects. Bomb damage makes re-entry opera-               SCENARIOS
                                                                             tions more difficult (see Space/Atmosphere Interface, p. 78).
                                                                                 Cargo: Part of the unit’s cargo sustains damage, deter-                PAINTING
                                                                             mined randomly between general cargo bays and unit bays.                  MINIATURES
                                                                                 To determine the percentage of cargo destroyed, divide the
                                                                             amount of damage that caused the critical hit by twice the
                                                                             vessel’s SI. Multiply that number by 100, rounding fractions                INDEX
                                                                             up. For DropShips and small craft, use the standard-scale
                                                                             damage value in this calculation.
                                                                                 When a cargo hit occurs, roll 1D6. On a result of 1–3, general
                                                                             cargo suffers damage (if present). On a result of 4–6, unit bays
                                                                   PMEPME   (if present) are affected. Round any fractions up.

           COLLISIONS AND                                               To determine damage from a collision or ramming attack,
                                                                     roll 1D6. On a result of 1–5, the two units collide and suffer full
              RAMMING                                                damage as indicated below. A result of 6 indicates a glancing
                                                                     collision that causes half the normal damage.

phase in which they occur.                                           divided by 10, multiplied by the net velocity of the impact.             PLAYING

   If the ramming unit is destroyed, the attack fails.               AIR-TO-AIR ATTACKS
                                                                                                                                             VEHICLES
                                                                        Atmospheric combat uses the same rules as space encoun-
                                                                     ters, with the following exceptions.
                                                                        Units at the same altitude can fire at each other using the          SUPPORT
         RAMMING ATTACKS TABLE                                       standard rules. Units at different altitudes can fire at each           VEHICLES
                                                                     other, but they add one hex to the range for each different
   Base To-Hit Number:                                               level of altitude. For example, two fighters are ten hexes apart.
                                                                     One is at Altitude 3 and the other is at Altitude 5. The effective      INFANTRY
        6 + (target Piloting Skill – attacker Piloting Skill)

   Modifiers                                                         cannot aim into the area immediately above or below their
                                                                     own hexes.                                                            AEROSPACE

                                                                     target in an adjacent hex). If the attacker and target are two         SCENARIOS
                                                                     altitudes apart, the target must be three hexes away, and so on.

   If players choose to use the printed side of a BattleTech map-              the low-altitude map and enter Row 1 of the atmosphere on
sheet for low-altitude movement, then they must also take terrain              the high-altitude map (see p. 79).
into account. Use the rules for Intervening Terrain, page 100.
                                                                          Aerospace Units on Ground Mapsheets
                                                                             If players are using the Aerospace Units on Ground Mapsheets
                                                                          rules (p. 91) and an airborne aerospace unit launches an attack
                                                                          against another airborne aerospace unit, keep in mind that the
                                                                          ranges for such weapon attacks must be multiplied by 16 to fall
                                                                          into line with the necessary changes of scale. For example, the
                                                                          standard-scale short range bracket would extend to 96 hexes,
                                                                          while the standard-scale medium range bracket would extend to
                                                                          192 hexes, and so on. Likewise, players would need to multiply
                                                                          the capital-scale ranges by 16.

                                                                          Airborne Aerospace Units vs. Other Airborne Units
                                                                             Airborne aerospace units may only execute air-to-air attacks
                                                                          against other airborne aerospace units. Against all other targets
                                                                          (including airborne non-aerospace units such as WiGE and VTOL
                                                                          vehicles, and other units expending VTOL MPs), airborne aero-
                                                                          space units may only execute air-to-ground attacks (see Air-to-
                                                                          Ground Attacks, below)

                                                                          AIR-TO-GROUND ATTACKS
                                                                             Though more fragile than ground-based units, aerospace units
                                                                          can carry a massive array of weapons that allows them to cripple
                                                                          or destroy ground units from the relative safety of the sky. Even a
                                                                          handful of aerospace units can provide a devastating advantage
                                                                          when supporting ground combat.
                                                                             When an aerospace unit ends its movement in an atmo-
                                                                          spheric hex of a high-altitude map (or any hex of a low-altitude
                                                                          map) containing a ground mapsheet, it can attack targets on
        • low-altitude air-to-air attacks diagram •                       that ground mapsheet. When an aerospace unit is declaring its
                                                                          weapon attacks, the player must first nominate an attack path,

    while the fighter in Hex B is at Altitude 7.                          using the Aerospace Units on Ground Mapsheets rules use their

                                                                                                                                                            243
                                        In the Air-To-Ground Attack diagram at left, a primary Shiva
                                    fighter (Gunnery Skill 4) ended its movement at Altitude 3 in a
                                    hex on the low-altitude map that corresponds with the Canyon
                                    map (the ground mapsheet). The enemy player has numer-
                                    ous units holed up in the canyon, tearing up the controlling
                                    player’s ground troops: a vehicle (moved 3 hexes) and a con-
                                    ventional infantry unit (moved 1 hex) in Hex A; a battle armor
                                    unit (jumped 3 hexes) and a ’Mech (moved 5 hexes) in Hex B;
                                    a ProtoMech (jumped 5 hexes) in Hex C; an airborne VTOL at
                                    Elevation 3 (moved 5 hexes) in Hex D; an airborne WiGE vehicle
                                    at Elevation 1 (moved 7 hexes) in Hex 1; and another ’Mech
                                    (moved 4 hexes) in Hex 2. The controlling player has brought in
                                    the aerospace fighter to try to soften things up.
                                        Regardless of whether the controlling player decides to
                                    launch a strafing, striking or bombing attack, he must first
                                    select an attack path across the ground mapsheet. He chooses
                                    his attack path (as noted on the diagram) so that he can fire on
                                    as many enemy units as possible.
                                        If the controlling player were using the Aerospace Units on
                                    Ground Mapsheets rules (see p. 91), then the fighter’s actual
                                    flight path—the specific hexes the fighter traversed across the
                                    Canyon map during the Movement (Aerospace) Phase—would
                                    need to coincide exactly with the selected attack path in the
                                    diagram for the fighter to make the same attacks.
                                        The controlling player chooses to have his fighter make a
                                    strafing attack, and so he selects five continuous hexes (in this
                                    case, A, B, C, D and E). He also chooses to exploit the Shiva’s
                                    four large pulse lasers. He must make a separate to-hit roll for
                                    every target in the five nominated hexes.
                                        The modified to-hit numbers for all four pulse lasers against
                                    the various units are as follows:
                                        Against the vehicle in Hex A: 7 (4 (base to-hit number) + 4
                                    (strafing attack modifier) + 1 (vehicle movement) –2 (pulse
                                    weapon modifier) = 7). If the attacks succeeds, the player
                                    makes the hit location roll against the front side of the vehicle.
                                        Against the conventional infantry in Hex A: 6 (4 (base to-hit
                                    number) +4 (strafing attack modifier) –2 (pulse weapon modi-
                                    fier) = 6. Infantry have no arcs, and so the attack direction does
                                    not matter for applying damage. However, conventional
                                    infantry take damage from non-infantry units in a unique way,
                                    which would result in only three troopers eliminated per pulse
                                    weapon (doubled to six troopers because the unit is in a clear
                                    hex) that strikes (see Attacks Against Conventional Infantry,
                                    p. 215).
                                        Against the battle armor in Hex B: 10 (4 (base to-hit number)
                                    + 4 (strafing attack modifier) +2 (battle armor’s movement), +1
                                    (attacks against battle armor modifier) +1 (light woods), – 2
                                    (pulse weapon modifier) = 10). Infantry have no arcs, and so
                                    the attack direction does not matter when applying damage.
                                        Against the ’Mech in Hex B: 9 (4 (base to-hit number) + 4
                                    (strafing attack modifier) +2 (’Mech’s movement) +1 (light
                                    woods) –2 (pulse weapon modifier) = 9). If the attack suc-
                                    ceeds, the player makes a hit location roll against the right
                                    side of the ’Mech.
                                        Against the ProtoMech in Hex C: 10 (4 (base to-hit number)
                                    +4 (strafing attack modifier) + 3 (ProtoMech’s movement) +1
                                    (light woods) –2 (pulse weapon modifier) = 10. As hits against
                                    a ProtoMech are not affected by attack direction, the attacking
• air-to-ground attacks diagram •   player would simply roll any successful hits on the ProtoMech
                                    Hit Location Table.
       For the VTOL in Hex D: 9 (4 (base to-hit number), +4
    (strafing attack modifier) +2 (VTOL movement) +1 (air-
    borne target) -2 (pulse weapon modifier)). If the attack
    succeeds, the player makes the hit location rolls against                                                                         INTRODUCTION
    the rear of the VTOL.
       Had the Shiva had been at Altitude 1 (NOE) when
    making the strafing attack, the attack would have missed                                                                          COMPONENTS
    the vehicle in Hex A, as the adjacent Hex 3 would have
    blocked LOS.
                                                                                                                                         PLAYING
Striking                                                                                                                                THE GAME
   An attacker flying at Altitude 5 or lower can make a preci-
sion strike on a single ground unit or ground hex (a building,                 • altitude bombing scatter diagram •
                                                                                                                                        GROUND
woods and so on). The target must lie along the flight path.
                                                                                                                                       MOVEMENT
A strike attack reduces the attacker’s altitude by one, applied
before any attacks are resolved that turn. Units flying at
Altitude 1 (NOE) cannot make strike attacks.                                                                                           AEROSPACE
   The unit may fire one, some or all of its weapons at the                                                                            MOVEMENT
target, except for bombs (which cannot be used in a striking
attack). See Modified To-Hit Number, page 243, to determine
                                                                                                                                        COMBAT
the target number for a striking attack.
   The attacker must make separate to-hit rolls for each
weapon. Apply weapon hits using the standard rules for the
appropriate unit type. Use the column of the appropriate                                                                                  HEAT
Hit Location Table that corresponds to the attack direction,
based on the direction from which the fighter entered
the target’s hex, rather than its position at the end of the                                                                           BUILDINGS
Movement Phase.
   Variable Damage Weapons: Striking attacks made with
these weapons always treat the attack range as short.
                                                                                                                                      PROTOMECHS
        In the strafing example above, the controlling player
     of the Shiva could have decided to make a striking attack                   • dive bombing scatter diagram •

                                                                                            for the engine roll (critical), 7 for the gear (no critical), 11 for the

                                                                                            danger.

                                                                                            goes out of control. Next, he rolls 1D6 to determine the number
                                                                                            of altitudes lost and gets a 2. The aircraft drops to Altitude 1
                                                                                            (NOE), surviving—for now—to continue the fight.

     ILLIUSHIN                                       lifting their SMGs into shooting position. Standard
     TAURIAN CONCORDAT                               procedure: the weapon points where the shooter
      7 OCTOBER 3067                                 is looking.
                                                         The door of the house was three steps up from
              No windows glowed in the target        the sidewalk. The team crossing the street raised
            house as Subaltern Alan Noppadoi         their weapons from under their jackets, all but the
           waved two fingers to the left, then       lead man. He’d stayed with Noppadoi. He moved
           to the right, sending agents to           swiftly forward, then leaped and hit the door with
          cover the dwelling on either side.         both feet. It flew open with a crash as the man fell
          Backup waited on the next block to         to the stoop and rolled out of the way, into the
         catch any runners. The houses on this       bushes by the steps. The other four men rushed
         block faced almost onto the sidewalk,       through the door, one after the other.
        with narrow strips of bushes or vines            Noppadoi continued his stroll, barely reaching
        between them and concrete.                   the steps before the kicker was on his feet, fol-
          He wasn’t an old man at forty-two, but     lowing the rest of the team in. Noppadoi stepped
       rapidly running out of time if he wanted to   through the doorway and stopped, turning
      ever be more than a subaltern of security.     to face the outside. He drew his service pistol,
      Capturing these Blakist spies was his way      clicked the safety off and waited.
     up. So far that year, they had collected more       Behind him, he heard the insertion team
     than twenty of them, across the various         moving through the house, announcing each
    planets that made up the Concordat.              room as clear before they started up the stairs.
       Blakists. Supposed allies.                    They made no effort at quiet. Quiet had ceased to
      He hooked a finger over the whisker on         be part of the plan.
   the side of his headset and pulled the micro-         Above him and to his right, Noppadoi heard
   phone down to his cheek.                          the near-silent scrape of a window opening,
    “Close in. Slow. Quiet.”                         plastic on plastic.
    Noppadoi started walking across the street,          He waited. Then he stepped back outside and
  while the five agents that had remained behind     hopped off the stoop to his right, just in time to
 him ran past him, heading for the front door of     stick the muzzle of the pistol into the face of the
 the target house.                                   man who had jumped from the window above.
  “Go, go, go,” he murmured. No need to shout.           Proof that his anonymous source was reliable—
No need to warn whomever else might be in            at least enough that his score of captured agents
the house.                                           kept growing. As long as he could count on that,
  As the two flanking teams came up on their         Noppadoi didn’t give a damn who his source
destination, they turned in between the houses,      really was.
   “Live or die,” Noppadoi said to the man squatting on the
 ground. “It makes no difference to me.”
    The man didn’t look like he came from the Concordat. He
 looked more like a Magistracy dog. Regardless, as easy to
 shoot him as not. Behind them, the house rumbled and shook
 as the insertion team cleared the upstairs, then clattered back
 downstairs. Agents took the man into custody, cuffing his hands
 behind him.
    Grinding his molar down on the transmit key, Noppadoi
 said, “Bring up the cart. Insertion team, hold the perimeter until
 relieved.”
    His Number Two, Coronet Jaruwan, stopped at his side.
“Should be lots of intel in there. Lots of papers upstairs.”
     “Good.” Noppadoi nodded toward the captive. “What we
 really need is in his head. We’ll get it.”
    Jaruwan grunted. “Then we recondition him, like the rest?”
    Noppadoi nodded. “Make him think he’s a double for FedCom,
 then drop him in the Confederation.”
    Jaruwan grinned. “Break up this stupid alliance with Liao.”
    Report him as a FedCom spy rather than whatever he might
 actually be, Noppadoi thought. Stir up Shraplen’s paranoia some
 more. Move me another step up the chain.

SAMANTHA
TAURUS
TAURIAN CONCORDAT

   Protector Grover Shraplen did not look happy, the demi-
precentor thought. From the information given him by his
superiors at ComStar, Zwick assumed much of Shraplen’s ill
humor stemmed from the mere fact of this intelligence brief-
ing. He also knew that much of it sprang from his presence, as a
representative of ComStar, in Shraplen’s inner sanctum. The
Word of Blake had succeeded brilliantly in turning Grover
Shraplen to their cause.
   Zwick prayed that whatever powers watched over
humanity would not let Shraplen know how desperate
things were for ComStar these days. Desperate enough
that Zwick had to get to Shraplen any way he could.
   What the Taurians called the Secure Room was empty
except for a conference table and eight chairs. The masonry
walls hid a set of tubes that surrounded the room, where
air bubbled through water, creating a white-noise screen
around the entire space. Even the door contained the tubes,
in a closed loop. With a Faraday cage and a double-core
induction electricity supply, no more secure place existed
anywhere on Taurus.
   Brellan Stark, Air Marshal
Senior Grade, was giving
this briefing. Tall, gaunt
and stiff, his informal
attire did not hide a mili-
tary bearing. Stark was
one of the last partisans
ComStar could count on in the Concordat. Given Shraplen’s feel-
ings about Taurian Military Intelligence, getting to him through
them was indeed an act of desperation.
  “I have asked, ah…” Stark turned slightly in his chair to indi-
cate the ComStar officer in the corner. “…Mister Zwick to join us.
We have information on Blakist agents in the Concordat.“ Stark              “As of the last reports from the Concordat, our network remains
cleared his throat. “Brought to us from our friends in ComStar.”          in place, while we feed the Toyama agents to Noppadoi as fast
   Shraplen raised a hand, cutting Stark off. “I assume that              as they arrive.” The young man spoke almost without emotion. A
Subaltern Noppadoi is still capturing Blakist spies, then reporting       good trait. A pity.
them to us as FedCom.”                                                       And if that little farce worked, using Capellan and Canopian citi-
  “Yes. Yes, exactly,” Stark said, visibly shaken at being interrupted.   zens as agents to divert attention away from the Word, so much
   Zwick felt his insides twist. A bad situation to start with, it was    the better.
getting worse by the moment. “You know these agents all claim                The Magistracy was not ready for the Blakists to do much more
to be from the Magistracy or from the Capellan Confederation?”            than attempt to steer them. When the time came, though, True
he said. He must remain calm. Cool. “That’s a lie. They are spies         Believers would take charge in the Magistracy and the Concordat,
from the Word of Blake. They claim to be your friends and yet             not the fools of the Toyama faction.
they send these agents, these provocateurs, to your worlds. The              It could be no other way.
Blakists are not your friends.”
  “Is Noppadoi getting them all?” Shraplen snapped.                       LANTAU ISLAND
   Stark paled. Zwick merely shrugged. “Our intelligence says             SIAN
probably not.”                                                            CAPELLAN CONFEDERATION
   Shraplen nodded.
  “And we know Noppadoi supports Marshal Kithrong,” Stark said,               “Jiang-jun Chi’n.” Chancellor Sun-Tzu Liao glided into the study.
attempting to regain some respect from Shraplen. “We can arrest           His long, painted robe hid the movement of his feet, its long
him most anytime, or transfer him elsewhere.”                             sleeves concealing his hands. “I thank the deputy commander of
   Shraplen shook his head. “No. For the time being, he’s where we        the Maskirovka for coming all this way for our discussions.”
can watch him closely. We know who his subordinates are. We can              Chi’n bowed at the waist until his upper body was parallel to
collect them anytime we want.” Raising a water glass, Shraplen took       the floor. The motion almost made him drop the contents of the
a long drink. “And I will be long dead,” he said, “before Noppadoi        thick leather folder under his arm. Without straightening up, he
commands anything larger than a twenty-man security squad.”               said, “I am honored to serve, Chancellor.” He tried to ease the
   Shraplen did not care, Zwick realized, that the Word of Blake          papers back into the folder.
was sending operatives into the Concordat. Shraplen cared more               Years before, Chi’n had been here, in the main room of the
about a single ambitious intelligence officer than about whatever         Chancellor’s retreat. Then, overstuffed chairs and long plush
the Blakists might do. ComStar is doomed in the Concordat, he             couches had filled the chamber. Now it held a single enameled
thought. There is no hope.                                                chair, the wood of the seat curving up to make armrests, with a
   At least, not as long as men like Shraplen ruled in this realm.        single embroidered pad for comfort. No other furniture broke up
   But if they were swept out of the way …                                the expanse of thick carpeting on the floor. Sun-Tzu Liao’s guests

that marched down from the Stearns Mountains to the wide,                 cross-legged. He waited, carefully not looking the Chancellor in

on the last grass.                                                          “Proceed,” Sun-Tzu said.

  “Have you passed that report on to Precentor Blane, yet?”               double agents for the FedCom, but obviously tampered with,

  “It has been done,” his aide replied.                                   people will make themselves insane trying to determine what

                                                                                                                                               VEHICLES
    The BattleTech Master Unit List (MUL), can be accessed at www.MasterUnitList.info. This ever-growing and free to use
  database enables players to build setting-accurate forces by identifying the ’Mechs, vehicles, fighters, and infantry elements
  covered by the last thirty-five years of Technical Readouts and other sources.                                                               INFANTRY
    Players can browse unit types simply by clicking “Units,” and entering information into the various filter tabs. These filters,
  and the results returned by a search, include:
                                                                                                                                              AEROSPACE
  %     Basic data: weight ranges, BattleTech Battle Values from Total Warfare, the unit’s year of in-universe introduction, and                UNITS
        costs in C-bills—the dominant unit of currency used in the BattleTech setting;
  %     Technology bases: including Inner Sphere, Clan, Mixed, and Primitive;                                                                 CREATING
  %     BattleTech rules level: introductory, standard, advanced, and experimental;                                                          SCENARIOS
  %     Unit Type: e.g. BattleMech, ProtoMech, Combat Vehicle, and so forth;
  %     Source: the original BattleTech book or product the unit appears in, as well as the Record Sheet product containing its
        record sheet;                                                                                                                          PAINTING
                                                                                                                                              MINIATURES
  %     Faction: Lyran, Federated Suns, Draconis Combine, and more;
  %     Unit Sub-Types: VTOL, OmniMech, etc.;
  %     Production and Availability Eras: from the Age of War through the Dark Age setting.
                                                                                                                                                INDEX

     By clicking on the name of a listed unit, the user can view more stats for it, including links to images of the unit courtesy
  of CamoSpecs Online, and online ordering options to obtain the appropriate miniature via Iron Wind Metals.
     Armed with this tool, players can access the broad selection of playable units and begin building custom armies suitable
   for any era or faction in the official BattleTech canon.

                                                                                                                                                                259
   Jumping: Jumping over hexes does not reveal units hidden in
them. If jumping into a hex would reveal a unit hidden there, the       DFDF
jumping unit still lands in that hex, causing a domino effect (see
p. 152).
   Pointblank Shots from Hidden Units: A hidden unit revealed
by enemy movement may immediately make a special pointblank
weapon attack against the unit that revealed it. It may first torso
twist or rotate its turret, and uses a Range of 1. Ignore Target
Number modifiers for movement or terrain. Any damage dealt and
Piloting Skill Rolls required take effect immediately. If the target
unit is still capable of moving, has MP remaining, and isn’t jumping,
it may continue its move after the attack. If the damage from a
pointblank shot triggers a forced withdrawal and the damaged
unit has MP remaining, its forced withdrawal begins immediately.
   If an enemy unit skids, jumps, or is displaced into a hidden              With the Blakist’s violation of Clan honor, Clan Steel Viper surrounds a lone ’Mech.
unit, any pointblank shot made occurs before any collision or
displacement.

                                                                                   CHASE
                                                                                      In a chase scenario, the attacker’s forces must race across the
                                                                                   map to reach their DropShip before a larger force of pursuing
   Defender                                                                        defenders can reach them. Chase scenarios use the same rules
   3 points for each attacking unit destroyed or crippled                          and victory conditions as breakthrough scenarios, with the fol-
   2 points for each attacking unit forced to retreat off the map                  lowing exceptions.
                                                                                     The defending player does not deploy his forces until Turn 2.
                                                                                   During Turn 1, attacking units may enter and move unopposed per
                                                                                   standard rules. The defending units enter during the Movement
Force Composition                                                                  Phase of Turn 2, from the same map edge as the attacking units.
  Both sides should have the same number of units.
  Battle Value: If using the BV system, each side should be worth                  Force Composition
the same number of points.                                                           Set the defender’s force at twice the size of the attacking force.
                                                                                   For example, if the attackers form a single lance, the defending
Victory Conditions                                                                 force should contain two lances.
   The scenario ends when all the defending units have been                           Battle Value: Under the BV system, the defending force should
destroyed or retreated off the map, or when all attacking units                    be worth twice the point value of the attacker’s force.
have retreated, been destroyed or been crippled. For this type
of scenario, units become crippled when they lose one or more
legs or their gyros are destroyed. Attacking units may intention-
ally exit the map only at their home edge; a unit exiting from any                   SELECTING MAPSHEETS
other edge has retreated.
                                                                                     After selecting a scenario type, determine the terrain by select-
                                                                                   ing mapsheets. Certain scenarios require specific types of map-
                                                                           DFDF
                                                                                   sheets according to their descriptions. In most scenarios, however,
                                                                                   players can simply select whichever mapsheets they like or make
                                                                                   random dice rolls and consult the appropriate mapsheet tables.
                                                                                     Before selecting mapsheets, determine how many you want
                                                                                   to use. For most BattleTech play, an appropriate ratio is one map-
                                                                                   sheet for every four units. For example, a scenario that pits one
                                                                                   lance of attackers against one lance of defenders (eight units
                                                                                   total) works best with two mapsheets. A scenario that pits one
                                                                                   company of attackers against a company of defenders (twenty-
                                                                                   four total units) works best with six mapsheets. Different sce-
                                                                                   narios may work best with other mapsheet/unit ratios, so check
                                                                                   the scenario type description.

                                                                                   USING THE MAPSHEET TABLES
                                                                                      Players may either select a terrain type and then roll 1D6 to
        A Castillian Brigada Vulcan and Pegasus on patrol in the Deep Periphery.   determine each map used, or roll 2D6 to determine the terrain type
                                                                                   for the scenario and then roll 1D6 to determine the specific maps.

   Before play begins, one player rolls 2D6. The result indicates which
terrain table is used. For example, on a result of 7, use the Flatlands
Terrain Table; on a result of 6, the Hill Terrain Table; and so on. Players
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       UNIT GENERATION
may simply select a specific Terrain Table and skip this step.                                                                                                EXPERIENCE RATING AND SKILLS                                         INFANTRY
                                                                                                  After determining the units employed by each force, the
                                                                                               players determine their forces’ experience ratings: Green,
                                                                                                                                                                   AEROSPACE
                                                                                               Regular, Veteran or Elite. If the players are using elements of
                                                                                                                                                                     UNITS
                                                                                               specific units, they can consult the appropriate field manual.
                                                                                               Alternatively, the players may simply set any experience
                                                                                               ratings they wish, or use the Random Experience Rating Table.       CREATING
                                                                                               (Players can help ensure a balanced battle by setting the same     SCENARIOS
                                                                                               level for both forces.)
                                                                                                  After determining his forces’ experience, each player             PAINTING
                                                                                               determines the Gunnery and Piloting skills of his warriors.         MINIATURES
                                                                                               If the players agree, they can simply assign all warriors the
                                                                                               standard starting skill ratings (see p. 39). Alternatively, each
                                                                                               player may use the Random Skills Table to randomly deter-             INDEX
                                                                                               mine skill ratings. To do so, each player makes two 1D6 rolls
                                                                                               for each warrior in his force. The first die roll result deter-
                                                                                               mines the MechWarrior’s Piloting Skill; the second, his or her
                                                                                               Gunnery Skill.
DFDF                                                                                             For Clan MechWarriors, add 2 to each roll result; subtract 2
                                                                                               for Clan combat vehicle warriors.

                                                                     Clan honor codes fall into four categories, each dealing with
     4–5           1–2    0       —                6        4
                                                                  a different part of combat: batchall, zellbrigen, physical attacks
     6–7           3–4   1–2       0               5        4     and retreat. Because the batchall (bidding) takes place before
                                                                  the scenario begins, guidelines for its use appear on page 266.          HEAT
      8            5–6   3–4      1–2              4        3
                                                                  Within each of the other three categories, a number from 1 to
     —             7–8   5–6      3–4              3        2     4 represents the level of commitment to Clan honor. Honor
     —             —     7–8      5–6              2        1     Level 1 means strict conformity to the Clan codes; Level 2 and        BUILDINGS
     —             —     —        7–8              1        0     3 require conformity only under certain circumstances; and
                                                                  Level 4 throws Clan honor out the airlock.
                                                                     Normally, a fighting force’s overall commitment to Clan           PROTOMECHS
                                                                  honor is rated at Level 1, 2, 3 or 4. However, scenarios may

   CLAN HONOR INTERPRETATION TABLE                                                          duel. A duel ends when one combatant is destroyed, disabled or
