                                                                                               At Honor Level 1, the Clan warrior upholds all the rules of
         Clan Name                 Pre-Invasion                 Post-Invasion               dueling regardless of his opponent’s actions.
       Blood Spirit                   Strict                  Opportunistic                    At Honor Level 2, the Clan warrior follows the rules of dueling
       Cloud Cobra              Opportunistic                 Opportunistic                 until the Inner Sphere side takes an action that violates the Clan
                                                                                            honor codes (a third party interfering, a unit involved in one
          Coyote                      Strict                        Strict                  duel firing on a ’Mech involved in another duel, and so on). If
     Diamond Shark                   Liberal                       Liberal                  this happens, the duel immediately degenerates into a free-for-
       Fire Mandrill            Varies (Strict)*                Varies                      all. During the Weapon Attack Phase, if a player declares that his
                                                            (Opportunistic)*                unit will fire on a target already involved in a duel, any Clan player
                                                                                            whose declaration of zellbrigen follows the Inner sphere player’s
        Ghost Bear                    Strict                       Liberal
                                                                                            declaration of a “dishonorable attack” may attack that Inner
    Goliath Scorpion                  Strict                        Strict                  Sphere ’Mech without regard for honor rules. Only the violator
       Hell’s Horses            Opportunistic                 Opportunistic                 may be attacked in this way.
                                                                                               At Honor Level 3, any infringement of Clan honor in the dueling
        Ice Hellion             Opportunistic                 Opportunistic
                                                                                            rules renders the entire ritual of zellbrigen null and void, leaving
       Jade Falcon                    Strict                  Opportunistic                 the Clan ’Mechs free to attack any enemy unit without restraint
         Nova Cat               Opportunistic                 Opportunistic                 for the remainder of the game. At Honor Level 4, dueling rules do
                                                                                            not apply.
       Snow Raven               Opportunistic                 Opportunistic
                                                                                               Using zellbrigen in a game requires a certain degree of coop-
        Star Adder                    Strict                  Opportunistic                 eration between players. A crafty Inner sphere player can exploit
        Steel Viper                   Strict                        Strict                  the rules of engagement to deny the Clan player any targets. Not
                                                                                            only is this grossly unfair, but it is also inaccurate in terms of the
            Wolf                     Liberal                  Opportunistic
                                                                                            BattleTech universe (plus, it’s not much fun for the Clan player).

                                                                       David L. McCulloch




COCHRAN VALLEY                                                        The closest law was in Fort McNeil, and Sheriff Brandon
RANDALL’S REGRET                                                   packed nothing more lethal than a gopher gun. The nearest
THE PERIPHERY                                                      BattleMech was way over in Silverpeak. Marshal Hancock and his
11 APRIL 3065                                                      deputies could be here in three weeks—perhaps two with luck.
                                                                      More hammering pounded at Garret’s throbbing head. After
   Tendrils of black smoke curled into the chill azure of the      splashing more water on his unshaven face, he pulled on a shirt
morning sky—hazy tombstones around which blue-tailed               of green homespun wool as he walked across the yard. Echoes
buzzards circled. The Jameson place had been hit two days ago,     from two nights before were everywhere he looked. A low-built
the third ranch to be raided in the past week. The hammering       house with thick walls that was supposed to keep storm and
started up again, making Mathew Garret wince as he scanned         bandit at bay. Weighty tiles of red clay on the roof. A veranda
the horizon with blood-shot blue eyes. Stooping at the rough-      to shade the women from the burning sun as they sewed and
hewn water trough, he splashed icy water over sandy hair and a     wove. The barn Garret and his brothers had built three years ago.
face weathered by years toiling under the blowtorch sun.           The Jamesons had helped. A string of outbuildings arranged in
   Perhaps it had been a mistake to drink quite so much of         a defensible square on a solid base of ferrocrete to keep the lion
cousin Errol’s home-brewed liquor last night.                      moles—burrowing predators colloquially known as “gophers”—
   The night before, Garret had been creeping through Satan’s      at bay.
ruddy moonlight. The silvery face of Gabriel was cresting the         Leaning on a doorpost that had taken two days to carve
horizon when he reached the smoking ruins of the Jameson           from the trunk of a titan palm, Garret observed the frenzied
ranch. He’d smelled the stink of death everywhere. Dead cattle.    activity inside. Errol couldn’t read too well, but the skinny man
Dead pigs. Dead nurfers. Dead people. Garret had not lingered      was exceptionally skilled with his hands. The air was thick with
in that charnel house. The main building had been blasted apart.   the odor of coolant and spirits, cut with the ozone tang of an
Giant footprints traced a path of destruction. The feed store he   arc-welder. Errol had completed mating a boxy missile launcher
had helped the Jamesons erect two summers ago was unrec-           to the chassis of Garret’s CattleMaster—a tired and battered
ognizable. The barn that had served as a DropShip in childhood     IndustrialMech brought to the Regret by Randall’s original colo-
games was a smoldering pile of wreckage.                           nists fleeing the chaos of the Succession Wars. Now Errol’s atten-
   It looked like the raiders had at least one ‘Mech, an armed     tion had turned to the squat form of the Kerns’ faded yellow
IndustrialMech or maybe a BattleMech. Not good.                    HarvesterMech. He was working to dismount a wide-throated
                                                                   combine unit from the front of the quadruped’s chassis.
   “How’s it going?” Garret looked over to where Jonathan Kerns
 was propping up the other doorpost, shouting to be heard.
   “Your cousin says he reckons he can get those guns onto
 our Harvester.” Jonathan’s face bore the scars of a run-in
 with a lion mole five summers back. Garret looked at the
 mismatched machine guns leaning against the barn’s
 rough-plank wall. He didn’t know where his eternally grease-
 smeared cousin had come by all these weapons. Right now
 he wasn’t going to ask.
    With a hollow clanging that drove spikes of pain through
 Garret’s brain, the combine unit dropped away at last. Errol
 turned, his grin of triumph shockingly white against a wide,
 grimy face. Garret gestured toward the guns. “How long do
 you reckon?”
    Errol glanced over his shoulder at the IndustrialMech he
 was tearing apart. “Say three or four hours. More time and I
 could maybe tack on some metal plating around the cockpit.
 Maybe give Jonathan here a might bit more protection.” He
 flashed another grin at Kerns. “Don’t think they maybe put
 this critter together with combat in mind.”
   “I’d appreciate it.” Kerns smiled back, then looked at Garret.
“What do you figure we’re looking at? How many gun hands?
 How many ’Mechs?”
   “Hard to say. They made a real mess of the Jamesons’.”
 Garret thought for a moment. “Nobody’s seen any
 BattleMechs wandering around. Reckon they must be holing
 up somewhere out of sight. About the only place you can
 hide something that big would be over by Cedar
 Creek.” He nodded toward the foothills in the
 west. “Reckon I’ll go have a look.”




   No doubt about it. Things
were getting more dangerous by
the day. Ringo sat cross-legged on
the foot of his faithful Bessie, back
against the cool armor of her right leg.
The tree-dotted hills offered little cover from the elements,
but the winding canyons made a perfect place to plan his
next move without getting bothered by the locals. It had
not rained for several days, and the air was thick with the
scent of tree resin and nose-tingling pollen. He scratched
absently at a scraggly ginger beard that had been
growing since he first set foot on the sorriest excuse for
a planet it had ever been his misfortune to see.
   He had picked off all the easy marks. Outlying farms
and ranches that lacked support from the others. Worse,
by now the locals would have some idea what they were
up against—probably gone hollering for help to Silverpeak
and Marshal stinking Gregor God-damned Hancock.
   Ringo spat on the mossy ground.
   Hancock had run Bessie and him out of Southfield, and
now word was that the grizzled Marshal was none too happy
about Ringo shooting out some of his right leg actuators.
Spare parts were hard to come by all the way out here—
they’d cost a body an arm and a leg. No, he didn’t want
to cross trails with Hancock and his deputies again.
   Tugging at the collar of his cooling vest, Ringo wondered            More than two hours’ hard riding had carried Garret to
whether he should ditch the bulky garment. It had quit on            where the hills broke like a sea of stone on the rocky shore of
him three weeks ago, and only the protection offered by              the river valley’s edge. Bringing a second nurfer had allowed
the rough ballistic cloth armor covering had prevented him           him to switch mounts every few kilometers and make good
from throwing it away on the spot. Ammo was becoming a               time, but the ride had left his rawhide coat and knee-length
problem, too. The local dirt farmers made a good enough              boots coated in dust. Scaly, ugly and smelly, the hexapedal
source for food, drink, clothing, women and a little coin if he      nurfers were native to the Regret. Once domesticated, they
was lucky. But Ringo was barely scratching a living out here         had proven to be excellent draft animals, despite a strangely
in the boonies. He needed one good caper. One big payoff,            jointed backbone that gave them a swaying side-to-side
and Bessie and he could get themselves off this rock. Maybe          gait guaranteed to invoke nausea (or worse) in a novice
he could even get Bessie a much-needed refit. She’d been             rider. Resting the barrel of his wide-bore gopher gun at the
barely operational when he won her in that smoky poker               ready across the pommel of his saddle, Garret threaded his
game on Tortuga. To think! His mother had said he’d never            way between thick gray cedar trunks and under the sprawl-
amount to anything. But he’d proved her wrong. If only she           ing canopy of titan palms as he worked his way into the hills,
could see him (wherever she was now). He was a high and              following the familiar game trails to the accompaniment of
mighty MechWarrior now! Not some two-bit stick-up artist             chattering stone squirrels.
or petty thief.                                                         His quarry proved ridiculously easy to find. Garret was sure
   Now all he had to do was get himself off this accursed rock       even a deaf man could have heard the rhythmic thumping of
to someplace where the real action was.                             ‘Mech footfalls.
   Randall’s Regret. Ringo snorted in disgust. Randall                  Beneath the winding trail Garret was following, a lone
(whoever the hell he was) doubtless regretted even landing           BattleMech plodded laboriously along a narrow gully, follow-
on the Blake-forsaken place. Ringo certainly did. Damned             ing the bed of a dried-out wash. Half-obscured by the cedars,
system wasn’t even on the charts. Maybe he should’ve lis-            the outline of the machine’s bulbous body was broken up
tened to that Precentor back on Herotitus. The Robes had             with a peeling gray-and-tan camouflage paint scheme—and
been scurrying around the back streets like mice in a cheese         by the cargo nets stuffed with a bizarre collection of items.
factory. Looking for a few good men (and women), they’d              Mixed in with the crates and barrels, Garret could see farm
said. Good pay, they’d said. Chance to be part of something          implements, furniture, even a glossy black stove that had
special, they’d said. Ringo had always stayed well clear of          been Mrs. Jameson’s pride and joy.
religion. It had never helped his mother and sisters. Weekly            He was unfamiliar with the design—Garret was no expert
visits to the rickety old church had certainly never done him        when it came to BattleMechs. Jack would know, perhaps.
or his family a single shred of good. Some of the stories about      His sister’s ten-year-old son was fanatical about everything
the toaster-worshipers had made him a might bit nervous,             connected with the combat versions of the IndustrialMechs
though. But now that he was stuck on an elbow of a planet,           the colonists had brought with them. This machine lacked
signing on with the Wobbies looked like a much more attrac-          the humanoid profile of the half a dozen BattleMechs he
tive proposition.                                                    had ever seen. It moved slowly, kicking up dust from the dry
   Pulling a scrap of paper from the breast pocket of his            wash. Garret cussed. Slow meant big. Perhaps too big for him
cooling vest, Ringo unfolded the rough map he had sketched           to deal with, even with all his neighbors along for the ride.
out during the week he’d spent scouting out the region. The          The only good thing was that the BattleMech appeared to be
three farmsteads he had already visited were spread out along        alone. No vehicles or hordes of gun-toting bandits on foot.
the edge of a river valley fertile enough to attract enough             Garret considered taking a shot at the ’Mech, but then
families to create a self-sufficient community. After last night,    thought better of it. His gun had the stopping power to put
those farmers would probably expect him to strike further            down a three-meter hexapedal lion mole at close range, but
north. Probably some hotheads would have gotten the idea of          it’d hardly scratch the paint on that manmade monster out

giving him a bad case of lead poisoning or (if he were really        would tell everyone where the BattleMech was?
unlucky) fitting him for an uncomfortably tight rope necktie.           Garret spat another curse into the afternoon heat. He

sulking since the adults had appropriated it. Flicking through            her was Emma, holding tight to young James to keep him out
the pages, Garret jabbing a finger at the book. “That’s it.”              from under the crushing ‘Mech feet. He was too far away to see
   Jonathan Kerns took the readout. “An Imp? One hundred tons.”           their faces clearly. That made it a bit easier. “Let’s go kick some!”
He glanced at the others as they gathered round to read. “PPCs.              Throttling up to walking speed, Garret led them into the west
Lasers. Missiles. You think we can stop this thing? All we’ve got         to fight for their home.
here are two IndustrialMechs and a bunch of rifles. We haven’t
got everybody organized yet.”
  “I don’t see that we have any choice, Jon. If we run for it now,           Ringo yawned and stretched mightily before tossing another

                                                                                                                                                 281
   For additional mottling, mix some Vallejo
#816 with glaze medium. Carefully add
areas of darker gray in small angular pat-
terns to further discolor the overall gray.

Brown Body (BM-8, BA-6)
  Paint all areas that you’d like to be brown
with GW Bestial Brown. Use a fine-tipped 0
brush and take care to “keep it in the lines.”

Brown Highlights (BM-9, BA-7)
  Thin some GW Snakebite Leather with
water and make random triangular patterns
with a 00 brush. Then mix a little Vallejo       BattleMaster-1   BattleMaster-5    BattleMaster-9
white with the Snakebite Leather and
carefully paint the highlights (sharp upper
edges and details).

Yellow Highlights (BM-10, BA-8)
   Paint all areas intended to be warning
stripes with GW Golden Yellow. You can
highlight this yellow by adding a little white
to the mix and using a 00 brush.

Metallic Touches (BM-11, BA-8)
  Paint all areas intended to look metallic
with GW Boltgun Metal using a 00 brush.

Black Wash (BM-12, BA-9)
   At this point, apply a targeted black wash    BattleMaster-2    BattleMaster-6   BattleMaster-10
(also referred to as black lining). The most
common method is to thin black paint with
water and apply it only to deep engraved
details or joints to create shadow. You can
also achieve this by adding glaze medium
to black or using future floor finish with
black. I used a mix of turpentine and stan-
dard black oil paint for this miniature. Be
warned—turpentine plus oil paint is not
only smelly, it’s flammable and toxic, so
take precautions! I prefer this type of wash
because it works so well on an acrylic base.
Use a 5/0 or 000 brush to carefully create
shadows and panel lines. The mix will flow,
so take care not to apply too much at a time.    BattleMaster-3   BattleMaster-7    BattleMaster-11

Finishing Touches (BM-13, BA-10 Final)
   In this stage, finishing touches are added
to the miniature. Paint the black checkers
on the leg, black stripes on the warning
panels, black canopy and numbers with
appropriate brushes. The checks can be
traced out using a sharpened mechanical
pencil to make painting easier. You can use
decals from Fighting Piranha Graphics for
unit insignia, and for numbers and checks
as well. The unit insignia, numbers and
checks on the BattleMaster pictured were
hand-painted.

                                                 BattleMaster-4   BattleMaster-8    BattleMaster-12
Style the Base (BM-Final, BA-10 Final)
   I finished the base in the following
manner. First, I painted the edges
painted black. Then I painted the top                                                                                       INTRODUCTION
Bestial Brown. Once dry, I painted the
top again with a white glue and water
mix. While the glue mix was wet, I                                                                                          COMPONENTS
dropped a few small rocks in place,
then added fine ballast around these
rocks. Finally, I covered the base in                                                                                          PLAYING
fine sand and then turned it to dump                 BattleArmor-1   BattleArmor-4                          BattleArmor-7
                                                                                                                              THE GAME
off the excess. This combination looks
realistic and didn’t need to be painted.
                                                                                                                              GROUND

applied another layer of white glue
where I wanted the flock to be, then
sprinkled it with flock.                                                                                                     AEROSPACE

line the entire tank.

                                                                         USING SPRAY PRIMER
PAINTS AND BRUSHES                                                         Primers are usually available in brush-on and spray types.
   Your paints and brushes are the most important materials and          Spraying produces thinner and more even undercoats than
tools you’ll be using, so it’s worth saying a little more about them.    brushing. Here are a few tips to remember when spraying:
   Though other types of paints are available, most modelers favor
water-based—or acrylic—paints. Acrylics dry much more quickly            •    Always spray in a well-ventilated area. You can get very
than most other types of paints and produce “cleaner” colors. And             sick if you breathe in fumes from the spray can, so it’s best to
because they are water-based, you can use water to clean your                 spray outside if possible. Some painters spray in garages or
brushes and thin your paints. Acrylics are waterproof once they               sheds. If you spray in an enclosed area, keep the doors and
dry, and they also tend to be kinder to your brushes than other               windows open.
types of paints. Acrylic glaze medium can be used to extend              •    Use a spray booth. Using a spray booth will keep the paint
the drying time of acrylic paints and makes an excellent mixing               from getting on things you don’t want painted. You can
medium for washes. Future floor finish also works for mixing and              make a spray booth out of a cardboard box. Simply remove
washes, but leaves a glossy sheen.                                            the flaps from the top and set the box on its side. Place the
   Acrylics come in a wide variety of colors from many manufac-               miniatures inside the box. The excess paint that misses the
turers. For this guide, I used only three brands of paint, each of            miniatures when you spray will stay inside the box.
which is described below. Feel free to try any acrylic paints and        •    Check with your parents, your spouse or whoever is in
find what works best for you.                                                 charge before spraying. Make sure the place where you’ll
                                                                              be spraying is okay.
•    Vallejo makes two ranges of paints, model color and game            •    Read the instructions on the can. Ask for help if you don’t
     color. Model color covers better and the colors are more                 understand the instructions on the can. When you’re set up
     military-looking. Game color is vibrant and excellent for                and ready to start, hold the can about 10–12 inches away
     blending. This manufacturer also makes additional items like             from the miniatures and spray with light, smooth strokes.
     glaze medium, slow dry and other useful additives for acrylics.          Don’t try to drench them in one fell swoop! Once this first
•    Games Workshops paints are rich in color but dry out quickly.            coat has dried, turn the miniatures around and spray the
     The inks are fantastic but can stain paint, so it’s best to thin         backs. You can use brush-on primer to touch up any small
     them down. These mix well with most other paints.                        areas the spray missed.
•    Iron Wind Paints are excellent for dry brushing. IWM Ivory is
     one of the most useful paints I have ever worked with, and is       BASIC TECHNIQUES
     my primary weathering paint for dry brushing.                          Here are some common painting techniques and terms. Most
                                                                         of them are used in the examples provided in this guide.
   Brushes can be the most expensive tools you use, so be sure to
take good care of them. Wash them in warm soapy water or use             Washes
brush soap between colors, and do not leave them in the water               A wash is a thin layer of color, usually applied on top of another
cup or the bristles will curl. The more a brush is used, the better it   color that has already dried. The wash is semi-transparent, so that
becomes. You can paint a very fine line with a well cared for 0 brush.   the base color under it still shows through. For example, a red
   Most brushes used for this guide were Windsor and Newton Series       wash applied over a black base color will produce a dark red tone.
7. Other brushes should work fine. Like paints, the choice of brush      The black base provides the darkness, while the wash adds color
should be up to the painter. Store them with bristles facing up.         to the final tone. A dark wash may also be applied to a lighter
                                                                         base color. In this case, the thin wash color will seep into the
HOBBY KNIFE SAFETY TIPS                                                  deeper details of the painted section, accentuating the surface
    Hobby knives are very sharp, and it’s easy to give yourself a bad    texture of the miniature.
cut if you’re not careful. To avoid accidents, follow the safety tips.      The simplest way to create a wash is by thinning paint with
If you’ve never used a hobby knife before, ask for some help.            water. The consistency of the wash—how thin or thick it is—
                                                                         determines how much of the base color shows through. The
•    ALWAYS cut AWAY from yourself! It may seem easier to                consistency of a wash will also affect how easily the wash flows,
     scrape the blade back toward your thumb, but sooner or              or bleeds.
     later your blade will slip and you’ll give yourself a deep cut.        To apply a wash, first mix a little black paint into a puddle of
                                                                         the base coat color. Add water to this puddle until it turns semi-
•    Always cover the blade and store your hobby knife when              transparent. Now you have a choice: you can do a coverage wash
     you’re done using it. Don’t leave the blade exposed, and            or a detail wash. A coverage wash will often darken, or stain, the
     don’t leave the knife laying on your work surface. It’s too         basecoat. A coverage wash is good to use before you block in the
     easy to forget it’s there, and you’re likely to lean onto the       panels or dry-brush the miniature. For detail washing, you use a
     blade or knock it off the work surface.                             fine-tipped brush such as a 0 or 5/0 to paint the wash into panel
 lines, crevices and details. Detail washing is similar to blacklin-             with less pressure than the last one. This technique is easy
 ing/outlining. Detail washing works best after dry brushing                     to learn but difficult to master. Practice and do what you are
 but may be used before. Another use for detail washing is to                    comfortable with.
“clean up” panels and large areas that have been dry-brushed.                       Experiment by leaving differing amounts of paint on the          INTRODUCTION
 Refer to the banding technique, but use a wash that is the                      bristles and altering the amount of pressure you apply with the
 same as the base coat.                                                          brush. You’ll soon see what looks right and what looks messy.
    Vary the consistency of your washes, and watch how the                                                                                           COMPONENTS
 wash affects the final color.                                                   Panel Painting and Blending
                                                                                    Panel painting and blending are two advanced techniques

highlights, to a section already painted. This method is used                    panel painting technique when adding our middle tones.
to apply colors to the raised surfaces of the miniature, rather                     When panel painting or blending a section of a miniature,

than the recessed details. Dry brushing can be hard on the                       first paint the entire section a darker shade of the base color;

bristles of a brush, so use your roughest brush (in this case,                   a coverage wash will suffice for this purpose. After this coat of
the Size 2 brush) or an old/cheap brush. Many painters                           paint or wash has dried, paint each panel with the base color.
reserve specific brushes for dry brushing. Size 8 is an example                  Be sure to use a size 0 brush for this so you get a clean, even      AEROSPACE
of a dedicated dry brush.                                                        coat on each panel. If you make a mistake and paint outside          MOVEMENT

be a good idea to let the paint sit for a few minutes, giving it                    If you are blending, you can paint onto the base color

  Panel painting and blending can be a bit tricky, so it’s a good
idea to practice on an old miniature before you try these tech-
niques on your favorites. In addition to changing the tone of each
                                                                                    FINISHING TOUCHES
successive layer, you can change the consistency of the paint by              Unless you plan to lock your miniature away in a glass display
mixing in a bit of water to thin it.                                        case, you should probably finish it with a coat of protective
                                                                            varnish or sealer. The varnish will enhance and protect the paint
Blacklining                                                                 job (If you use an unvarnished miniature in games, parts of the
   Blacklining requires a steady hand and fine brush control, so            paint will eventually start rubbing away).
you may want to wait to try this technique until you’ve had a                 You can find protective varnish at your local hobby shop,
bit more experience. Blacklining consists of painting fine lines            available in brush-on or spray varieties. If you plan to use spray
between different parts of the miniature to separate one area               varnish, be sure to apply it in a well-ventilated area. For best
from another. Often this is done as one of the last jobs on the             results, tell your hobby dealer what kind of paints you used
figure. Many modelers use black to do their outlining (hence the            on your miniature (acrylic paints, in this case) and ask him to
term blacklining), but you can also use dark grays and browns.              recommend a varnish or sealer formulated to work with that
   When blacklining, the consistency of the paint is the most               type of paint. Acrylic brush-on lacquers are the safest and can
important factor. Generally, the paint must be thinned a little so          be found at local game stores.
that it flows freely and can be applied in thin lines. If the paint
is too thin, however, it will run, making it difficult to paint con-        MISTAKES
trolled lines. Acrylic inks and fine permanent ink pens also work              Even the best figure painters in the world make the occasional
well for blacklining. Inks can be purchased at local hobby stores;          blunder, and everyone makes mistakes when they’re first paint-
most paint lines have a selection of inks. Art and craft stores also        ing. Don’t let mistakes discourage you. Mistakes are an important
carry artists’ ink. If you use this ink, it’s advisable to thin it before   way of learning, and sometimes a mistake may look better than
applying it to the miniature. Ultra-fine 005 permanent pens can             what you intended to paint or give you an idea for something you
be found at art and/or craft stores and come in a variety of colors.        wouldn’t have thought of otherwise!
                                                                               Also, keep in mind that you can fix most mistakes by painting
Adding Details                                                              over them. Most of your mistakes will probably involve getting
    Details are the really small areas and features of the miniature,       paint from one part of the miniature onto an area where you
such as the cockpit, gun and jump jet ports. Details also include           don’t want it. If you get a bit of light-colored paint on a dark area,
any “extras” that don’t show on the unpainted miniature, such as            you can fix it by painting it over with a darker color. If you get a
divisional and unit insignias, rank insignias and “kill” markers.           bit of dark paint on a light area, you may need to paint over the
    Usually, details are added after all the other painting is finished.    dark color with a little white before retouching the area with the
Painting details requires a steady hand and fine brush control, so          original light color.
it’s best for beginners to add only a few. You can also add details            Most often, you’ll make mistakes when you’re getting tired. This
using commercially available decals. Licensed BattleTech decals             is a sign to stop painting and start fresh the next day or evening.
are available online at http://fightingpirannhagraphics.com/.               Otherwise, you’re likely to make more mistakes and get frustrated,
                                                                            which is the last thing you want. Painting miniatures is supposed
                                                                            to be fun; don’t get so caught up in the challenge of the work that
                                                                            you forget about the fun part.
DFDF
                                                                            A FINAL NOTE
                                                                                Hopefully, this section offers you enough guidance to paint
                                                                            some cool miniatures. However, you don’t need to rely on this
                                                                            information alone. Ask other painters how they like to paint their
                                                                            miniatures. Ask the people at your local hobby shop how they do
                                                                            it. Most people will be happy to talk about their hobby. Online
                                                                            BattleTech forums often have good tips and discussions about
                                                                            painting and modeling, and most painters online are happy to
                                                                            help as well.
                                                                                As you continue to paint miniatures, you’ll get better and better
                                                                            at it. You’ll probably learn new techniques and new skills. Pretty
                                                                            soon, people will be asking you for advice. Just remember to be
                                                                            patient. You may not be the greatest painter when you start, but
                                                                            if you keep at it you’ll get better.




                    SDR-9K Venom (un-identified mercenary)

by Ray Arrastia                                                             paneling needs sculpting and minor details should be added.            INTRODUCTION
   Kitbashing is a term coined by model kit builders to
describe the process of building a model with altered or addi-              Land Train Cargo Transport (LCT)
tional pieces (including parts from multiple kits) to achieve a                This kitbash is loosely based on the vehicle by the same            COMPONENTS
completely different model than the original. The term applies              name from TRO:VA. The compact design of the Swift Wind
equally well to the miniature gaming hobby, and to BattleTech               scout car [IWM stock # 20-306] can be used as the basis for the
in particular. Kitbashing techniques are invaluable for creat-              six-wheeled pod-like cars of the LCT. For the three cargo pods,           PLAYING
ing ’Mech and vehicle variants not currently in production, as              we can remove the crew compartments, detach the wheels                   THE GAME
well as miniatures of your own design. We’ll be looking at a                from the discarded crew segments and affix them to the
few different projects as we go along to help illustrate the                cargo pods. A Swift Wind can stand in for the LCT’s crew car
                                                                                                                                                     GROUND
various stages and skills involved in kitbashing.                           with a minor modification—adding an extra pair of wheels to             MOVEMENT
                                                                            bring its total to eight.

   Before you grab any tools and start cutting up miniatures,
                                                                                                                                                    MOVEMENT
you need a concept and a plan of action. Decide what your

parts, tools and techniques are necessary to complete the                                                                                            COMBAT
project. Below are a few sample kitbashing projects that we’ll
describe throughout this section. Additional details and tech-
niques for these projects will appear in later sections.

  All OmniMechs have several standard configurations, and the             In kitbashing, the old
Widowmaker is among the more unique configurations for the            adage, “use the right tools
Daishi (Dire Wolf) OmniMech [IWM stock # 20-607RE]. Kitbashing a      for the right job” proves true.
Widowmaker may pose a bit of a challenge, but the modular nature      For minor work you can get
of OmniMech weaponry and design principles makes project              away with almost no tools at
planning and acquisition of parts relatively simple. The current      all—but if you plan on lots of
arm weapons should be replaced, the left top torso filled to cover    kitbashing or intricate work,
the unneeded SRM slot, the right torso filled out and an AC/20        it’s a good idea to expand
installed, medium lasers positioned on either side of the nose, and   the collection of tools at your
the chin box modified or replaced in order to house a small laser.    disposal. Always keep in mind
                                                                      the need to take proper safety
                                                                      precautions.

                                                                      Knives and Saws                         Cutting Tools
                                                                         A hobby knife with an inter-
                                                                      changeable blade is an ideal
                                                                      kitbashing tool. Besides minor
                                                                      cutting, some blade replace-
                                                                      ments are suitable for sawing,
                                                                      and a few are even helpful
                                                                      when sculpting small details.
                                                                      Hobby knives are extremely
                                                                      sharp; always cut away from
                                                                      yourself and use caution
                                                                      when cutting. Some hobby
                                                                      saws are good for cutting
                                                                      styrene and balsa wood, and
                                                                      jeweler’s saws allow for very
                                                                      fine cutting. A pair of hobby
                                                                      clippers is also useful for
                                                                      cleanly removing items from
                                                                      sprues and for cutting though
                                                                      hunks of unwanted material.
                     CAD Rendering of Daishi Widowmaker
                                                                      Files and Sandpaper
                                                                          Use jeweler and needle files
                                                                      for metal or for hard-to-reach
                                                                      areas; sandpaper is more useful             Files
                                                                      for larger surfaces or non-
                                                                      metallic items. Use finer (high-
                                                                      grit) sandpaper to smooth sur-
                                                                      faces and clean up any imper-
                                                                      fections created by rougher
                                                                      filing or sanding. When doing
                                                                      heavy filing or sanding, wear a
                                                                      protective mask.

                                                                      Pin Vise
                                                                        The old stand-by tool for
                                                                      drilling into plastic or metal
                                                                      miniatures, a pin vise is
                                                                      basically a hand-powered
                                                                      drill. It fits in your hand as
                                                                      you turn it, just like a screw-
                                                                      driver. The force from your
                                                                      palm helps push the drill bit
                                                                      deeper. Most pin vises allow
                                                                      for interchangeable drill bits,
                                                                      completely compatible with         Pin Vise and Rotary Tool

Superglue) are the bare minimum required for gluing metal                A vise or hobby vise is a necessity for complex modifications.

                                                                                                                                             SUPPORT

known as “kickers” are available for cyanoacrylate glue that          If using a rotary tool, a vise is an absolute safety must-have.
can accelerate the curing process and strengthen the bond.                                                                                   INFANTRY

traditional modeling clays. When deciding what to use as                                                                                      INDEX
                                                                      details, a hobby knife is sufficient. Brace the piece on your
sculpting tools, the possibilities are vast: fingers, hobby knives,   work mat, and make sure to cut away from yourself. Use only
toothpicks, erasers, pen nibs, nails and pins, dental tools and       moderate pressure—too much force and you may send the

                                                                        resort to a rotary if you don’t care about damaging the piece
                                                                        you’re removing. Even then, proceed cautiously. You can also

                                                                        material, but this requires patience, and again must be done with
                                                                        caution. When using the rotary tool, secure the miniature in a vise,
                                                                        and wear protective goggles and a mask. Using a rotary even
                         Cutting with a Saw Blade                       briefly on pewter miniatures heats them up a lot, so you’ll need to
                                                                        wait for them to cool down before handling them.
   In our four kitbashing projects, I chose to use the standard            In our sample kitbashing projects, I found the rotary quite
hobby knife blade only while trimming flash and the large lasers        useful in removing the Daishi’s weapon pods at its “wrist,” and
off the right and left arm of a Vulture (Mad Dog) for later use on      for removing an AC/20 from the left arm of a Ryoken (Stormcrow)
the Daishi. I also used the knife to scrape off the nubs under the      [IWM stock # 20-611] for later use. I also cut the Daishi’s right torso
Daishi’s nose, as that area must be flat in order to attach the small   vent and “subtracted” extraneous material from that area, in order
laser housing.                                                          to create a flat vertical surface later on. I also had to use the rotary
   When dealing with a slightly larger cut, or one that requires a      to cut down the two Mobile HQ cabs, shortening one to form the
little more control, swap out the standard blade in your hobby          LUT cab and the other to form the walls of the Heavy Truck bed.
knife for a saw blade. At this point, we recommend you use a vise         You can use hobby clippers as a quick-and-dirty way to cut
to hold the mini, as the sawing motion may throw off your cut           through lots of unwanted metal, but the clippers will flatten and
otherwise. Lubricating the blade with water or petroleum jelly
helps ensure a steady back and forth motion. Depending on
how you position the piece in the vise, you may need to remove
it and flip it once you reach a halfway point. Keep in mind that
saw blades destroy a lot of material where the cut is made. In
our sample kitbashing projects I used the saw blade on the Swift
Wind cars to remove the wheels from the crew segments, as well
as cut the crew segments from the rest of the vehicle.
   For pieces that require a longer cut, or are thicker in diameter,
you can use a jeweler’s saw or hobby saw. Use the same technique
as when using saw blades; however, these blades tend to do less
damage, and you can use them with a little more finesse. On the
other hand, jeweler’s saw blades are very fine and can snap if too
much pressure is applied or if they get stuck. Luckily, the blades
are easy enough to replace.
   Extremely thick pieces might require a rotary tool. The rotary

   Pinning: Pinning refers to the practice of strengthening a                                                                                      SUPPORT
bond by drilling into both pieces and inserting a metal pin                                                                                        VEHICLES
or rod. I use brass rods for most pinning, but straightened
paperclips work just as well and are usually at hand. Before
                                                                                                                                                   INFANTRY
you drill, put a small dot of paint on one of the pieces where
you plan to drill. Carefully test-fit it with the piece you’re
attaching it to—now the other surface has picked up the
                                                                                                                                                  AEROSPACE
paint showing you where to drill so that the pin will be cor-                                                                                       UNITS
rectly aligned. A pin vise is sufficient for most jobs, but at
times you may want to drill deeper, and the rotary tool will
make things easier. Make sure you don’t drill clean through                                    Assembled Light utility Vehicle (LUT).              CREATING
the other side of the piece! Once you have your pinholes, test                                                                                    SCENARIOS
the length of pin you’ll need, and cut it to size using hobby

our sample kitbashing projects, most of the Daishi’s limbs                                                                                       MINIATURES
and weapons needed pinning.
   Gap Filling: Regardless of whether you’re attaching stan-
dard parts or fitting kitbashed pieces to your miniature, you                                                                                       INDEX
are often left with small gaps that need to be filled. Though
there are excellent products made specifically to fill gaps, we
can manage just fine with material on hand. Very small or thin
cracks can be filled with Superglue. Use a kicker to quickly
cure the glue, and see if more is needed. Sometimes the
gaps are significantly larger, longer or deeper, such as when                                         Assembled Heavy Truck

                                                        Limb-Swapping and Magnets
                                                           The differences between some ’Mech variants are so minor                PLAYING
                                                        that you can achieve them simply by swapping out a limb or                THE GAME
                                                        weapon for another that is similarly equipped. This is espe-
                                                        cially true with OmniMechs, given the uniform appearance
                                                                                                                                  GROUND
                                                        of their limbs and weaponry. You can even go a step further
                                                                                                                                 MOVEMENT
                                                        and outfit a miniature with multiple limbs and weapons that
                                                        swap out. The way to achieve this is similar to pinning. Drill a
Magnets allow for on the fly miniature customization.   hole on each of the connecting surfaces, test-fit a pin or rod,          AEROSPACE
                                                        and then glue the pin to the limb instead of gluing the parts            MOVEMENT
                                                        together. You may need longer pins and deeper drilling to
                                                        create good temporary holds.
                                                                                                                                  COMBAT
                                                           If you are seriously interested in limb swapping, the most
                                                        useful items at your disposal are rare earth magnets. These
                                                        powerful, compact magnets are not rare despite their name
                                                        and can be obtained from numerous vendors, as revealed by                   HEAT
                                                        a simple Internet search. Rare earth magnets not only make
                                                        limb swapping a breeze, they also allow you to attach or
                                                        detach other external equipment at will. Numerous shapes                 BUILDINGS
                                                        and sizes are available, but I find the 1/8” x 1/16” and 1/16” x
                                                        1/32” discs the most useful.
                                                           Once you determine the size of the disc you’ll need and
                                                                                                                                PROTOMECHS
                                                        where it will be placed, choose a drill bit a little larger in diam-
                                                        eter than the disc. Drill a little and test-fit the magnet. It should
                                                        lie flush against the surface; if it doesn’t, drill a little further
                                                                                                                                  COMBAT
                                                        and repeat. Once the magnet fits perfectly, use Superglue to              VEHICLES
                                                        set it in place, and fill any gaps as you normally would. Once
                                                        the glue and/or epoxy are cured, you may repeat the process
                                                        for the other surface. Before gluing the second magnet in                 SUPPORT
                                                        place, make certain the polarities are properly aligned. Allow            VEHICLES
Your standard friendly neighborhood ForestryMech.       the loose magnet to attach itself to the first magnet. Then
                                                        paint or mark the exposed face of the loose magnet. Now you
                                                                                                                                  INFANTRY
                                                        know that the non-painted side of the second magnet must
                                                        face outward. In our sample kitbashing projects, I installed
                                                        magnets in all the component cars of the LCT, allowing the
                                                                                                                                 AEROSPACE
                                                        vehicle some modularity.                                                   UNITS

                                                        FINISHING
                                                           Once you’ve completed your kitbashing, you can use the                 CREATING
                                                        techniques described earlier in the section under Painting               SCENARIOS
                                                        Miniatures to paint your miniatures, allowing you to place
                                                        your own creations on the field of battle. For examples of the           PAINTING
                                                        final results for the sample kitbashing vehicles, see the photos        MINIATURES
                                                        in the Support Vehicles section, beginning on p. 204.

   A ForestryMech armed and ready for combat.


                                                                                                                                  295
      PREPARING TERRAIN
   BattleTech is usually fought on hexgrid maps. However, players
can play on hexless terrain—either using miniatures conversion
rules, or the Alpha Strike: Commander’s Edition rules. In both
instances, players will be using 3-D terrain.
   Once the miniatures are ready, players will need to construct
the terrain on which their miniatures will fight. We recommend
creating a playing surface that measures at least three feet square.
That size allows you to add enough terrain to make the battlefield
interesting. An ideal playing surface measures six feet by four feet
or larger.
   For beginners, virtually any objects found around the house                                       Single Trees
can be used as miniature terrain. Foam packaging, small boxes
and a piece of felt spread out over stacked books for hills can be        The advantage of Styrofoam is the ease of cutting it to a desired
used to represent a useful (if not realistic) playing surface. Once    shape and size. We recommend that you paint and flock the hills
you have gotten accustomed to 3-D gaming, see the following            to match your playing surface. Use latex paint of the appropriate
suggestions for ways to represent various terrain features more        color to seal the hills; this gives a good undercoat for the flock
realistically on the tabletop.                                         and helps protect the hillsides. Flock or model railroad grass
                                                                       can be found at your local hobby store and should be glued to
CLEAR                                                                  your hills with a 50/50 mix of white glue and water. If using a felt
   For clear terrain, we recommend a flocked gaming table. The         playing surface, simply glue a piece of extra felt to the hilltop and
most common type of game table is a four-foot by six-foot piece        cut around its edge with scissors. You can find white Styrofoam
of flocked hardboard or plywood with one-by-two battens. If            at hobby and craft stores. Local home improvement or hardware
space is limited, you can use commercially available flocked mats,     stores carry blue and pink insulation foam.
which have the bonus of being portable. The most inexpensive              To get a rock-like texture around your hill, apply sculptamold
playing surface can be made by covering the table with a felt          papier maché to the edge. Crumple some aluminum foil and then
cloth of the appropriate color.                                        open it back up. Wrap the edge of the hill with the foil so the crum-
                                                                       pled surface of foil makes a pattern on the sculptamold. Once the
HILLS                                                                  sculptamold is hard, you can peel off the foil and paint your hill.
   Make hills out of blue or pink insulation foam measuring from       Paint washes are an excellent way to paint the rock texture.
half an inch to three-quarters of an inch thick, or out of white

individually, then stacked to make hills of various sizes. A good      wooded. When placed next to water, marsh templates and roads,
utility knife will work, or you can use a purpose-built foam cutter.   these individual tree stands add realism.

                             Road                                                           Complex Building

                                                                                                                                        297
BUILDINGS
   Foam and plastic packaging, painted and detailed, is an inex-
pensive and convincing way to make your own buildings. Plastic
caps for spray paint as well as many other household items that
would otherwise be tossed out can be put to use on the battle-
field. A little imagination can go a long way

   Buildings appropriate for BattleTech miniatures play can also
be found at hobby and model railroading shops. We recom-
mend products scaled for micro-armor or “Z-gauge” scale model
railroading. Resin-cast buildings produced especially for micro-
armor gaming are also available, as are kits that allow you to fabri-
cate buildings in plaster to your own specifications (these require
quite a bit of time and effort).                                                                                                        Rubble
   If the BattleMechs in your game jump a lot, you might prefer
buildings with flat roofs. Remember that each half- to three-
quarter-inch of height represents one elevation level. Mark the
Construction Factor (CF) on the bottom of each building model,
preferably with a pencil so it can be easily changed from scenario
to scenario.

RUBBLE
  You can use anything you have lying around to represent
rubble. Broken buildings, pieces left over from making other
parts of the terrain, or even other models can be “distressed”
using woodworking tools, a soldering iron and/or a hot knife.
Please be very careful using such tools. The result should look like
something that got blasted apart or burned down. You can also
make rubble with gravel, sand, leftover foam, bits of plastic and
even BattleMech parts from your bits box.                                                                                         Rough Ground




                                     This tank shed was easily constructed out of molded/textured styrene sheets and foam core board.
ROUGH GROUND
  Rough ground can be represented by gluing small rocks
or model railroad ballast to a gator board, plastic or card
                                                                                          ASSEMBLING THE
template. Gravel, sand and flock can be added to make it
more realistic. We recommend that you paint the template
                                                                                           PLAYING AREA                                       INTRODUCTION

and then glue flock around its edge so it looks good on your                   Now that you’ve created all the necessary terrain, it’s time
playing surface. If using a felt surface, cut some extra felt and           to assemble a playing area and start a game. Enjoy!               COMPONENTS
place a couple of small rocks on it.
  You can make rocky outcroppings using extra bits of foam
and papier maché.                                                                                                                                PLAYING

*See the Weapon and Equipment Types Table, p. 304, for abbreviation explanations, or Weapons and Equipment, p. 113, for           ‡Maximum range for weapons mounted on aerospace units; see Range Modifier, p. 235, in the Aerospace Units section.
  complete details. If weapon types are separated by a slash, then those weapon types all apply at once. For example, an          ‡‡May use Cluster Ammunition; see LB-X Weapons and Cluster Ammunition, pp. 120 and 141 respectively.
  LB-X can either be fired as a Direct-Fire Ballistic Weapon, or as Cluster Weapon, which means it also has switchable ammo       ‡‡‡Missile Weapons display their Damage Value uniquely; i.e. an LRM 20 is “1/Msl, C5/20”, meaning each missile does 1 point
  and is flak capable.                                                                                                              of damage (1/Msl), the largest Damage Value grouping is 5 (C5), and that 20 missiles are fired with each shot (/20).
**See Other Combat Weapons and Equipment, p. 129; for aerospace units, see Weapons and Equipment, p. 236.                         §See Burst-Fire Weapon Damage Vs. Conventional infantry Table, either p. 217 or p. 310.
†When fired as a Cluster Weapon by non-aerospace units; aerospace units always apply the –1 to-hit modifier.                      §§Against ’Mech/aerospace fighter/small craft targets only, every time the weapon is fired, before the to-hit roll is made,
††For Missile Weapons, the number after the slash represents a missile weapon linked to Artemis IV FCS (see p. 130).                the player may announce he is applying the Damage Value as heat to the target, in place of damage (see Heat-Causing
†††Rapid-Fire Weapons display their Damage Value uniquely; i.e. an Ultra AC/2 is “2/Sht, R2”, meaning each shot that is fired       Weapon, p. 113).
  deals 2 points of damage (2/Sht) and it can fire a total of two shots in a turn (R2).
